text:
Five stars for a cute little cafe that does coffee, breakfast, lunch, and baked goods. I came by for some breakfast. There's a parking lot around back as well as plenty of street parking. The store was clean, and the staff were super friendly. It seemed like a local business that knows its customers. The guy that helped me answered all my questions and let me sub out the cheese on my breakfast sandwich. Prices were reasonable as well.

Where they earn the five stars is their cold brew coffee- one of the best I've had in the area by far- probably second and I've tried a LOT of local coffee shops. It was super smooth and didn't need a lot added - I did a pump of vanilla and a little bit of half and half. Will definitely come back to try other breakfasts and get more coffee.

text: Awesome service the employees were extremely nice and the food was delicious, honestly this is the best location to my experience in central Florida.

text: Very terrible Costumer service, I've went their so much times cause how close it is but I can't ignore the facts and experiences I've had with this location I can list them but this review would be a book with just too many chapters, I'll list some though and before I do I need to say I love Dunkin donuts, but I'll list 3, so this one time I went with my family and we waited in line for 10 minutes and the cashier told us to go to the spot to the left of where u order, we waited and waited for 20 minutes and then you wanna know what happened a different employee asked why we're waiting there (there were no orders or food being made for people in the Dunkin donuts just the drive through) and we told her we were ready to order and she told us to go order where we were supposed to, when we finally did order the food was unsatisfactory compared to other locations, I will say when you enter in this location they always seem angry and make you feel unwelcome, one time we went and the lobby was closed and there was a paper saying they open in 5 minutes we wait and we knock when the 5 minutes passed and someone came and put another sign for another 10 minutes, we finally wait and they let us in then told us to lock the door so no one else could come in, one final time is they had no food available apart from 3donuts, no ice cream, no sandwiches nothing and worse when we started ordering the cashier gave us a attitude and dirty looks, I'm not saying to put this place out of business, Im saying to make it better, instead of going here go to 18419 on highway 19 they have excellent service and food

text:
So happy to have finally made it over to the Tea Lounge! It is located in the Little Saigon Plaza (which I also had not known about before either). Looking forward to see more businesses in this plaza!

The menu is huge. So many Chè and boba options. Chè is Vietnamese dessert. The ones they have here are cold desserts that consist of mostly sweet bean, fruits and chewy jellies. So yummy and refreshing! Similar to halo halo a bit (Filipino dessert).

They have food too! We tried a few things: chicken rice balls, sunshine wings, and a bulgogi beef combo box. Rice balls were packed full of flavor. Umami bombs. Sunshine wings are a sweet and spicy Korean wing, pretty much gochuhang wings. Delicious! We didn't finish these (too full) but they tasted even better the next day. The bulgogi box was yummy too. Although, it wasn't in a box lol. 

So my niece was visiting. She loves boba. We went to 7 different boba shops in the area and Tea Lounge is number one. Tapioca was fresh and chewy and the different teas she got (we came back again because she liked it so much lol) were all good. Not watered down and artificial tasting. 

Nice interior with tons of space. A lot of room to spread out. They have a few board/table top games and a foosball table. Also saw an ice cream counter to one side? But I don't think they sell ice cream? It wasn't being used when we visited. Tons of parking. Everyone working here was friendly and helpful in explaining the menu options to us. 

#1 boba and Chè shop in the area that everyone should visit.
label: positive

text:

text:
Had dinner here last night. If you are looking for underwhelming Italian food at a reasonable price, then this is the place for you.  I started out with Lobster Bisque soup which I thought was way too thick.  I'm an East Coast transplant so I know my seafood dishes.  Lobster Bisque is meant to be more of a delicate soup with a lot of rich flavor.  This soup just did not cut it.  My next course was the Caprese Salad. The tomatoes were underripe and had no flavor.  If you can't get flavorful tomatoes to use  in a tomato salad, you shouldn't serve this salad at all.  Using heirloom tomatoes would have greatly improved the taste of this salad; instead, all the flavor came from their balsamic glaze and the basil which was the topping on the tomatoes and the cheese.  My next course was the Pork Scallopine with mushrooms and a balsamic creamy sauce.  What a surprise when it arrived as two large chewy pork chops which were pounded not so thin.  The typical Pork Scallopine or the Pork Scallopine I have always had in an Italian Restaurant, and how I prepare it at home,  has been made with the tenderloin of pork which is pounded very thin to create a tender, melt in your mouth feel from the pork.  If there are going to serve you a somewhat pounded pork chop, there need to describe it as such.  I asked the owner's wife what cut of pork they used and she told me it was not a pork chop and the server asked the kitchen and she told me it was a pork chop???

I had to give them 2 stars for the service we received and the fact that they exchanged my meal for something else.  The replacement was the Lamb Lollipops which were overcooked and strangely served with waffle fries.  They seem to be a bit confused about what they are serving as Italian food--  waffle fries did not seem to compliment the lamb at all.

I was so hopeful when we found out another Italian Restaurant opened in town since we seem to lack in number in Italian Restaurants compared to the other ethnic ones we have here in Tucson.

I am sorry to say we will not be returning for another disappointing dinner here.
label: negative

text: Great texture. Cool flavor profiles and we enjoyed the flight of ice creams. Excellent price points for the quality. The only bummer part was 3 of the flavors we had come for were not available. We will have try them again, I guess. Lol. Would recommend.

text:
Curious about maybe, possibly theres something fresh to drink that will help boost my immune system during this time of Covid confusion. I found myself once again in the mix at the Row in Downtown Reno. Friendly reminder to anyone visiting Circus Circus, Silver Legacy or Eldorado. You will be asked to wear your mask properly. Protect  yourself,family, friends and others.
   Unfortunately, I was on a semi failed mission. After taliking to a friend about where to go to get a fresh fruit smoothie of some type. She told me maybe I might find a kale shake at Madame Butterwork's  Curious Cafe. What a name for a breakfast/brunch spot BTW. The name alone makes me want to buy something 
  Pressed on time I chose to skip the large plush over-sized booths and to just sit at the bar. A nice lady greeted me as soon as I sat. I asked her if there was anything like a Jamba Juice smoothie I can enjoy. Sadly, no.  But  they do have a fresh orange juice machine that'll help me OD on vitamin C. Good enough! I went with a large cup and felt satisfied as soon as it hit my lips. The juice tasted sweet,sour and fresh. Along with the perfect temperature I will recommend to anyone who loves fresh orange juice.
   5 stars still after not finding what I wanted ,but still able to get something that will help me not get sick!. Check them out if you haven't already. There's breakfast,brunch, coffee, pantries and a cool looking automatic orange machine. When's the last time you drank fresh orange juice with no additives or preservatives. So good! Get some =)
label: positive

text:
Alessia came from a restauranteur family and brings decades of experience including time at Bouchon. The pastry counter is open at 7 but breakfast isn't served until 8. It's right across from the El Presido with a clean, classy interior.

Breakfast: We got the crab crepe, french toast, and granola yougurt for the kids. Everything was well-cooked and beautifully plated, especially the yogurt dish. Only feedback is slightly too much hollandaise on the crepes and the french toast was a little soggy on bottom. Otherwise high quality and well portioned. 

We wanted to give the kids some solid food and ordered a croissant, it was so good we got up while waiting for our breakfast and got more pastries for later.

Pastries: 5 stars, the croissant is better than the one at Bouchon and Alexanders. It has an incredible outside crunchy layer and soft cloud-like inside. Cheddar bacon scone and kouign-amann also amazing, loved having these for a quality breakfast the next day.
label: positive

text: I've ate here plenty of times but when did the food become so bland with NO flavor The onion rings tasted like nothing and had to add our own seasoning on them and my husbands burger was all soggy. text: Maybe I came on an off day, but I have been wanting to try this bakery for some time now after seeing some great photos and was excited that I had some time to stop by. The croissant was not flakey and had more of a burger bun consistency, similar to packaged croissants you get from a grocery store. The scone suffered a similar fate, it was most definitely the consistency of a biscuit, it was missing the slight crumble and sweetness of a scone and the blueberries did little to elevate it. I think I'll stick with the other 3-lettered bakery in Old City

text:
Le meilleur que je n'ai jamais eu!
I was very surprised to discover this little French gym right downtown. It reminded me of places in France I had been. I ordered the French onion soup and a half grilled cheese sandwich with aged cheddar and gruyere, onion jam (caramelized) on  sourdough. 

It was so delicious I ordered one to go! It really was such a generous portion that I could barely finish the soup but I "forced" myself and left a token sip of the broth in the .tureen. Full of onions, cheese, bread, and delicious savory broth. No skimping on their portions or the recipe. 

The grilled cheese with onion jam was on toasted buttery sourdough and melted in your mouth! It was not too large or too much bread but just right to go along with the soup. It really was the tastiest grilled cheese I've ever had in my 71 years of life, and that's not an exaggeration! 

I cannot wait to return here with friends to try several items i saw as the staff were serving others. It was quite a spectacle actually has several of them paraded by together with plateful's of fantastic looking foods presented beautifully. It reminded me of a fashion show but he might see on television in France showing there new creations off. 

I commented to my friend that it looked like a parade! My kind of parade for sure! I found the price is reasonable and so will not be prohibited by that factor. 

Another nice added touch is they bring your bill to the table in a little machine and you don't have to go check out somewhere or wait for the staff to go process your credit card and then come back. It's done right at the table and your CC never leaves your sight. I regret to inform you that 

I did not try any of their pastries as I am on a New Year's resolution to cut them out till I takeoff my Covid weight and then some but I promise you as you will see in my pictures everything looks fantastic! 

My friend ordered two eggs bacon and berries. She loved it but I can't imagine ordering some thing that ordinary in a place like this! However it was prepared well and generous portions as you can see by the picture. Great for low-carb eaters!
label: positive

text:
This cafe has super calm and chill vibes with really friendly staff. 

This cafe makes really good espresso drinks! I always get vanilla oat latte - it's so creamy with sweet taste of vanilla. My boyfriend always gets a cappuccino and he really appreciates the perfect ratio of milk and espresso. The store itself is very warm and welcoming as well. They have some pastries, which I've never tried. They seem like they get their donuts from Hello Donuts, which is really good! I've tried their donuts from the actual store before. 

One plus is that when you buy coffee beans, you get a free coffee! So when we bought one today, my latte was free and only had to pay for the cappuccino!

text:

The space is very open and I think it's seats around 130, so waits don't seem to be long even for a Sunday brunch crowd. 

I ordered the daily quiche. Now let me tell you, I wasn't ready, it was amazing. I was told there are o my 8 slices made each day. I ATE TWO OF THEM. Literally ordered two orders of it. It was that good. 

I'd definitely recommend this place and look forward to coming back.

text:

I would just skip sushi nice time we come.

text:
As a Queens girl, I was so excited to go to the Mamajuana in Tampa hoping for the same awesomeness from back home but boy was I wrong. The restaurant is beautiful, however that's about it. I ordered calamari (yes I know...who orders that at a Spanish restaurant) as an appetizer and they looked more like onion rings because they were so greasy and fat and were way too chewy and had zero flavor. Our drinks (party of 8) were terrible. Either it tasted like rubbing alcohol or was too watered down to taste anything. 

My friend ordered the chicharrón the pollo app and it definitely did not taste the way it should and it was also way too soft, something just wasn't right. Chicharrón is supposed to be crunchy and tasty and as soon as you bite into it, it takes you back to the motherland...but this took me to a dark alley I never want to walk through. My husbands rice was undercooked which he couldn't even eat and he barely ate the rest of his meal because it just didn't have any taste. I ordered a chicken mofongo with plátano, not the yuca one, and man was it underwhelming. I took three bites and left the rest.  My friend ordered a salad and they said they were out of romaine so the waitress said we have mixed greens instead, friend says yes and receives a plate of spinach only (the only mixing being done was with the DJ). 

Speaking of which, nice touch with the live band, however you couldn't even hear the singers because the base sounded so bad and loud which drowned them out. The option of being able to go to the back lounge portion was nice for a change once you're done not stuffing your face. 

Unfortunately I would definitely not be returning. Not one of us fully ate their meals or even half way, the prices are outrageous (I've eaten better food for way less and didn't have to drive 45 minutes), they just need to do better. If they don't pick up the pace a lot of people are going to be out of a job. Take notes from your fellow NY branches and get some act right in you, otherwise the doors will be closing sooner than you know. Wishing y'all the best but some major changes need to happen.
label: negative

text: We came in on a Saturday morning, which is expected to be quite busy, and we waited a short 20 minutes for a table which wasn't bad at all. However, the place is very small and they had many tables crammed in the restaurant so everyone was bumping into people as they walked by. Servers continually bumped into us as they walked by with food. Due to the amount of people in the small area, the place is very loud and they had music turned up loud as well. The food was okay, and pretty quick to be served. However, some of it smelled like propane from being cooked. We did not have a chance to mention it to our server because he never came back after taking our order, only to drop off the check. It might be better when it's less busy.

text:

text:
Came here looking for another beignet place we're had years ago. It didn't look familiar but we decided to try it anyway. 

Waiting to get seated was confusing. We sat at the bar. The bartender was very nice and attentive. We ordered some beignet and beer. The beignets were too doughy, not what we had in the past at this other place. But it wasn't bad. We ordered the Ghost in the Machine beer and it was really good!! I really liked it. Would probably go back here for the beer, yum!!
label: neutral

text: Tried their original tiger sugar bubble tea, brown sugar mochi with soybean powder, and brown sugar bubble tea with pudding. All were delicious! The mochi has a weird texture and is stringy so that probably isn't for everyone. I'd definitely recommend trying the original first! It is pretty pricy, around $7 for a medium and $8 for a large. I don't like how you can't customize it. You end up paying for half a cup of ice.
label: positive

text: Darling place in historic Automotive Row in the 3000 block of Locust in St Louis.  Really neat old white brick (tile?) one story building.  The vibe is electic coffee shop/breakfast place with an international feel.  I enjoyed a delicious Tunisian dish; my friend had biscuits with mushroom gravy.  The lattes were great, & our international server was delightful.  I'll be back!
label: neutral

text: They get my order wrong 90% of the time and they always seem grouchy and irritated that your making them work
label: negative

text:
What a cute, eccentric cafe! With signs all over the wall, decorations everywhere, and so much color in everything, this cafe definitely has a fun vibe to it. 

I ordered the California scrambler. Scramblers are their version of an omelette, just more open-faced. This one had salsa, avocado, choice of cheese, and bacon, but I subbed out the bacon for sausage. You also get a biscuit with it and your choice of four different sides. 

The scrambler was good (the flavor of the sausage was the best part!) but I found the taste of the eggs to be a bit weird. They were kind of crumbly, reminding me of the  powdered eggs I used to eat in college. I loved the grits though! They were definitely homemade and delicious. My favorite part for sure. 

I found it unique that they serve fresh squeezed tangerine juice. It's a bit more tart but very refreshing. Not my favorite but a crowd pleaser in my group and a nice addition to set them apart. 

Finally, I commend them for their service; it was great! We were promptly seated and our food came out fast and hot. 

Definitely another hidden gem to check out!
label: positive

text: So many yummy options! I couldn't pick just one, so o got a bit of everything. Made myself sick stuffing my face, but it was soooooo worth it!
label: positive

text: Hard pass. One of those businesses where you feel embarrassed for the people running it.  They weren't familiar with their own menu--serving sizes, prices, ingredients. Quite an awkward experience. So many places to dine in NOLA, find one of those instead.
label: negative

text: Jack's Bagels on Milpas is the best!! Their crew is so nice, the bagels are delicious and fresh and it is close to the beach. The Linden on an everything bagel is my favorite and their paradise iced team is bliss. Would highly recommend!
label: positive

text:
Worst service ever. Cashier girl was nice but everyone else seemed mad and bothered. 
We left upset we just took our food to go not pleasant.
label: negative

text: Coming from NYC where brunch is abundant, Ruby Slipper was nothing to write home about but was a great option when we've spent days eating New Orleans staples and wanted something different. We started off with the NOLA style iced coffee and each got an entree and was stuffed by the end of our meal. If you want to try different benedicts, I'd recommend the trifecta or you can get the peacemaker and mix and match two. Eggs cochon, chicken st. charles, bayou shrimp, and crawfish & grits were all tasty. The stuffed French toast was exactly what I needed along with the benedicts for a sweet and savory meal.
label: positive

text:
Warm inviting locals cafe.  Especially great if you want plant-based, vegan, gluten-free options. They really aim to please. This place is always a favorite in part because of the commitment to offering many options and no up charge for non-cow pus milk. 

Also a thank you shout  out to the staff like Carly. You can appreciate her smile through her eyes. They really know service even though you order and usually pick it up at the counter, but they will bring it to you as well sometimes. 

They've got a lot of super food lattes. There's outdoor seating and shade or sun. They leave the doors open so there's good airflow. And there's also wonderful shirts on the wall honoring first responders and celebrating different fire stations.

They have fresh soup of the day and seasonal additions to the menu. We've had about three different types of the avocado toast. Today I had the savory seed one which was delicious and I feel so healthy after indulging in it
label: positive

text:
Amazing place, definitely go if you visit green hills. 
We usually get the breakfast sandwich and they're always so good. It's really nice because you can watch them as they're making your food, it's not like those sketchy joints where you don't know what's going on behind those walls. The coffee is really great and the customer service is amazing as well! My husband and I always like to visit here whenever we're in green hills.
label: positive

text: These are the nicest folks that work here. No matter when I go in, it's great service and they're always super friendly, no matter how busy they are. Love these guys, they always make me smile.
label: positive

text: First time ordering from the place I got a vegan gyro, grape leaves and macaroni and cheese. That gyro is so good. I'm definitely going to order from them again.
label: positive

text: 0 stars my wife and I went to the park center location for breakfast this morning. We waited patiently for an hour while our server kept making excuses about how the kitchen had a bunch of to go orders and our food would be right out. We noticed people getting there food that ordered after us. The server then came and told us the kitchen had lost our ticket and asked to take our order again. At that point we just got up and left. I've never experienced anything like that and cannot believe this place does so much business. In my opinion this is a prime example of where things are headed if you can find people to show up they are absolute idiots. My wife and I are owners of two business and are completely disgusted with how we were treated.
label: negative

text: First time here and I will definitely be back! The owner is so charismatic and welcoming. He walked me and my fiancé through the meal and helped us create our own combination meal so we could try a little bit of everything. Not only is the service fantastic but the food is out of this world! The flavors and spices are so wonderful and tasty!
label: positive

text: Really good coffee and service is very knowledgeable and friendly. The Moxie iced coffee is fantastic.
label: positive

text:
During my trip to Nashville I was advised to visit this local coffeeshop, and I've already made two trips this week. The frothy monkey seems to be well loved by locals, so it's a little busy but has a very cosy atmosphere. We came on a weekday afternoon so we were seated as soon as we walked in. (Indoor and outdoor options)

I was surprised to see they have such a vast menu, not just coffee and lattes but they also prepare breakfast and brunch foods as well as wines. I tried a French toast there and it was alright.

I LOVED their golden monkey drink. I felt like it was so perfectly balanced. Just the right amount of creaminess, giving it great mouthfeel combined with the right blend of spices. I appreciate that it was not overly sweet like so many other coffee shops' drinks. It was honestly one of the best golden milk latte I've had yet, and I'm someone who's been drinking turmeric milk since my childhood. I can't forget to mention the pretty latte art designs that top each cup
label: positive

text:
Visited this location in the central business district. Absolutely loved it. Best part was they had an online queue for reservations. You didn't have to wait until you got to the lace to put your name down and you will wait. It was extremely busy and although I was there on vacation, it seems like a place that is regularly bumping with customers. 

We sat outside on patio, had a fantastic server who was attentive and helpful. Food came out at a good pace and it was delish. There was a lot of good stuff on the menu to choose from - it was tough! 

Not pictures apps were bacon biter and French toast bites. Pictured is a side of corned beef hash and the Peacemaker, 2 benedicts bayou shrimp and crawfish and grits. 

Highly recommend if you can make it here!
label: positive

text: Totally awesome place. Products made fresh regularly throughout the day; very creative style. Everything is sourdough!!!!! Fresh ground whole spices for the Chai. Everything they do that I have tried is terrific and special.
label: positive

text: Returned to this restaurant to try out other things on their menu. We had the shredded beef empanadas, the pabellón entree (shredded beef, plantains, rice and beans) and the arepa llanera (shredded beef, white cheese, tomato and avocado). The empanadas were delicious but the pabellón and the arepa were really bland, missing salt and seasoning. I'd definitely recommend this place for the cachitos and Venezuelan empanadas, although pricey they're really good. I'd skip the rest of the menu items though.
label: neutral

text: Consistently good service and short wait times. One of the few good ones left in central Indy - other Starbucks around don't even compare!
label: positive

text:
This place just wasn't my cup of tea.  
I really like how they had the outdoor seating set up, I was very warm. Warm enough that I could take my jacket off and I was getting hot.  Servers were attentive to all customers in the outdoor seating area.  When I say all customers, I mean all, those on 2 legs and those with 4.  
They allowed dogs inside the outdoor seating area which I had not seen until I ordered my food.  Servers were seen petting dogs and then coming to serve you your food. I do not appreciate having to eat a meal in an establishment and have a dog shaking itself, especially in an enclosed area. 
I wasn't overly impressed with my meal. I ordered the eggs, meat, hash brown and pancake/French toast.
label: neutral

text:
My 5,000th Yelp Check In was at this wonderful cafe. We visited to pick up cookies and roasted tomato toast on this cold day.

Don't forget your vaccine card.
label: positive

text: Tried for first time, if had little more meat they'd be the best, but overall good bagels I'd come back
label: neutral

text: I have lived in the area for over five years and can't believe I just discovered this coffee bar. I came here to meet with a friend and only ordered a cup of oolong tea. I like the design and decorations inside, cozy and relaxing. Oolong tea actually exceeded my expectation, definitely not a cheap tea bag. If you need a place for a quick break or hang out with some friends, I would recommend this place!
label: positive

text:
Hate to say it but I don't understand what all of the hype is about! Was expecting long lines and crowds but ended up getting a table in the back room in the apartment complex lobby. Tried to order online with the QR code but it kept giving me an error that items in my cart were no longer available, but not specifying which items, so I ended up waiting in line to order anyway. 

No one in my group liked the grilled cheese Jerusalem bagel. One compared it to frozen Ellio's pizza- and they weren't wrong- yikes. The avocado kubaneh toast was good, but nothing special beyond your average avocado toast. The pistachio sticky bun was by far the stand out item, but definitely doesn't compare to the pastries at Suraya. I don't drink coffee but others in the group strongly disliked theirs. My Bloody Mary was horrible, one of the worst I've had. 

The items were delivered to the table sporadically with huge gaps of time in between. For example, we ordered 2 sticky buns and one came out right away and the other came out after we finished everything and were packing up to leave. 

Overall, a big ol' disappointment.
label: neutral

text: Terrible service.  Left after waiting 40 minutes for drinks.... And still did not get them
label: negative

text:
Was visiting from LA so I wanted to try something different and new. Right when I saw Turkish delights, I knew I had to try them. I went in and was not greeted nor was I offered any samples. It was obvious that I was confused on what to get and I also didn't know how it works. Unfortunately, I wasn't able to receive that excellent service everyone received. This explains why I only ordered two since I had no idea what I'm getting myself into. 

The baklava was a bit on the sweeter side. So if you're into sweets, this snack is for you. It's also on the higher end of the price considering how it's $3 for a piece. If you've never had Turkish food, I would still recommend experiencing it. Would I come back though? Probably not.
label: neutral

text:
Cute little spot with a grass roots feel and solid coffee. The inside is a bit sparse but there is plenty of seating if you need to work or study. I will recommend you bring headphones though since the interior has a very pronounced echo which may be distracting. We ordered an americano and an oat milk latté and both were par for the course compared to other Nashville coffee spots. 

There is a selection of "toasts" such as your standard avocado and a few other varieties. These were tasty but the bread wasn't toasted which is a bit misleading right? They to have a deal on toasts as well, one for $5 or two for $8. They also sell their own beans, cold brew and soap which looked nice. 

Couple small tweaks and this could be a 4 star place easily.
label: neutral

text: I've only had a smoothie them, but I distinctly remember it being a lifesaver.  Picture this, it's a crowded late fall or early winter day.  So it's cold, and you're wearing a thick down jacket.  But it's not cold, cold out yet, ya know?  Okay so you're inside Reading Terminal and realize you are actually roasting under your ginormous parka.  You need something cold to counter all the sweltering heat.  Enter Kamals, with their fresh ingredients and delicious smoothies!  I made a custom smoothie with papaya, guava and mango.  They use real fruit. It was very refreshing. I wish I got the largest size because it was so good!  I didn't get to try anything else from Kamals.  Next time I will for sure!
label: positive

text: I've been to a lot of coffee shops over the last couple of weeks around Indy and the vibe at this one was my favorite. I had their biscoff latte with oat milk and it was so delicious if you're feeling something sweet. The decor is beautiful, the staff was incredibly friendly, the place is clean, plenty of tables, the pastries looked amazing. They have a couple little nooks that would be great for working. Their prices might've been a little bit higher, but I have no problem paying for quality coffee with a purpose.
label: positive

text: I've come in several times for take out, and I've not been disappointed yet! I can walk here for lunch from work, I've tried the chicken Cesar salad, which so great! Lots of chicken, fresh lettuce, nice big flavorful croutons, and bread! The mango penne was also delicious - big portion and what a great combo! The lobster Mac was to die for!! Lots of big chunks of lobster, and creamy pasta-enough for 2 people! My favorite dish so far was the lobster bisque!! I called ahead and arrived about 15 minutes late to pick it up, so they made me a new bowl! They wanted me to have a fresh one!! There were big chunks of lobster in the bisque, it was creamy and enough for dinner with the bread that came with it!! Service is always spot on, and the owner comes out to make sure you're happy. I'm really excited to sit down and eat here in person soon!
label: positive

text:
Okay it's about time I wrote a review about The Horn. I love supporting local businesses and this is one you HAVE to check out: Delicious food? Check. Great coffee? Check. Friendly staff? Major check! 

Everything is super fresh and just downright delicious. Who knew you could make so many different and creative kinds of sambusas? I want to applaud the person/people at the Horn who created the sambusa filled with ingredients from a Philly cheesesteak as well as the apple pie one. Definitely have to try those flavors when you go! It's also important to get yourself an iced Somali chai while you're here too. Probably one of the best coffee drinks I've ever had. 

I will always visit here whenever I get the chance
label: positive

text: Great empanadas!!! We got to go and had their beef and cheese empanadas and not only we they delicious they were pretty big in size too.
label: positive

text: Franklin Social is an average hotel bar. The cocktail selection is decent. They had the game on at the time.
label: neutral

text:
A nice place for some modern American diner food with a slightly healthy twist. It's clean and comfortable. And while it's not as inexpensive as say, Denny's, it's also better food overall. 

The service was very rushed. But probably because they are understaffed. 

Omelets are decent. Try the green chile with pico. Yum.
label: positive

text: Went to the drive-thru and sat there for 30 minutes. One of the cars honked just to see what was going on and then finally a worker spoke in the intercom screaming "honking won't get you your food any faster". Both windows had 2 people working and I am sure 2 more in the kitchen. They charge extra for sauces...I got a large fries and it was literally the size of a small. Very poor service!
label: negative

text: Food was tasty and so was the coffee, our server was amazing and answered our questions and gave delicious suggestions. More depth in flavor than most things around. The inside is tight and I was a little uncomfortable at first like most small places but eventually got comfortable.
label: positive

text: I'd passed The Mode countless times and finally went in while bar hopping with friends on New Year's Eve. You're partially paying for the atmosphere here. The drinks are pricey but the vibe is very cool, it feels young but still very upscale and the word "lounge" is truly perfect to describe the business. It was of course crowded on NYE but the bartenders were great at keeping up and while I just ordered a glass of wine everyone who had a cocktail enjoyed it (Whiskey Sour and Moscow Mule are the two I know were for sure ordered) all drink sizes are generous for both cocktails and wine. My glass was easily the size of my head, and the photo shows it when just poured. However, while the wines are more fair in price the cocktails, even those not on the pricey section of the menu, are on the expensive side. Other group members compared the pricing to other craft cocktail bars downtown saying they were similar though. I can't speak to if the experience with the bartenders compares to other bars since we were there at such a busy time when it wasn't appropriate to ask for recommendations or talk about the different offerings. I'd be interested in visiting at another time to get a better sense of the experience. Some of the seating is quite close together as well. Depending on your comfort level with Covid or the flu this could be a place to wait on visiting. Based on my own vaccine status and comfort levels I felt fine but did sit against a window towards the back of the room.
label: positive

text: Love not only the coffee and drinks but the mission. Their breakfast sandwich is by far one of my favorites and I drive there just to get that. Love trying the specialty coffees as something new but dave is either caramel or hazelnut latte. Can't wait for warmer weather and outdoor seating to chill. I wish there was some interior overflow. But then, I probably wouldn't leave.
label: positive

text:
Really bad service. 
Waited forever to get any attention after being told to seat myself at any table. 
Had to remind the staff that I exist and would like to make an order. 
I ordered Omaha open faces tritip sandwich. I asked for medium had well done. It says it comes with salad - however there was no salad on my plate. My friend got a herbed chicken. They forgot sauces and chicken was way over cooked. 
Also, had to go and ask for silverware after food was served. 
Won't come back.
label: negative

text:
We love this place! It's so nice to have something that's not another chain restaurant and it's a welcome addition to speedway blvd (we didn't need another Dutch Bros amiright?). It's full of natural light with brightly coloured chairs, complete with a beautiful wall-to-wall mural of a Greek coastline. The owner of the restaurant always greets us when we come in and it's nice to feel welcome. The portion sizes are massive for what you pay (like, the Tucson Winter Fair would try to charge you $30 for what you get at Santorini ) and the food came out very quickly and was so fresh and flavourful! 

Definitely try the fries with tzatziki sauce. The beef & lamb gyro plate/wrap is so good. The rice is amazing (idk how it's so great but just try it, you'll see). And the Greek salad is our favourite!
label: positive

text: I absolutely love this place. It's quiet. It's quaint. The food is always good. The service is always kind. Location is great. They're always serving breakfast and lunch no matter the time of day. It's by far my favorite French spot in St. Louis.
label: positive

text:
This was such an incredible experience, from the entrance, to the service, and of course, to the food. As soon as you walk in, you're greeted with a bookstore, coffee shop and a champagne bar. The atmosphere is indescribable - it's beautiful, especially the outdoor dining area. Their brunch/breakfast menu is large with a variety of options.

It was so good, that we returned the next day. The first day I had the smoked salmon bagel with fresh orange juice and coffee. I mean WOW, the best lox bagel I've ever had. The pickled red onions added so much flavor to the dish. The second day I ordered what the waiter recommended - steak sandwich with tomato soup/dip and it was incredible. I don't see it on their online menu, but you'll see it at the restaurant. Be forewarned that it is SUPER filling and got me through lunch and almost dinner as well 

I can't recommend Oxford Exchange enough. 5/5 without a second of doubt.
label: positive

text:
I remember seeing this Vegan Ice Cream shop posted somewhere and have been meaning to stop by. Finally..The day arrived :) I was so excited. 
Coco N'Ice is a Vegan Ice Cream shop located in Tampa. Their slogan; "The Nutrition with a Good Conscience". In speaking with the owner herself, just because it's vegan it doesn't have to taste different. They offer delicious homemade ice cream flavors free of sugar & additives, Smoothies, Plant based shakes, Bliss balls, Açaí Bowls amongst so much more. Her shop is super cute, quaint and very clean. 
She allowed me to taste most of her flavors available. I chose Pistachio in a Charcoal flavored waffle cone and also brought home a delicious Tiramisu cake and few Bliss balls. 
They also offer a Loyalty Card for frequent customers and Catering. 
I can't wait to go back and visit when I'm the area.
label: positive

text: Absolutely cool wonderful experience.   The food service environment was incredible.  Bring your wallet, You are going to need it!!
label: positive

text: This place just makes you want to stay and hang out a while. The drinks and food are just as great as the vibes. There's indoor and outdoor seating available. Probably the best biscuits in Tampa and one of the best cinnamon rolls. Highly recommend!
label: positive

text:
The five stars is mostly for the coffee. I got a cafe au lair, which is simple but depends on having good beans prepared well, and theirs was fantastic. They get their beans from Counterculture, which is one of the better coffee roasters around, and that shows, but I've also had coffee that wasn't as good from other places using the same beans.

The service was pleasant and reasonably quick. It's also a pretty nice place to sit, with a number of tables in a space that has a sleek, modern design: light (maple?) wood, clean lines, semi-minimalist, with current local artists works on the walls.

Way back in the day, when I was a brooding high school student, I was a regular at the Gryphon Cafe in Wayne, where I sipped coffee while writing character sketches in my Shinola notebook making fun of pretentious customers there X).  I don't know if I've gone soft/too bougie, or the coffee's improved, or my palate has grown to better appreciate good coffee (I suspect a mix of all of those), but all I could think of visiting the new WC Gryphon location was how good the coffee is.

P.S. My girlfriend got a chai latte and loved that, too.
label: positive

text: I love this spot! The coffee is excellent, and the space is clean and comfortable. There's a high ceiling and lots of light. Perfect place to meet a friend or read. It's my go-to.
label: positive

text: Definitely one of our favorite places in Nashville! Musicians are outstanding! Great atmosphere! The only downside remains the chatter from those who obviously miss out on why people come to the listening room. Great place to go otherwise!!
label: positive

text: service SUCKED. had appetizers took forever to get.  never came back to take order. . left.
label: negative

text:
One sentence to sum it up: layers of flavor.

The dip was outstanding. Wasn't filled with binder like mayo. The fish was the star. Had a depth that I had not had. 

Fish tacos were killer! Perfect portion for me.

The place is a vibe. Swanky. I'm definitely putting this in the rotation whenever I'm back.

text: Rudest customer service ever "unbelievable". I was visiting from NYC and unfortunately visiting this coffee shop was the first thing I did in Philly which really ruined my day. You will feel uncomfortable, not welcomed, and disrespected. One of the staff tone was very harsh and condescending. text: I'm in town visiting, and after my first visit the barista (with tattoos and a Hawaiian shirt, working Sunday January 9th) remembered my name, how to spell it, and how to say it, which isn't something I usually get! The coffee is delicious, I've tried a few of their drinks as well as one of their vegan pastries. I intended to try as many coffee shops as possible during my stay but I might be making this my regular spot instead. Thank you!

text: If you're a Santa Barbara local. You already know. It's the best of the best in Brunch flair! The Homemade blueberry pancakes are unreal and the Lavender Latte is Lauren's favorite!
label: positive

text: I feel it's time for an updated review! This place is more reliable than Starbucks now! Nice employees, clean store inside, and good coffee choices as well as other drinks! Sometimes service is slow in drive thru but today I ordered inside and it was quick and staff were all very friendly! Go here instead of Starbucks!
label: positive

text:
So pumped to have a LOTH Coffee in the neighborhood and to have it legit beside us on Charlotte Pike! I fell in love with the brand in Atlanta, where they originated and have several locations throughout the metro area...and fun fact, all their coffee is still roasted in Atlanta! This location in West Nashville is their only TN location and as mentioned, I am so glad it is in the area I now call home! 

This location is one large open industrial space that combines modern and industrial. It sits in front of the Cortland apartments. While it is an open space, it still feels like separate spaces with a mix of well laid out couches, high tops, picnic tables, booths and lounge seating coupled with games such as pool, skee ball, connect four and a few others. And you can't forget the great outdoor spaces! The inside also features a small stage for local performers. I haven't caught a live act yet, but the space is absolutely perfect for it! 

While they are known for their coffee, they also offer a selection of local beers and ciders, as well as a few varieties of wine and house cocktails. They also have some eats including breakfast sandwiches, muffins, pastries, sandwiches, etc...

I of course came in for the coffee and the iced coffee specifically. The cold brew was simple, flavorful and perfection. Their coffee menu is pretty limited, however they can make pretty much anything requested, hot or iced.  With my coffee, I ended up getting a blueberry muffin, warmed, of course, and it was delightful! 

Overall, I am a massive fan of LOTH for a multitude of reasons! Not only do I love the coffee, I also love that they are an afternoon/evening spot to grab a beer at as well, plus what they represent and support goes so far beyond coffee. I enjoy my local coffee shops, but I feel slightly attached to this LOTH given its location in my first Nashville neighborhood and its Atlanta roots. So, in short...I will be back, a lot.
label: positive

text: Their Spanish latte was delicious (and beautiful) if you're feeling something sweet. A lot of people in there studying/working. Not too many tables, but it was super cute and comfortable.
label: positive

text: Great food, but kind of an unusual set up.  Mainly catering to takeout sushi type venue. Service very friendly.

text: This place is so cute and the staff are friendly. So, many options to choose from but your sure to find something tasty!! You have to stop in for some good coffee and a sweet treats that will not disappoint.

text: Joined the waitlist on our way, arrived and were seated early on a busy Saturday morning. We started with the doughnuts which were yummy but we liked (didn't love) the chocolate dipping sauce. All of our meals were tasty and the service was good. Definitely recommend!

text: This place is amazing. Incredible food, environment, and staff. I got the brioche breakfast sandwich and it was so good. The prices are really reasonable here too. It's a great place to go with people or alone to get some work done in a great environment.

text: I stumbled across this coffee shop as I was staying at an Airbnb nearby. I am so glad I did! text: Great local restaurant with awesome and friendly service.   The food is great here with large servings.  The breakfast sandwiches can feed at least two they're so huge.  You also have to try the masitas.  Can't wait to go back again?
label: positive

text: House blend coffee out. Dark roast cold. No onion bagels. Ample staffing with 4 employees and only 3 patrons. Took 15 mins for a breakfast wrap. Not impressed.
label: negative

text: My husband and I were craving some after meal dessert and thought of trying this shop in Ardmore's Suburban Square. We reached the store pretty close to their closing time. They did seem to have most of the stuff in their showcase and we ordered 3 small cakes for $8.50 a piece. We ordered chocolate chip cheesecake (award winning tag was placed on this cake), strawberry shortcake, Triple chocolate cake with fudge icing. All  three cakes were horrible in taste. The cheesecake had gotten rotten sour taste, felt like it has been kept like this for days now. The chocolate cake was very hard to bite in and almost felt frozen. The cream of strawberry shortcake had also gone bad. Overall had a horrible experience with their cakes. Will not be trying this bakery again.
label: negative

text: They should be called Make America Great Again Donuts since the owner is such a narcissistic nutjob. The donuts themselves are great but I won't be giving them another cent and haven't for the last two years. A blight on an otherwise excellent neighborhood.
label: negative

text: This place is so delicious and very detail oriented when it comes to your dining experience. Very new and already booming . Please go check it out
label: positive

text: Ordered the Whole Fruit Cream Cake which is a gluten-free sponge cake. I requested both strawberries and blueberries (they usually give you a choice of one or the other with online orders). The cake was light, not too sweet and the fruit was fresh. I paid for extra fruit. I'm not sure what the normal amount of fruit is, but the "extra" seemed to be what I'd expect as the "normal" amount. That said, we were all very happy with the cake! Only suggestion is to have two people go pick up, one driving and waiting at curbside and one run in a get the cake. Parking is very limited downtown.
label: positive

text: The spring rolls were good, the pork baboons were good but that one piece of mini cucumber did not give the crunch factor. The pork chashu ramen was a lukewarm, tepid broth. I'm not sure what the red strands were in the broth but by golly they were bitter.  I asked for spicy chili oil on the side....$1.50 extra...I didn't know I paid extra until I looked at the receipt! It's not the money but the principle
label: negative

text: This is my first time trying this place.Food was excellent! I ordered  the 10 piece wing and my husband order the Philly cheesesteak. The wings were very crispy and my husband enjoyed the philly. Definitely will be back.
label: positive

text: This place is a dream! We went the morning after a hard rain. The staff was kind and the beignets are so tasty. The coffee has a great flavor, too!
label: positive

text: Really good gelato. So tasty and great flavors. Amazing spot with old fashioned Italian music piped into the outdoor seating area.
label: positive

text: Best breakfast in clearwater beach. Always something new on menu. Bloody marys are amazing!
label: positive

text:
I tried Latea for the first time this week and really enjoyed it. I tried a brown sugar milk tea, the mango honey green tea, the tiramisu milk tea, and the strawberry matcha. I enjoyed all four drinks, but especially the tiramisu milk tea and strawberry matcha. I plan to go back and get the tiramisu milk tea with oat milk! 

Service was amazing, the drinks were great and I loved the location right on mass ave. I also felt the prices were really fair.
label: positive

text: Loved this place! It was a short walk from where we were staying and despite the crowd, we didn't have to wait. Also got the crab fries and they were good!
label: positive

text: I was visiting from out of town and we came to Mercado. Unfortunately kids were blowing bubbles near where we were sitting and it got in my food. Because of Covid, I did not want to eat it so I purchased another dish. When Morgan found out, she refunded my money for the second dish. Thank you ! Btw the food and service were fantastic!

text: Great food and good vibe!  Really enjoyed our dinner there and highly recommend you go
label: positive

text: This place was good! I got a BLT and added turkey. It was quite delicious but too ezpensive for what it was. I spent almost $30 for the sandwich + tip. I also didn't love the peppered chips, but I will still give this good scoring because the bread, meat, and everything in between was very good. Service was great, and speedy too.
label: positive

text: The problem isn't the food but the staff . Walked in and the gentleman at the register so can I take your order I said hi how are you . Blank stare from him . Then when when order was done the don't here say it out load but throw it on a counter and you are left guessing if your food was ready or not . I watched customers just walk back and forth seeing if their items was read .  Poor management I would say for this location .
label: negative

text: First, really great service! Second, great simple boba! I tried it hot for the first time and it was delicious. They also have combo options to try an appetizer and a dessert along with your entree and all 3 were very delicious! My friend got the Yakisoba noddles and they were amazing! We'll definitely be coming back:)
label: positive

text: The food and service is top tier. I don't remember our servers name but he was exceptional.
label: positive

text:
Pork buns were tasty and fluffy. Beef buns were fluffy but meat was dried and grizzle couldn't enjoy the 3rd.

Fresh roll chicken and shrimp pretty plain could have used some fresh mint. Good peanut sauce had to use hot oil for a kick.

Milk teas size was smaller than desired. No Boba.....1st world problems!
label: neutral

text:
This bakery is in a cool arts complex area with a few other restaurants and bars (we came to this same area for dinner the night before at The Lark). Very cool Santa Barbara feel. I had the Farmers Market sandwich (add ham) and he had the taco. We shared the amazing challah French toast (only avail on weekends). The baked egg sandwiches are huge. We loved the outside patio and it's of course dog friendly. 10/10 for us. The espresso
Machine was down which gave me the opportunity to walk to a coffee shop, Dart, down the street.
label: positive

text:
The staff is always very nice and pleasant, but some of the food is really just awful.  Standard breakfast fare like eggs, bacon, pancakes, waffles, etc. are good.  

But don't even think about ordering anything remotely ethnic or "fancy" from the menu.  My girlfriend ordered a Cuban sandwich.  See the picture for what she received.  One slice of ham, one slice of turkey?, mushy pickles that were so gross that when I tried it I spit it out,  white bread, and the sandwich was not even put into a press, way too much mustard, don't know if I need to go on.  We ordered a lobster dish one time and the lobster was obviously freezer burned and mushy texture.  

Again the staff is all very nice and they actually took the Cuban sandwich off the bill, but we would have rather just had a good sandwich.
label: negative

text:
My favorite dessert place is Frost gelato! The portions are small and pricey, however excellent, fresh and there's is many selections to choose from. 
What amazes me is how full you feel after having even a small portion. The staff are always anxious to serve you and the service is good! Many times they will offer you water after you finished with your gelato if you dine on site.
label: positive

text: The food is terrible and the service is even worse. Don't waste your time or money. Waited for about an hour after ordering before the food came because the waitress forgot to put our order in.
label: negative

text: More of a mom and pop vibe than we expected based on the website. Breakfast was good. I wouldn't recommend the French toast. The bread was dry and not your typical French toast. But other people really enjoyed the grits. Service could have been better. We kept having to ask for refills.
label: neutral

text: The tiny little bakery located behind Casey's garage was packed with people waiting outside on a Sunday morning.  They have birria tacos on the weekend, 3 per order, served with onion, cilantro, grilled pepper, and consume for dipping.  The tacos were so overstuffed that you can make at least 3 more tacos out of the meat.  Service was friendly and prompt, and the price was low for the quality and quantity.  This is a hidden local gem.
label: positive

text: My family and I love this coffee shop. It is our favorite place to go on the weekend and get some coffee for the adults and a smiley cookie for the kiddo.
label: positive

text: I love State Street Kitchen. Misha is the sweetest and makes the best food. You can tell Misha loves all of her customers and that she takes pride in making great food. I really wish I had started coming here sooner. It's always a wonderful experience here.
label: positive

text: Their pastries and beverages never disappoints me. Always fresh. My new favorite boba/ pastry shop in Philly
label: positive

text:
We almost always stop by here after having dinner in Pass a grille. Friendly staff and fresh flavors with generous portions. 
Can't go wrong!
label: positive

text:
I have eaten here about five times. Four have been outstanding. I just picked up my order (1/9/21) and it was definitely less than stellar. I always get the same order...garlic noodles with beef and extra veggies and extra garlic. This time I got no garlic and the only veggies I had was broccoli. I was missing the onion and peppers. I will tell you that missing the garlic and veggies definitely add a big difference in taste. 

I will chock it up to their year anniversary and the, being very busy this weekend with offering the coupon. Hoping next time is back to the great food I have become accustomed.
label: neutral

text: I'm not a vegetarian but I've eaten here twice.  Amazing!  I cannot say enough about the flavorful and unique offerings.  Very, very good!  They have smoothies and juice drinks, but if you want coffee, you can run next store and get a great cup of coffee and bring to enjoy with your food.  Cannot wait to visit again!
label: positive

text: Nice place. Friendly staff. Pretty busy when I went. Interior looks nice. Food came out quick. Different varieties of fruit teas. Pho was excellent. Would recommend
label: positive

text: We went here for lunch and the food was amazing. The dishes are large, served family-style. We ordered the chicken bites to start, Singapore Rice noodles, orange chicken, kimchi pork fried rice and hot & sour soup. The flavors were vastly different but equally good in each dish. Food came out quickly and the service was attentive. The restaurant itself was very clean and not overly crowded. 10/10 recommend
label: positive

text: Location has finally Jeff saying that the dining room is open but they have the doors locked when I tried to go in they claimed that the dining room close but they had people inside eating so she refused to open the door even though the sign says the dining room is open the young lady who claimed to be a manager was very rude and nasty and told me to go somewhere else if I didn't want to go through the drive-through when I asked her what her name was she refused to give me mine her name the best way to describe her is she was a short Caucasian woman with right now teeth I have gone to this location on several occasions and nothing is ever corrected the communication is very poor because no one speaks English

text: I love red hook. I order very frequently and always purchase a side order of extra sauce for .50. I ordered two this time and got a teaspoon of each sauce, I'm not sure why it's never consistent. I feel ripped off paying for this sauce.
label: positive

text:
I like playing the live cards. Three card poker is my favorite. I had a hand dealt and used a call from the pit boss regarding the cards I was dealt that are used to compete against the dealer with. I also was using the separate odds area on the table and won a $200+ simultaneous deal. The pit boss rejected my plea about my hole deal. He then retreated out of my field of play. The dealer then quickly snatched my winning chips and I was left with nothing.
I then looked over the dealer's shoulder and saw the pit boss snickering as he watched the saga unfold.
This so called casino is regulated by itself and they rip off its patrons at every turn.
label: negative

text: Everything that I have tried has been very flavorful! My favorites are the Carne Asada and fish tacos blackened while my husband prefers the Nashty and the Dos Perros. If you need an appetizer go for the Mexican flag so you can try their two salsas and the queso! With delicious ingredients like these you can't possibly make a poor choice!
label: positive

text: By far the worst experience I have ever had in a restaurant. When we got there it took over 20 min to get our coffees, the server was terrible. She never came around to refill drinks we always had to ask her for refills. Our food took over an hour to get out, the tables that came after us got their food before we did. The steak was trash, it tasted like a sponge. The manager had the audacity to comp a $1.25 off our meal...... I will never go back there. I have never in my life ever complained about a restaurant or the serve but this was beyond terrible. DO NOT GO HERE!!!!
label: negative

text: Excellent service and the food was good for the money. Highly recommend biscuits and gravy........definitely going back.
label: positive

text:
ANTI-FORMULAIC well-designed hotel, it was originally an Art-School founded in 1920 and is now a small boutique hotel with only 24 rooms but oozing with charm. The attention to detail is amazing from the welcoming tiles in the parking garage to the pleasant smell of flowers when you get on the elevator.

It has a perfect low-key appeal and design that feels "Santa Barbara" without the artificiality of primarily using white stucco and orange tiled roofs to fit local vibe like some fancy hotels in the area. Very cool place to stay and we loved it. The rooms are eclectic but memorable and we enjoyed resting in some of the public areas like the fireplace and fountain. 

The rooms have a GIANT bathtub for you to soak in and my wife was all chill and calm until she saw the shampoos, soap, and lotion were from "Diptique" and she went crazy texting her friends while I wondered why it felt slimy and made my eyes burn.

Location is tops, its easily walkable to some of the heavy-hitting sites and eateries in the Presido area, the longest we had to drive to anywhere else was 10 minutes.
label: positive

text: They are very friendly here and offer super breakfasts!  Great regular coffee and super unique coffees and lattes. Very good service.  Highly recommend.
label: positive

text: Delicious French breakfast, real bread, real croissants, Street side, not pedantic.  The macchiato is an Italian-style macchiato, not the Starbucks version .  Friendly personal service in French or English.  Just awesome.
label: positive

text: Great view!  Excellent choice for families with children.  I love that you can sit and eat with your feet in the sand.  Food was average
label: positive

text: They went up on the ox tails but there still the best I've had from a Caribbean restaurant. The food is usually good I staff it's rude. Just wish the line would move faster.
label: neutral

text:
This sweet little cafe is exactly what this side of town needs. We stopped in today for lunch for the first time. We'd grabbed to-go orders of pie before, but had never tried their lunch menu, which they serve between 11-3 each day. 

I ordered the full sized Asian chicken salad. My husband had a salad and sandwich combo plate. Both of us were quite pleased with how great our meals were! My salad was perfectly fresh and delicious, and his salad combo was great as well. Plus, both meals came with a fluffy fresh croissant...which is never a bad thing.

The staff was very friendly and helpful. The manager came by a couple of times to check on us, and cleared our plates when we were done. The restaurant is clean and tidy, with ample seating. There's a large outdoor patio space that will be really nice once the weather warms back up. Everyone working there seemed to actively enjoy working there, which is honestly not often the case in many restaurants nowadays. It was really nice to see.

We will definitely be back!
label: positive

text: This place is a gem for a taste of NY. It's an occasional treat because it's really pricey for bagels but soooo good every once in a while. I love the everything bagel with scallion cream cheese and NY Lox. The biscuit sandwich with brisket and egg is superb. Good pastries including hard to find babka too.
label: positive

text: !!!Brunch Alert!!! This little new cafe has some fun Mexican breakfasts, sandwiches, and salads. We tried the Dream Omelette and Chilaqueles and both were delicious. The nice waitress brought us 2 house made salsas: creamy avocado and habanero toasted tomatoes... Flavorful and not too spicy. It has a dog friendly patio on the side.
label: positive

text: Coffee was so, so good. The baristas were friendly. Lacking the 5 star rating only because it felt a little dismal with no seating area... grab and go cause of the damn pandemic.
label: positive

text:
Incredible ambiance, inventive lattes, great smells from the bakery. The only reason I removed a star was the lack of gluten free options. They had an impressive selection of alternative milks. The yellow lighting and bricks created a woodsy, old fashioned feel. A gorgeous and brightening coffee shop for a rainy day. Conveniently located next to a cute thrift shop and lots of parking. Love love love. 

Ratings: 

Dairy Free options: 9/10

Gluten free options: 2/10

Low carb options: 8/10

Overall deliciousness: 8/10

Ambiance: 10/10
label: positive

text: This place was unexpected and so yummy. The food and the coffee were exceptional! I would go back just for the vanilla latte. Staff was friendly and very accommodating. The restaurant is very small, so be prepared to wait for a seat, or maybe eat in the car, take it to go. We happened to get there at a good time and got a seat right away. Loved the deep fried egg sandwich! Hollandaise sauce was amazing.
label: positive

text: We pass this coffee place all the time and finally stopped in for some coffee. It is on a busy road but as plenty of entrances to pull in. We also decided to stop because it has a nice drive thru. I ordered the 16 oz hot latte with vanilla. It was perfect temperature to drink immediately and tasted exactly as I would expect. The price is pretty great too! We also got the command coffee which is two shots of espresso and their drip coffee. You can pick either a light or dark roast with creamer or sugar. Great service!
label: positive

text: This is our favorite breakfast spot. Pancakes is amazing I never had before. Food was fresh and so delicious. You won't be disappointed.
label: positive

text: Very good cheesecake, at least for my preference, because they're not too sweet at all. This place is on my list of dessert place now
label: positive

text: Best coffee, AMAZING FOOD! Staff is always helpful and friendly. Make this your first stop in the morning! You won't be disappointed!
label: positive

text: So we have been going to this place a couple years now. I first fell in love with the fried pork egg rolls they are my favorite in town still! The brisket is cooked well but the broth is sweeter then I like . I'm having a hard time justifying $15.50 for a bowl of pho I first loved pho because it was economical but this place has changed with all the upgrades they want to increase prices. Maybe we only go on slow days but I never see many others in there maybe they have caught on too. The prices went up twice the last few times I've been there. Since I can get pretty decent pho elsewhere I can no longer justify the prices . Service is good. Food was excellent prices are more than they should be for this area and because there is competition near by. Thanks for the good food and good luck!
label: neutral

text: Great sandwiches and personable service. The best place for lunch near the medical center.
label: positive

text:
Review long overdue. Hands down my favorite pastries and bread in Reno. Reminds me of tartine bakery in SF. I absolutely love the atmosphere here at Rancharrah. On a sunny day you can eat outside perfect for brunch. 


I LOVE their Niçoise salad! Lovely fresh veggies, including radish (hell yeah!) and perfectly cooked salmon. Perfectly dressed just how I like it.  

Perenn smash burger was yummy. First time ordering it, the meat was the perfect thickness and very flavorful. I looove the shoe string crisp French fries too.

kale salad was well massaged with oil and fresh. Not the highlight though! 

I got the blue jasmin tea, it looked, smelled, and tasted amazing. 

And lastly two pastries I got almond croissant and cruffin.  Crispy and flaky exterior and moist marzipan inside, ugh the almond croissant is soooo dreamy. All pastries from Perenn are amazing, you can't go wrong getting any item. Make sure to grab a sourdough bread with you to eat for the week :)
label: positive

text: Cute little place with plenty of parking. Nice place to get away and work. I had an americano, which was pretty good. Not the best I've ever had, but good nonetheless.
label: positive

text: Luna Cafe is decent. It was one of very few options near the hotel. Service was quick, which was appreciated.
label: neutral

text: This is my go-to spot when I'm craving a burger on the east side. It's a fairly limited menu but the small menu is full of good stuff! Good tasting burgers, everything is a la cart though. If you are wanting something other than burgers they do have hot dogs and tenderloins but I think the burgers are the star. They also have shakes which have some unique flavors. I recommend checking this place out!
label: positive

text: First I have to say that I love love love that they have more vegan and vegetarian friendly options now! We came around 10:30 on a cold Sunday and there was no wait. We started with apple pie mimosas and cinnamon pull a part (both specials) and they were so yummy. Our main entrees were good, but not mind blowingly delicious. They originally brought the vegan egg instead of regular eggs, but remedied it before I could ask the server. Service was a slow given the size of the crowd vs workers, but not too bad.
label: neutral

text: This coffee shop was down the way from my Air BnB and it had amazing breakfast sandwiches and amazing americanos.
label: positive

text: My aunt got a variety of mochi donuts for us: sesame, Nutella, cinnamon sugar, UBE, glazed, strawberry, cookies and cream, coffee and mango! All of them were very good!! They're so sharable, fluffy and good! Not too sweet at all. We were so happy with our rainy Sunday treat! Although I do want to try the bubble tea and the corn dogs so we will definitely be back!!

text: Was waiting outside the restaurant for an hour for my food, i when i got it, the buns were soggy like it had been sitting for a while. Both me and my friend who ate here got sick. Do not eat here.
label: negative

text:
Average compared to others I've had. We were eating at Sa Ri One and someone had a tea cup from here. Soo what's the first thing I have to do is locate this shop. Yea I'm a tea addict -- that's the first step is admitting lol. 

Found that is literally right down from Sa Ri One, walking distance. Soo I make the hubs drive there!! Beautiful shop and the staff is friendly and welcoming when you enter. They do have a few seating areas available. 

I wanted to try the matcha but they said they were out! Darn. So I got the green tea with sea cream topping and the brown sugar milk tea fired my daughter. The teas were just ok. Daughter actually didn't even finish hers. They have 2 sizes available and I just had to get the large since the cups are just so dang cute!!! Although the teas were ok I'll definitely come back as I want to try the matcha based on some of the other reviews.
label: neutral

text: Quaint cute and delicious. Excellent for breakfast too. Great ambiance. Highly recommend
label: positive

text: This was a much needed addition to NW sector of Indy. We previously were limited to wet / doughy kroger variety of yeast donuts. This place is oasis in our 'donut desert'. They offer a wide variety donuts in the dozen. They have good yeast version, but our favorite are the Cronut and Sour Dough fried. I have gotten coffee, and its good mild roast drip donut version. I have gotten sandwich with egg/bacon on bagel. This was bit bland and bagel was dry. As such, review rating is solely based on DONUTS. Buy them by the dozen, because even after a day, they were delicious.

text: The smoothie bowls are LIFE!!!! I think I have developed an addiction . Everything is fresh and so good!
label: positive

text: Food was great. Service was ok. Why make me show a vaccine card if half the staff has mask below nose?  So stupid. Drink timing was off. Server should ensure guest has drink pairing to match course. Not fun eating while your drink is waiting to be delivered.
label: neutral

text: Everything is delicious!!  We are vacationing here from NYC and we're blown away by how good the food was. I've had a lot of subpar vegan food in Florida so we went expecting much but this place is amazing!!!! Smoothie was soooo good, the vegan bowl was beautiful, the vegan hot dog was BOMB, brownie was so yummy. We ordered a bunch of food to go because the food was so good! My non vegan partner also really enjoyed his food so it's a win win for everyone!

text:
I wasn't super in the mood for Boba but we stopped here on a whim and wow was I ever glad we did. 

We ordered the grapefruit yogurt and kumquat lemon iced tea. They were both so refreshing and delicious that we decided order their popular monster drink (also VERY GOOD). Shoutout to Andrew for being patient and giving very helpful recommendations.

Will most definitely be recommending this place to all my family and friends. Can't wait to be back!

text: Update: Saturday seafood boil is an absolute must.   The Cajun spices and the seafood is fantastic and has the perfect combination of flavors and food.   I shared it with another person and it was perfect for 2

text: Thrilled with their food. I ordered a spicy tuna burrito with avocado, cucumber,sesame seeds, spicy mayo and soy sauce. My partner ordered a spicy tun bowl, I had a taste and it was amazing. text: Very disappointed. My partner tried the sushi a few weeks ago and loved it so we decided to try the Thai food tonight. The chicken was dry, flavorless and tasted a few days old. It honestly tasted like they batch cooked a bunch of the chicken at the beginning of the week and then topped the meal with that protein instead of making the chicken fresh with the meal.

text: The smoothie was super super sweet and very powdery not to mention it was way overpriced

text:
Wow! I'll be ordering from here often. I finally got a chance to grab some First Watch. The menu looked great but I decided to try the Floridian French Toast -- YUM! 

Everything was really good - I really enjoyed it all! I would recommend you try it next time you have a chance! 

I ordered to-go so I'm not sure how it looks inside -- someone ran in to get our order.

text:
We waited in line as they allowed someone with a huge order ( when they should've called it in!) to take almost everything left so my kids and I were in line waiting almost 20 minutes) were upset. text: Great diner stop on the road! The San Francisco burger was worth every calorie.  The staff was super attentive and accommodating.  I would highly recommend.

text:
BAMBU is not your typical bubble tea shop! It's an amazing Che Dessert place with Vietnamese drinks. I order the #2 Combo drink because it's filled with different toppings (jelly, red bean, white bean, taro, tapioca, and more). The "drink" is more like a chewy dessert and it's super filling!

Definitely give it a try if you're in the area. The inside doesn't have much seating though. And parking can be really difficult to find when the shopping center gets busy.

text: Delicious! Heard great things about this place, and we were not disappointed! The Breakfast Potatoes with the moray cheese sauce is a must-have!

text: Good coffee spot.  Hubby's first hand pour and he's hooked. The spicy egg sandwich is definitely a do again. Yummy
label: positive

text:
Exceptional service and food. 
We will definitely be back!
Don't forget to add yourself to the wait list.  Otherwise you might have a little bit of a wait.

text: We had so many people recommend this. I have never been to New Orleans so I was excited. I think maybe it was a bad season for coming. The taste was good, but they were cold and already pre-bagged. I wish they would have been fresh. Quick service and friendly.

text: I love the bourbon wings they're the bomb real crispy
label: positive

text: Fabulous! The people who work here are the best.  So kind.  The ice cream comes from Sarasota and it's decent. The doughnuts are so amazing just wish they sold smaller portions like 3 doughnuts.  Long hours.  8 am to 10 pm.  Parking might be a challenge at times yet I got lucky and went when it was slow.  Look forward to a return visit.

text: I have been seeing this place all over Facebook and finally headed over to Clearwater to check it out. I was blown away. So many options on their menu I had a hard time picking something! I went with the lobster and brie omelette and fell in love. Also if it's your first time they give you complimentary beignets and I loved that touch! They came with orange marmalade and I felt like I had dessert before breakfast. This place is only open till two so I can see it getting packed on weekends. But it is in a shopping plaza so there is plenty of parking available

text: I placed an order through the app and when I went to pick up they were closed, don't advertise being open and accept orders when there's no one in the restaurant to fulfill the orders, trying to get a refund is a nightmare because even a few hours after I ordered no one's answering the phone and customer service is absolutely no help at all.

text: Just tried Midtown boba for the first time and I have to say I was disappointed. I had the strawberry and coconut cream drink with boba and my husband had the milk tea with boba. Their boba was not sweet at all. Other Taiwanese boba spots make their boba with love (sweetness/honey, etc), but you can tell this place does not. The boba had no taste. It tasted very far from the authentic, Taiwanese, Asian-owned spots I usually go to. Although the boba wasn't good, the actual milk tea tasted good. You can only get a large and the drink selection is very limited. And yes, there is a cycling place connected to it, which is a bit odd. Fitness and boba don't really correlate, in my opinion.
label: neutral

text: This is the worse Starbucks I've ever had. I don't understand the amount of traffic because, the quality is horrible. I have giving them multiple tries from cold frappes, to hot  coffees. text: Such a cute place with fun decor and great coffee! I'm from Chicago and whenever I come to Largo to visit family this is our go to coffee shop! We love it! They have so many options and it's always been delicious! Trust me, we're slowly but surely making our way through the menu.

text: When it comes to coffee shops, I always opt for the plain, black, classic drip, pour over, or simply "house" coffee. Whatever their run-of-the-mill offering is. Reason being because if the shop is serving less-than-mediocre house coffees, they're probably dumping sugar into their speciality coffees to make up for it. Ambersons knocks it out of the park on all fronts. I ordered a Good Citizen blend and my partner ordered the Bar Nine blend. Their house coffee is spectacular and their baked goods are fresh. They also have a small market area to the right when you walk in where they sell some off the wall items, such as jowl bacon. The ambiance is very minimal and clean. Good tunes. Great service. I loved everything about Ambrose Coffee. Will be returning!
label: positive

text: What a pleasant experience! I ordered a western omelette which was delicious. Everything was served hot and quickly.
