text:
No, I'm not a ramen snob. But I've been to plenty of ramen places throughout my "ramen career." Rayaki in Cherry Hill is my go-to ramen takeout spot for this area. Why? Because the service is fast, the food is tasty, and they don't use any MSG. Hooray!!!

I usually get the tonkotsu or the shoyu ramen and am never disappointed. And, the takoyaki is the best appetizer ever.

If you're looking to avoid crowds because of the pandemic, this will not be your spot to dine in since it tends to get crowded (especially during weekends). But they do have a few tables out front that you can snag if you go during off-hours.

Since this sits in a complex with many other excellent Asian establishments, the parking lot fills up pretty quickly. But here's a hot tip...park around the back of the building--no one parks there besides the workers.
label: positive

text:

They also have a seviche which I also order and there's a big difference between seviche and salpicón., what I ate was definitely not a seviche. 200 bucks later was far from satisfied and Was a big let down. Even the beer was warm, like C'mon man.

I should have read the reviews before going to the place, after the owner replied to my review and by seen how he replied to others, I can tell you this:
The place is a reflection of poor management and ownership and would not return even if you pay for my meal 100 times..... the problem is not who pays or how I or anybody else pay, the problem is the food wasn't fresh and it's overpriced.

text: Mediocre food, and vegetarians and others beware! Walked by here on a weekend and lots of people there so we decided to give it a try on a weekday. Not crowded at all. I am a vegetarian and didn't see a lot on the menu for me so I ordered a grilled cheese with tomato, and NO BACON. My daughter ordered the donuts French toast, which sounded so good from the description. Our food was delivered by a different waitress and when I saw the pile of bacon on my sandwich I told her I was a vegetarian and ordered it without bacon. She was very nice about and said she was a vegetarian too and understood. About 2 minutes later my sandwich reappeared and our original waitress apologized and admitted it was her fault, and she put it in wrong. At first glance it looked ok but on further inspection, a few pieces of bacon were left behind, as apparently they thought it was acceptable to simply remove the greasy meat from my sandwich. I was disgusted that the kitchen/waitress would think this was appropriate. I specifically told both waitresses that I was a vegetarian. The donut French toast was not appealing either. It was dry and was served with small amount of a semi-congealed sauce  on top. The waitress finally came back and I told her about the problem with my sandwich. She made no apologies and never came back out. After 10 minutes I finally went in and spoke to the manager, who also made no apologies, but offered to make me something else.  I explained that they completely lost my trust and I didn't want anything else. I told them to just comp our food and be done with it. Absolutely unacceptable!!! No excuses!
label: negative

text:

They have indoor and outdoor seating with friendly staff. The HOLIDAY FLIGHT  MIMOSA looked interesting with colorful drinks.
FRENCH TOAST BITES- pieces of french toast with dipping sauce- a delicious appetizer.
If you are in the mood for a lunch item - definitely try the FRIED CHICKEN SANDWICH & make sure you get it SWEET HEAT-STYLE- such a good combination of salty, sweet and spicy! 

Highly recommend this cute place- be prepared to wait for quite a bit- especially for larger groups.

text:
This place is an enigma to me.  They rarely seem open, even fewer times do I see them with anyone actually standing there and ordering anything.  I get that it's on the edge of campus, and next to a bar, but there are lots of other (presumably) more filling option in the area to go to.

The staff are pretty indifferent to whether you want to give them money or not, and their through-the-window COVID (?) protections seem extreme.  It's a slow process, even if you're the only one there, and even if you're just getting cinnamon and sugar on yours.

If you like crepes, and you don't mind waiting, it's a good crepe.  It comes in a little pizza-slice style box, and if you ask nicely, they might even give you a napkin or two to wipe your face with later.  Is it the best fast-food style crepe I've ever had?  No.  Is it the best in NOLA?  Also no.  Is it good enough to walk over there if you're craving one and hope that they're open?  Yes. Probably even better if you're drunk and already void of some taste buds.

text:
I experienced none of the complaints mentioned in previous reviews today at brunch. There was a 45 minute wait, but we were seated within a half hour. It was delicious and everything I wanted my cheat day meal to be! Truffle fries were crispy and flavorful. My friend loved the cherry pierogies. Oh, let me not forget the yummy lattes! Perfect size to not fill up before a meal and tons of delicious flavors. White chocolate with almond milk is a winner!

& of course, the place is beautiful. Everything from the lighting to the silverware is a vibe!

10/10 and will definitely be back soon!

text:

I got a cafe de la olla coffee with a nopal huevos rancheros plate. The cafe de la olla had a very strong cinnamon taste and wonderful smell. With the huevos rancheros, I was expecting the nopal to be chopped up in the eggs or salsa but instead, the eggs were placed on top of a whole nopal leaf. I had never had nopal like this before but it was really tasty!! Having the whole leaf allows you to appreciate the nopal a lot more. 

Overall had a great experience here!

text:

I would say we were slightly disappointed with the food. I ordered a classic eggs and hash browns, and I thought they were a little plain and could have used more seasoning. My dad got the blueberry pancakes and was disappointed that the blueberries aren't cooked into the pancake. My fiancé got the steak and eggs and loved it, but his food came out late after everyone else had already finished eating. The waitress was kind enough to bring out a side of hash browns while he waited for his food. 

Overall I will definitely come back. They have great scones and it's a classic Sparks breakfast spot.

text:
I've been wanting to try this place for a few months, and was finally in the area at lunch time.  They are part of a small strip mall, so there was parking right out front.  Since it was my first visit, I had the guy behind the counter give me some recommendations.  I ended up with the Tonkotsu ramen (pork broth, pork chashu, pulled pork chashu, scallion, nori seaweed, seasoned egg, bamboo, and kikurage), gyoza, and honey milk/milk green tea.  I love that all their milk teas are actually dairy-free.

I ordered to-go, and I thought they packaged everything very well. There was so much ramen broth it didn't even fit into the plastic bowl provided, but that was useful for the next day's leftovers. I loved the flavor, and was very pleased that the pork was not stringy or fatty. The bubble tea was delicious!

This definitely fills the fast food ramen and bubble tea void in the area, and I would love to try more items on their menu.

I ordered the chicken and waffles Benedict. The potatoes were the best part - skin was crispy and the veggies mixed in were refreshing. Waffle was a dry.

I also tried the red velvet pancakes, which tasted more like cake than pancakes but were still quite good. I would definitely recommend you order the pancakes for the table though because it's way too big for 2, maybe even 3 people to finish.

text:
I've been a customer at the same
Location for over 20 years. (I think it has changed management more than once). The quality of there muffins has gone down so badly. Their Banana Nut was my absolute fave. It was the bomb. It tasted like my mom's homemade banana bread - super moist, tons of banana flavor, and plenty of nuts. I also use to enjoy the Carrot Cake muffin. It also tasted homemade. 
I've had both kinds of muffin recently and they were awful. So bad that my husband forgot I didn't like them anymore and he brought me one on Saturday and I refused to eat it. I just couldn't force myself to do it. 
Both flavor of muffin tasted weirdly the same. They tasted like the same batter/mix was used for both and they actually tasted like it could possibly be the same base they would use for a corn muffin. Both the carrot and banana muffins were dry and tasted almost exactly the same. Both had nuts and the carrot cake one had a weird glaze topping instead of the usual delightful cream cheese frosting. I don't know if the muffin mix comes from corporate like this awful concoction or if it's the franchisee cutting corners to save money, but either way both muffins are a horrible representation of this company. 

And one other note - the customer service has also gone downhill. I get that on the weekends, it's probably high school kids working that don't want to be there but a company needs to know who they put into positions as their representation of their company. The staff always acts completely put out by customers ordering food. They take no pride in their work. Again, this is just to let the owner know how their employees reflect on their business. Poorly, they reflect poorly....as do the muffins they make.
label: negative

text:

text: My absolute FAVORITE lunch spot in all of St Pete. I'm so lucky that my office is walking distance because I will never get sick of the food- and it's healthy! You get a choice of a base (like brown rice or greens), 2 vegetables/sides (you NEED to try the Mac n cheese), and 1 protein (they even have great vegetarian protein options like portobello mushrooms).

text:

DO HOOKAH HERE. The hookah tubes are busted and dirty.... The staff is very unprofessional and rude all they want is your money. text: A very easy, simple meal when you're ready to take a break from the Casinos. I really liked the open seating and the expedited service.  They had no trouble seating our table of eight. The menu items are basic like club sandwiches and French dips but everyone at the table was happy.  The Alfredo sauce was a hit with the kids.  I had the Asian chicken salad and the fresh cut veggies were appreciated.  I'd pass on the fried rice as we found that way too salty.  The French toast was devoured!

text: Absolutely heavenly baked goods! Like, unreal how good they are. I also got a grilled cheese, which was divine and came with a nice side salad, making it a complete meal for a decent price, which I totally appreciated. I also got a chai latte which was windy and not overly sweet.

text:
When we tried Wow Wow Hawaiian Lemonade for the first time, I was transported back to Hawaii and started missing the beach!
There are a lot of options for lemonade flavors and the staff are knowledgeable about flavor profiles and what you might like. We had the blackberry lavender and pina colada, they were both delicious and I would get either again in a heartbeat. 
The price might look high initially, however the ingredients are fresh and well worth it. I will be back soon!

text:
I really enjoyed my experience here. You could easily spend the entire day. From coffees to amazing teas, salads to desserts. Everything is beautifully prepared and delicious. 
The tea selection is really quite impressive and you can have it anyway you want it...even as a cocktail. 
I had the seasonal salad and it was fantastic. Fresh and filling. 
The restaurant has indoor and an indoor garden seating area. Both are spacious. Apart from the restaurant there is a gift shop with really exclusive items...from cards, ornaments, dinnerware, jewelry and toys. Also the book store...books are everything! There is also a section in which you can just enjoy coffee, tea and pastries. 
It's a lovely place definitely coming back.

text:

I ordered the mazapan milkshake and it has a nice smooth, creamy texture to it. It was sweet but not too overwhelming. I love the taste of the peanuts from the mazapan and they give you a whole one on top of the milkshake. My boyfriend ordered the pinguino and he really enjoyed it. If you love chocolate like he does, then I definitely recommend this one. 

They have a wide variety of shakes to choose from and it's so hard to choose just one. I'll definitely have to go back to try out the rest of the flavors that they offer.

text:

While we waited for our reservation, I went to the tea spot and ordered a Peachy Keen tea. We also browsed the gift shop and the upstairs section to marvel at the architecture. 

I had the French toast and my partner had the eggs Benedict. Both were tasty and the perfect meals to share (sweet & savory). This place is an awesome brunch spot and place to get some Instagram worthy photos!

text: Visited Jus the other day for my first time. I was greeted upon walking in by a friendly female employee who resigned to giving me a few minutes to look at the menu. I decided on a strawberry/banana smoothie with almond butter. It was a bit pricy, I believe about 7+ dollars for a medium, but from what I've seen that's pretty typical at smoothie and coffee places. The smoothie itself was made quickly and was delicious! The texture was perfectly smooth and there was just enough almond butter to taste while not overwhelming the other flavors. This is a good choice if you want to skip a full lunch and just get a drink, very filling.

text:
This. Place. Is. Bomb.

First, I've been to these concepts before. And they're all fine, because I'm a nerd, and like playing games, and like coffee.

But this spot has a full bar. Full disclosure: I'm doing dry January so I couldn't take advantage, but knowing I have the option is just *chef's kiss*.

Also, big game selection, from classic poker card decks to Clank: In Space, they've got what you need to pass too many hours in a cafe.

Food-wise, the seitan wings were super crispy and well sauced. Fries are also tasty, even after they get cold because you're planning your next move to make your play date cry or attempt to flip the table. Plus, all the food is plant based, so as a relatively conscientious omnivore I felt good about ordering whatever.

Very friendly staff, a cozy and bright space, and baby, you've got a Vichet's favorite new place going.

Also a funky, weird, fun tea selection. Dry January, what a larf!
label: positive

text:
Right across from our hotel and we were starving after a long day of checking out the city. Reviews were decent so we wanted to try. Ordered the Rotolo (pic looks cool on website), Escarole Caesar, and a large Margarita pie.  Server asked if we wanted anchovies on the salad, and even though my daughter ordered it, I said sure. I love a couple of anchovies on a Caesar salad, so I thought if I snuck a bite, bonus for me. Server came back - no Rotolo.  Ok - so we ordered the breadsticks.  Breadsticks and salad arrived.  Breadsticks - no dipping sauce, kind of a disappointment - pretty good flavor from obviously their dough, but nothing more than Olive Garden really.  Caesar salad - wildly underdressed, so we asked for more dressing.  More dressing added, and completely overloaded with anchovies. Again, I love them, but so anchovy forward that if you like a good Caesar salad, this was nothing like that. It was kind of disgusting.  I am pretty sure that Caesar Salad is not native to Italy.  This should flat out be removed from their menu unless they change the recipe.  Lemon or salt would go miles to help this.  We got the "metro" size pizza as we were hungry; presentation 10/10, price 2/10 (I think it was $28 for a marginally sized flatbread), crust 10/10 (great chew and char), sauce 2/10 (prob San Marzano, but salt and olive oil were nonexistent), cheese 1/10 (almost non existent), and a handful of fresh basil thrown around on the flatbread.  
Service was pretty much as nonexistent as the flavor on the pie - we had to flag someone down if we wanted something.  I wouldn't be inclined to write this review if one person asked how our food was - no one did.  Seemed like a healthy takeout business which is good for them.  However, if you're trying to pick back up, customer service goes a long way.
Service - 1/10
Food  - 3/10
Overall - 3/10
Chance of returning - 0%
label: negative

text: This was the first time for my date and I to go here, also our first date, Ricardo Andrade was our server. He was amazing! The best customer service I have received in a very long time.   When we go back we will defiantly ask for him again.  If you go here you should defiantly ask for him! The place is really a neat place and the food was good!

text:
I have to say, this is one touristy place that is 100% necessary and worth it in New Orleans.  I came during a recent trip on a weekend and there was a healthy line that moved quick.  Donut type pastries are one of my favorites so I was looking forward to them a lot. 

One order of beignets here is $3.40, and each order comes with 3 of them.  They come in a bag that has powdered sugar in them.  I also got the cafe au lait (medium is $3.50)  which seems like their most popular coffee choice.  Total came out to $7.50.  This place is cash only FYI.

The beignets were so perfect and delicious.  I guess they're so popular that they're always fresh and hot, at least at this location.  I almost had to slap someone when I bit into them!  So warm and doughy inside and the powdered sugar adds a delectable sweetness to it.  It was mind blowing and I couldn't get over how banging they were.  

The coffee was good too; it had a subtle sweetness to it and melted the beignets down really nicely.  It's nice and smooth and is a great compliment to the donuts.  I actually think the coffee is overpriced though; it's more expensive than an order of beignets by themselves.  

I think this was the best food experience I had in New Orleans.  It was so good that I came back again the next day for another round.  Definitely recommend it to everyone.
label: positive

text: I realized that some of the cars were taking forever. I thought they just ordered a lot but when it was my turn to order the girl didn't even welcome me or anything. text:

text: My fiancé went there Saturday night for his bachelor party. While there, he was playing the table games and drinking a few beers but not showing signs of over served in the least. He and one of his buddies went to the bar where his buddy bought them both a drink. The female bartender served my fiancé's friend, but not my fiancé. When he asked where his beer was, she said its coming then walked away. After waiting at the bar for about 10 minutes without getting his drink, I approached him at the bar asking what's taking so long. He reiterated what the bartender told him earlier that is was coming soon. He kept having to wave his hands to get the bartender's attention and she would just look at him and help someone else. He then asked the male bartender what's going on? He ignored him as the female bartender was explaining the situation. He approached us and said the bar staff got word to not sell my fiancé anymore alcohol and to talk to the manager for further info. Then a man in a suit walked up to us, asked for my fiancé's ID, wrote out his license number and explained that they are refusing to sell him alcohol. But he did not have a reason why. My fiancé has a Myotonic Muscular Dystrophy so he already stumbles and slurs when he is stone-cold sober. He only had at most 3-4 beers in the course of 3 hours. So he was not by any means, overly intoxicated. We explained the situation to the manger that he has a disease that makes him that way. He just looked at all of us and acted like his disease meant nothing. That my fiancé was over his drinking limit. All he wanted was a nice night out with his friends and family to celebrate our upcoming wedding! This is discrimination! We will be filing a formal complaint!
label: negative

text: Hands down one of the best coffee shops in Reno. I live in South Reno and make the drive to Midtown to visit JoStella's. I always have to get the breakfast burrito and my cold brew coffee to enjoy while I go though some emails.

text:
Grain and Berry was probably my favorite açaí bowl of all time! Açaí bowls are some of my favorite things to get when I travel and always looking for new combos/appearances of them. Well, the big glass bowl is comes in for starters was enough to sell me with how cool that is! 

The service was great, very friendly and bowls were fast. What I loved about the açaí was it was more puréed and not like ice. I hate when açaí places have icing açaí and is so annoying to eat! The toppings were a great combo and all the fruit was fresh. 

Really can't say enough good and really would recommend trying. Prices are on point with any other açaí place! Much worth the try!
label: positive

text: Sadly, I was really disappointed with my experience at Coffeebar this weekend. Still being relatively new to town, I've been searching highest rated ... fill in the blank, and trying to experience all Reno has to offer. Coffeebar popped up as very highly rated, so my boyfriend and I went there this weekend. First off, we showed up at 11, which was our mistake. If you're looking for assorted baked goods, definitely get there before 11 or you will be left to choose between a chocolate or plain croissant. I will say the plain croissant was pretty good though. We were somewhat bummed because my BF loves flavored coffees and I thought this place would definitely have them, but upon arrival we didn't see any listed and it was fairly busy, so we tried to keep it moving. I was also excited to try a lavender brown sugar latte I saw someone mention below, but didn't locate it on the menu, so I got my usual chai tea. Now, I love chai tea and I don't think I'm a snob. Before this weekend I had never met a chai tea latte I didn't like. Honestly, the Coffeebar chai tea latte was the worst I've ever had. I guess they were out of agave so they substituted vanilla, which was more than fine with me. The chai tasted like someone had made a very weak cup of chai tea, added half a shot of milk, and called it a day. No frothing, just unceremoniously dumped. It was super watery and there was absolutely no vanilla taste at all. For that matter, it was the furthest from a creamy latte I expected. My BF ordered plain coffee, but didn't find it to taste very good, so he actually made some coffee when we returned home instead. We also ordered 2 breakfast sandwiches which were pretty lackluster and ended up in the garbage. All in all it was a disappointing outing and I don't think we'll be back.
label: negative

text: The ladies working here were extremely rude. I came in with my husband and all he wanted was a cold brew. They didn't have it, fine. I ordered the Tres Leches latte. They started telling me they can't make it without whole milk even though tres leches means there are three milks in there. Whatever. text:
Had to stop at this staple! 

Was planning on going here way before our trip. After learning the actual history of this cafe made us want to go even more as it's more than just a cafe. 

It was started by a slave named Rose Nicaud. She purchased her freedom by selling coffee and beignets on Sundays on a push cart in the early 1800s. She saved the portion she was allowed to keep until she had enough to buy her freedom. 

The beignets were pillowy goodness!! There isn't any sugar in the dough itself...all the sugar is the powdered sugar on top. Flavor is delish and fresh. 

The coffee is delish as well. It's made with chicory which gives it its unique taste. 

It is cash only. Yelp states they take cards, but that isn't the case. 

There is plenty of seating but I suggest getting there early as they get crowded quickly.
label: positive

text: My favorite family-owned Hong Kong style bakery! I love stopping by to pick up buns after work. They always use the freshest ingredients and they have a great variety! I love getting the pandan egg tarts (which no other Philadelphia Hong Kong style bakery has). I also love how they have a different variety of buns made fresh daily. They make great drinks as well! I've tried their fruit teas, matcha, and smoothies and they were all perfectly made. I highly recommend this bakery to everyone! The staff members are also very friendly and they make sure you are well taken care of. I could not say enough good things about this bakery. Best baked goods without having to drive to Chinatown and they even taste better! I follow them on Instagram and keep up to date with what they have for that day.
label: positive

text:
This place is a truly unique find. Excellent food, spectacular service and incredible value for money.

It is a small, unassuming restaurant located in what I believe is a strip mall. When you walk inside, though, it is very beautiful. There's nice wall art and the colors and design of the place are very snazzy. The menu itself also has a simple, cool-looking design. They also highlight customer favorites in yellow, making it easier to make a selection. They have a bevy of options in terms of dishes, but at the same time it's not overwhelming. They have exactly enough options to give you good variety without it being too much. The only problem is that everything sounds so delicious that you don't know what to choose!! That's a happy problem to have, though. 

In terms of parking, again, it's in a strip mall so parking is super easy and there's plenty of it available. You'll have no issues there.

In terms of the quality of food, it is absolutely spectacular. I'm from Orlando and I've eaten at several breakfast joints here, and this beats nearly all of them. It is one of the best breakfasts that I've ever had in a restaurant. Hands down!! And in terms of value for money, it punches well above its weight. The food has the taste of a home cooked meal that was made with love and care. Even the presentation of the food shows a certain artistry and attention to detail. I would've paid twice what they charged me for their food without batting an eye because of how good it was! I had a breakfast for two for under $35! I couldn't believe it considering how high quality the meal was! I could scream from the rooftops about how delicious the food was. You have to try this place if for nothing else but the food. The restaurant is also incredibly clean. Absolutely immaculate. 

The service was one of a kind to me. It's incredibly rare in my experience to find service that is genuinely friendly. Most of the time, it's polite and professional, but here people are friendly and warm to you, and you can tell that it's genuine. They're nice to you, refill your coffee, everything that you'd expect from great service. So hats off to them there.

There's only one reason that I'm not giving them five stars: the staff generally doesn't mask up. This is out of step with current CDC guidelines on indoor masking, and I can't give five stars to a restaurant that isn't following those protocols. It's a crying shame, because otherwise this place would get ten stars out of five. It's doing everything else to perfection. That said, there's booth seating available, and if you arrive early you should be able to get a booth that will give you some distance from others. Wear your mask and you should be fine.

So, overall it's a phenomenal establishment. If they were masking indoors, it would put it over the top for me. As I mentioned earlier, though, the booth seating does give you a buffer from other customers and should be available if you get there early. As long as you don't mind the maskless servers, then this is truly a gem of a restaurant.
label: positive

text:
I would just like to start off by saying my attempt to write this review was derailed for approximately 20 minutes because just looking at the photos from this place at the top of its yelp page ignited a strong desire to purchase property in New Orleans and a subsequent thorough search on Zillow. 

I'm back now and ready to talk about Cafe Du Monde. You shouldn't even be looking at reviews on Yelp because regardless of what they are (they're good, as you can see), it's just an absolutely essential stop on anyone's visit to New Orleans. The only excuse is celiac disease but even if you have that, you can and should still sample the coffee so never mind, there is no literally no excuse.

Cafe Du Monde is a New Orleans INSTITUTION. Just go. It's close to anything you should want to do in New Orleans anyway and it's open 24/7. Please go, please try the beignets (unless you're celiac in which case, I'm sure in this day in age there are gluten free beignets somewhere). I highly recommend covering your lap with a napkin or leaning really far over the table because if not, you will be absolutely covered in powdered sugar as evidenced by any place you look on the dining area's floor. 

The coffee/cafe au lait is actually also pretty significant in New Orleans food history because during the civil war, union blockades cut off the port of New Orleans to trade and chicory was added to extend coffee's shelf life. So make sure you try that shit out too but be careful because it's scorching when they hand it to you. 

This place is also cash only. 

Both times we stopped by, there was some performer outside with blaring music. I found this really annoying but many presumably normal people around us seemed to enjoy it. 

We also sampled the frozen cafe au lait which was orgasmic. 

You really shouldn't need any more convincing at this point.
label: positive

text:

text:
Cafe Du Monde is a must-visit when traveling to NOLA and you definitely cannot miss it given the constant line pretty much during all times of the day outside of the shop. 

We got a bag of the beignets as well as a cup of cafe au lait. Service was fast and efficient and the beignets were all packaged in bags already by the cashier. The bags all come with three beignets, plenty for one person. The beignets were super fresh, crispy, and hot when we got them, and despite the mountain of powdered sugar, had just the right amount of sweetness. It was the perfect pairing with the cafe au lait (we even bought a can of the ground coffee to bring back home)! 

I really loved how simple the business was, to this day, they still only serve beignets and a few drinks (less than 10 items on their menu). Beignets are no-frills, but satisfying, definitely stop by and give it a try! Next time I visit NOLA, Cafe Du Monde will definitely be my go-to breakfast place. 

Tips and Notes: 
1) There is a long line for dining in, but there is a much much shorter to-go line on the other side of the cafe. There are plenty of park benches on the riverwalk not too far from the cafe to take and enjoy your food at. 

2) Cafe Du Monde is cash only. 

3) For those concerned about dining in and COVID, the eating area is outdoors and covered by an awning. The tables are decently spaced out.
label: positive

text:
Normally I just have the sweets here, but today I decided on lunch. Just a simple plate of cevapi, but it was pretty extraordinary. Perfectly cooked cevapi (10 of them!) on a whole Lepinje that had been toasted on the grill before the sausages were added - so the middle was warm, but the edges were crispy and heavenly.

If you've never had Lepinje bread, you don't know what you're missing - it is one of the most flavorful, chewy, springy, stretchy, fantastic types of bread I've ever eaten. And this was no exception.

The Bosnian coffee was a delight, and it was served beautifully (see the pic!). They give you a sugar cube to put in the bottom of your tiny cup and pour the coffee over. The grounds of the coffee float to the top, so that's what comes out of your little pitcher first, but they eventually sink to the bottom, and it is a nice strong cup of coffee.

I also added a piece of their baklava, and it was all the flaky, nutty syrupy goodness you could ever want.

The lady running the place was friendly and helpful as always, and also as always, the place was bright and clean, including the bathroom!

Today was Monday, and, I guess because they are closed on Tuesday, the bakery case was empty. They did have the baklava in the back, for which I was thankful, but your best bet, if you are looking for sweets, is to go Wednesday through Sunday.  But they always have the savory goods, so don't skip this place on any day! Well, except on Tuesday, when they're closed.
label: positive

text:
Visiting Cafe Du Monde is a experience you must have in NOLA! 

Beignets are crispy, doughy, and sugary all at once. Coming in at less than $4 for a set of 3, they're not even overpriced, especially compared to food in the area.

Cafe du Monde is hardly a typical restaurant, so here are some tips if you go:

1) Expect long lines. You seat yourself when you see an empty table. It's huge and the restaurant is partly open air. 

2) Service probably won't be quick, so make sure you know what you want to order!

3) Don't inhale while you're eating--you don't want to be coughing up powdered sugar. They provide water though!

4) Bring cash--That's the only payment they accept. It seems like they round up the total to the nearest dollar too.

5) To avoid long waits, consider going to the multiple other Cafe du Monde locations or going during an off hour (the main location is open 24 hours)!
label: positive

text:
Stumbled upon Croissant D'Or Patisserie while exploring New Orleans and ended up returning one more time before our trip ended! 

True to their name, you can't go wrong with getting any of their croissants. We got the almond and ham and cheese croissants and they were both fresh, flakey, and decently sized. 

The cakes are also a must-order. The Africaine Charlotte (checkered chocolate and vanilla mousse cake) was phenomenal and I probably could have eaten another slice. Every bite just melted in my mouth! Bonus points for being such an aesthetic cake too! 

I also really enjoyed their frangipane, which is an almond paste tart. I would order this again and it seemed like others enjoyed it too as there were only a few left. 

We also got the lemon cake, croquant, and coffee. These were good as well, but definitely give the items above a try first! 

Overall, Croissant D'Or Patisserie is a beautiful French-style bakery. Definitely visit if you pass by and enjoy some coffee and cake in their courtyard.
label: positive

text: My food was good and the service was good. I was enjoying my day and my time there. Then I got the bill. A 16 oz aluminum bottle of Bud Light was $6.50. That is SO MUCH higher then other bars in the area for the same thing. I am in Palm Harbor, Fl - not Yankee stadium. Everything was good, I just think the bottle beer was over priced.
label: positive

text: I don't have high expectation but my gf was hungry at the time. I got the korean BBQ wings and my gf got the Bun Bo Hue. We also ordered drinks. I have high standards when it comes to BBH. That said... its no where close to being to being good. Not even if its catered towards people who don't know that traditional taste. Korean wings were awful. They are just overly breaded wings inconsistantly drizzled in some type of korean bbq sauce. I already had hesitations since this place is known more for the drinks and desserts more than food. What really turned me off was the service and the atmosphere. I understand this place is new and they are probably understaffed but a lot of things can be changed. Very loud... blaring TV with screaming children is typical for a place like this, but its the also the very loud blenders that make it hard to have any conversations. The owners need to understand that if its a small restaurant than please contain the noise levels. Finally, the unprofessional rude attitude. We had to order at the front desk, pay, take a number and sit. We ate what we can.... and sat and waited without any drinks that we ordered. They gave us a number to bring our food, but they DO NOT bring the drinks. You have to get the drinks yourselves at the front desk when they are done making it!!! We sat their not knowing this "policy" and when we finally asked about our drinks. They told us that it was done. Sitting at the front for us to get. Not only that... its been so long that my mango shush is more like a mango juice. Lol. I have never ate anywhere, even a self service atmosphere, where your food was brought out without any drinks. And when I asked, they said it was our job to monitor our drinks. To get it ourselves... I appreciate that they used actual fruit and not powder to make drinks, but overall the experience was terrible. For anyone thinking about going here, order drinks to go and don't bother getting anything to eat. You might just be doing them a favor.
label: negative

text: I've never gone at 6am when it was open on time. I don't write reviews, so certainly wouldn't rate anyone this bad if it wasn't over a long period of time (about 3 years) and on such s as consistent basis.  I've driven by even 10-15 minutes after 6 and no one was there. I would  love to get coffee before work, but can't take 10-15 minutes out of my commute to stop at the Dunkin right next to my house. They're expensive. They're slow here.  More than half the time the breakfast sandwiches, wraps, and roll ups are not heated all the way through. I think they expect heat trapped in a paper bag to be what actually melts the cheese or makes the eggs warm enough not to seem like they just came out of the fridge. It hasn't seemed to be managed well at all, and there seems to be lot of young people employed but not enough of them actually moving behind the counter.
label: negative

text:
Service was good but the food was okay. We started off with Crispy Green Beans and Chicken Bites, both were delicious. The green beans had the perfect crunch and a delicious sauce with a little spice. The bites were very crunchy which I loved and you can pick from three different sauces.

The pho was a let down considering the name of the restaurant is Mopho. I love pho and eat it frequently but the pho here tasted more like ramen. I got the beef pho but I think the mushrooms and egg in it made it less like pho. That didn't bother me, but the broth was very bland and that's usually the best part. 

We shared a blondie for dessert and didn't even finish it. There were too many flavors going on with this dessert that just didn't work. It had a miso caramel, blondie, toasted apples on top with a sesame whipped cream. It was an interesting combination but it didn't work. 

The appetizers were great, but everything else was okay.
label: neutral

text: Easily one of the best cold brews in town. They are also lightening fast and all the staff are wonderful and friendly. Park on the side or in the back of the building (parking is free during the day) to avoid having to back out onto Gallatin
label: positive

text:
This place has great food, great drinks, really great service and lovely outdoor patio. All great combinations to bring a date or even take out relatives.  This place is both impressive and has great grub and it's in the heart of downtown SB. 

By far the king of brunch in SB!
label: positive

text: In the grand scheme of McDonald's I haven't had that bad of an experience here. The line does get super backed up sometimes and once they forgot my fries but didn't open the lobby so I had to wait in the drivethru a second time to get them (i was craving them and they're kind of expensive now for a large) so that ended up being almost an hour trip to get fast food but other than that I don't have any complaints. It's clean and usually has friendly service
label: positive

text: Yummy freshly made pastries (incl gluten free!) as well as nice strong espresso (Caffe D'Arte). Still a bummer masks aren't worn... but if u go during one of their many slower times (hello late afternoon middays ) less of an issue.
label: positive

text:
Order: Chicken Tacos w/ House-Made Asian Slaw, Kalbi Aioli Sauce & Lime | Beef Burrito w/ Jasmine Rice, Jack & Cheddar Cheese, Romaine, Pickled Carrots, Sour Cream & Kalbi Aioli Sauce

After circling the Foundry, Kalbi Taco Shack had the longest line & the most creative menu so we thought we'd give it a go & dayummmm it was so flavorful & satisfying!!! We loved both the tacos & burrito. Will definitely be back!
label: positive

text: Absolutely love the coffee from Suprema. I swing by once a month to get a bag of coffee to brew at home and always get an espresso while I'm there. Staff is  friendly and service is quick. For a special treat, try their hot chocolate. It's top tier!
label: positive

text: Fantastic!! Healthy options and it all tastes delicious. They can be busy so be prepared to wait a bit but the wait is worth it!! You have to try there frittatas and potatoes. Highly recommend.
label: positive

text:
This spot was on my list as soon as I booked my flight to New Orleans. Finally made it here with my boyfriend on our last day. 

We were greeted promptly and greeted by our server Jonathan. Jonathan was great and very friendly. 

We started with coffee. It was delish and fresh. 

For my meal I had the biscuits and gravy. It came with eggs, bacon, and a fried green tomato. Everything on this plate was absolutely delicious in flavor. The sausage gravy was one of the best I've ever had. Biscuit was airy and delish. This meal was made with love. 

My boyfriend had Bananas Foster Pain Perdu. Delicious as well. It was nice to see it was made with a baguette vs other bread. He enjoyed it a lot. I had a bite and enjoyed it as well. 

We also got a side of chorizo and a side of pork sausage. The chorizo as also the best we've had.

A super delicious, filling and fresh breakfast!
label: positive

text: Honestly one of the best Ramen houses in Boise! also they have boba and it is always so delicious!!! try the black garlic ramen! AMAZING!!!
label: positive

text:
I was visiting friends in St. Pete and we decided to hunt down a local shop for some coffee. I am very much a latte lover and always looking to experience new and exciting flavors and trends. When coming to Kahwa just know they have exceptional coffee but most of their choices are pretty straight forward. Don't try and hunt down anything too fancy here :) 
I got a vanilla latte with oat milk and it was smooth and delicious. It was well balanced and I enjoyed it.
label: positive

text: Just passing through Tampa on our way home from Sarasota and we were looking for a place for lunch and this place has a great rating on yelp so my wife and I stopped in there to try it hard to find place kind of hidden on the end of a strip center but we waited on by a waiter named Philip and everything was great I had a wrap with chicken and corn and God knows what else they put in it but it was delicious on whole wheat wrap and she had a wrap totally different with blackened chicken and they gave her a side of broccoli and she practically licked the plate and I had a side of fries crispy crispy crispy everything was great this is an easy five star
label: positive

text: I wanted Boba & decided to try a new spot! Sharetea is located in a strip mall so I always overlooked them! Let's just say, I won't overlook them anymore! The staff is super friendly & had no problem giving suggestions or letting me know what's more popular! I decided on the Oreo Ice Blended & added a scoop of ice cream on top! It was delicious! I can't wait to go back & try more!
label: positive

text: very good Wawa ! Recommend a lot for anyone nice clean and always fresh ! Or..... maybe I just love Wawa
label: positive

text: Didn't realize how much I'd missed coffee shops like this!
label: positive

text: The volcano roll was amazing!! I shared crab rangoons and I've never had them before but they were delicious. The workers are also really nice and they take so much time and effort in what they make. Definitely coming back again.
label: positive

text: They know how to make a breakfast sandwich, best food I've had at a cafe. Also you can't beat the neighborhood vibes and comfortable seating. The owners are super nice and make a fantastic coffee. Couldn't recommend a place more.
label: positive

text:
This is my personal Disneyland. I have been going here for years and I'm now lucky enough to work in the area a few times a month. I can't get enough of the amazing verity of food and goods. I have also never had a bad meal from any of the dozens of restaurants. I won't very go into my favorite picks because I have always enjoyed whatever I have tired.

Just a heads up it's super crowded on weekends and lunch time and parking can cost an arm and a cheese steak. 

This place is a must visit for any foodie.
label: positive

text: This new location is everything. It's more spacious and still so quaint! I highly recommend this for a weekend brunch or a night out since I have done both. The wine list is divine and the food is equally yummy, and somewhat unexpected. I like the fact that the menu doesn't go on for days, so far anything I have tried or chosen has been fabulous. I look forward to the next go!
label: positive

text: I was greeted when I walked in the door and treated like immediate family. The restaurant was very clean from the tables to the mirrors on the wall. The service was extremely fast. The food was prepared fresh.
label: positive

text: Do not come here if you have allergies or special diet restrictions. They were unhelpful and not willing to make any accommodations for my allergies. You get small portions and they do not have many options on the menu at all. The options they did have were all premade and we were very disappointed.
label: negative

text: There's no bad thing to say. For 100 years and variety, the doors are still open and always busy tells the story. If anyone says anything bad about Hershel's, look for a fight, with a little fat on it.
label: positive

text: The dinner portions are disappointing. They're the size of lunch portions, at least when you get it to go. The Amazing dish and Pad Thai are way too sweet.
label: negative

text: I cannot say enough good things about this new business! All of the staff was very welcoming. The inside of the cafe is small and quaint! I love that there is optional seating upstairs if you would like. Lovely place to hangout and spend time with cats while enjoying a drink from their cafe. Definitely will be back!
label: positive

text: The food is always delicious and the staff helpful and pleasant to interact with. I might be spoiled but it seems like every time I come in they give me a sample of something, and that little treat makes the experience even better. I love about an hour away, but I just have to have this food when my cravings kick in
label: positive

text:
We had reservations here for Christmas Day Reveillon Dinner. It was supposed to be a 4 course meal but ended being only 3 for us because after 2 plus hours we requested the check.

The servers were trying their best and fielding several complaints from tables around us. It was obvious there was some back of the house issues and I felt bad for the servers that had to take the brunt of the blame. They were so overwhelmed that nobody even batted an eye at our request for a check before the end of meal or gave thought to apologies or make it rights. In my opinion, management failed the servers and left them out to dry. 

Advertised menu items were not available. We were only served one drink and never asked if we would like another. Honestly it got to the point that it was amusing instead of infuriating. 

What food we did eat was good. Understandable that it was Christmas and may have been dealing with staffing shortages. There appeared to be plenty of staff just not utilized in best way possible.
label: negative

text:
So good! Not your average cafe. Didn't have any of the sweet stuff but the Mediterranean dishes were great. A little higher on the price tag for breakfast and brunch but I definitely got what I paid for! Gotta come again!
Sorry no pics. Ate it all lol.
label: positive

text: Great ice cream in Irvington with lots of unique flavors.  There is a parking lot in the back, as well as another entrance, so do not be concerned if it appears there is no street parking.
label: positive

text: Love this place and the idea behind it! The owners are so friendly and talkative! I loved it when they told us about the cats and their personalities! The cake pops were absolutely delicious and super cute! The aesthetic of the place was so pretty, even the bathrooms were decorated nicely. Would definitely recommend if you want to relax with some cute kitties! (:
label: positive

text:
My husband still talks about the Shopsin Club to this day, and we visited maybe a 6 months ago now. It's easily one of the top sandwiches you'll ever have. The cran miso spread is a game changer. Unfortunately for us, we live in Miami. Fortunately for us, we have fam that live in Philly, so we get to visit pretty often. I'm positive Middle Child will be added to our "must visit" list each trip. 

A very delish spot, highly recommended for a quick breakfast or lunch spot.
label: positive

text:
Brunch! A cool place to have one. The line to get in is always long, especially on the weekends. Problem is, waiting area is very small and they don't do messaging. They said they tried it before but it didn't work? My recommendation is, still use the messaging system, if the patrons don't show up in 2 min, move to the next one and strictly enforce the 2 min rule. That way, patrons can wait in their vehicle during cold seasons.

On to the food!!! I went with old school but with 3 eggs and bacon. Then wifey had the chicken salad. Then we had the cheesecake pancake, which is a must-have here. Really enjoyed the meal and such a cool place with good service except for  the tight space waiting area and with no other place to hang out other than right outside which is not feasible in cold weather.
label: positive

text:
Listen. I read through everyone's reviews prior to going to Farmacy Vegan today. 

I WILL RETURN. 

Location:
-I went around 12:30 pm, so I didn't experience any parking or traffic issues. Just parked on the street and walked a block.
-Finding the location within the Duckweed Urban Grocery store was easy.

Food:
-Churro Whoopi's Pie was perfection ... 10/10
-The Classic Philly Cheesesteak was great ... 8/10
-The Jerk Pasta Salad was amazing. The only downfall was too much sauce (for me) ... 9/10

Price:
-$$ 
-I mean, it's vegan food. Anything healthy costs these days (unfortunately). 
-Don't let that stop you. Just give the place a try. Splurge one time and you won't regret it. 

Sorry, no pictures. I was hungry, and excited.
label: positive

text: The food was very delicious. Eggs were cooked to perfection, the sauce was very flavorful, and it was very delicious overall. The servers and hostess were very nice. The only reason I took off a star is because the biscuit was a little dense and dry but overall very enjoyable
label: positive

text: This is a small bakery with the BEST steamed buns ever! I tried the bbq pork, curry chicken, mushroom chicken, and teriyaki chicken buns. Omg! I was not disappointed, but I did come away with a favorite. My favorite bun was the mushroom chicken bun! Mmm! It was a sweet and savory bun with sautéed onions inside. I bought them around noon and they were hot and ready to go!  There are several different bun fillings to choose from and I can't wait to try the other ones.
label: positive

text:
Myself and an old friend that I haven't seen in a while stopped here for lunch yesterday. We've both been wanting to try it out and the stars finally aligned for both of us.

We were able to get a table right away. The waitress came straight over to greet us and take our drink order. We both ended up going with burgers, fries, and a couple beers.

The burger was actually really good, as were the fries. It's rare, for my tastes, to find a place that nails both. Usually, it's one or the other. The service, the third aspect of a great experience, was also good.

I would definitely come back. The food was great, the service was good, and the prices were surprisingly reasonable (since they're practically on the UNR campus, I thought for sure it would be overpriced).
label: positive

text: Do not arrive at what they say is closing time. I was rejected for service as I entered the building one minute before the posted closing time. Customer service is not what it used to be. So. If the say they close at 4PM they really mean 3:50. Food is however good.
label: neutral

text:
Busy cafe. Limited seatings during peak times. Difficult to social distance but at least cafe had the doors open. No mask enforced at all. We were in line and of 25 people in front of us, only 3-4 were wearing masks. Everyone were standing right next to each other. A few people were coughing and sneezing. I was pretty nervous. 

After a long wait, we ordered. They check vaccine cards before you order. We also snagged a table before ordering since it'll be difficult to find an open table. 

We ordered a latte and some beignets. The latte was ok, and the beignets were good but doughy too, not like the flaky soft pillowy ones we've had at a different cafe in the past. 

There were only 2 unisex bathrooms so there was a line. Bathrooms were decent.

There was a utensils and napkins station so you actually have to go get your own. I find this inconvenient especially when it's super busy and you have to fight your way through crowds just to get some napkins.

Not sure if I'd return. Maybe during a less busy time, as the beignets were decent.
label: negative

text: Tried the Rooster Diner for breakfast today.  I have to say that they did a nice job renovating the former Denny's. New flooring, booths, tables and decor.  I had a breakfast entree and my wife had a salad. The eggs were cooked nicely. Hash-browns, rye toast and scrapple were very nice.  My wife declared the Santa Fe salad prepared as described and very good.  The service was good although the restaurant was not very crowded. We will return to try their lunch.
label: positive

text:
Tonight at 11:30 the manager threatened to call the police on me because she refused to honor their apple pie deal.  The sign at the drive through still says that if they forget to ask you about MyMcDonalds Rewards, you get a free apple pie.  The young lady at the drive through didn't ask about the rewards program, so I asked for the apple pie.  The angry night manager yelled through the window to me, claiming that they had quit that deal a month ago, and that I could just sit in the drive through until she "call da police, 'cuz he ain't getting' no apple pie."  
If that's so, then why have they served me the free apple pie twice in just this last week when they've forgotten to ask me each time?  And why does their drive through sign still say they have that deal if they don't?
What a nasty, bitter manager lady.  I should patronize the Wendy's, Culver's, Chick-fil-a, or other places right up the street whether the management is more polite.
label: negative

text:
What a pleasant surprise. The food is good, atmosphere comfortable, the people are very nice and attentive to you.  Good menu, easy to reach, highly recommended.

Merritt Weisinger
Spanish Springs, NV
label: positive

text:
The last couple of times I've been here I haven't been impressed. Both times, I ordered a flavored lemonade, the last time, it was the pina Colada lemonade. There wasn't any flavored added, very bland. I'd say it was a one off, but I think they always forget or very stingy with the flavorings. It also takes forever to get the drink, very slow service. For the price you pay for a lemonade, you'd think it would be amazing and good service. 
I also wanted to try the flights since there are so many options but they don't offer them if you go through the drive thru. This is very disappointing since we are in a pandemic and all. There could at least be communication on the drive thru sign about what they refuse to serve. I'm disappointed and hope they improve.
label: negative

text: Don't bother ordering pickup from here or their sister restaurant Baileys range. Poorly managed and 100% certain they'll mess up your order every time. Is it really that hard to read an order? At these locations apparently so...
label: negative

text:
A cute little business!!!! Blenders is great to grab on a hot day after going to the beach or if you're craving a quick, refreshing drink. 

Blenders offers many different juice/smoothie options and also give one free supplement such as vitamin C, chia seeds, flax seeds, etc. for every drink. 

I got the tropical guava with the "total" supplement. I loved the flavor!! The consistency was thick at first, but melted and became more of a juice pretty quickly. The portion size is pretty big, I would definitely split with someone the next time I come.
label: positive

text: The food here is amazing, the most I've enjoyed breakfast in a long time. The crawfish omelette was amazing and so flavorful and tasted so fresh and healthy, who knew eggs could be that good?! As for the beignets, I tried beignets both here and cafe du monde and they were hands down better here...lighter, fluffier, less greasy, and more powdered sugar, I'm talking not even close. I also liked the environment much more, the jazz music playing inside and the cute cafe vibes all around, it really seemed to sum up the feeling of the city. So delicious and I would highly recommend it
label: positive

text:
The coffee is a bit odd. I ordered a mocha latte. It was lukewarm and had a funny flavor - almost like a little whiskey was in it. 

I will say that the egg and cheese croissant was outstanding. They grilled it and used real egg (not that nasty scrambled stuff). 

Wouldn't go back for coffee but I would for the sandwich!
label: neutral

text: Recommend by our ghost tour guide.  Loved the atmosphere, ambiance, theme and the food.  Service was good.  Usually niche restaurants have a good theme or good food, this has both.
label: positive

text: We love CH Cafeteria! They have delicious chocolate and coffee with a Mexican twist. The owners are super kind and friendly. You can browse local arts and crafts for sale and they make the most interesting artisan chocolate flavor combinations. Their chocolate mousses are to die for and come in many varieties; try the blonde chocolate! One of my favorite beverages is their chaicolatte, it's a chai spiced hot chocolate. CH is such an enjoyable alternative to the big chain coffee stores.
label: positive

text:
Everything I've had here is good.  Portions are more than enough, service is great and the prices are reasonable.
Wish I could go more often
label: positive

text: I've been here a few times for breakfast. I like their French toast platters. Their portions are big.
label: positive

text: Cool, down to earth juice bar in Germantown. It was a crazy cold and rainy day when we attempted to walk around the strip and this was a bright spot. I ordered a spinach smoothie which was tasty and not overly sweet. My group really enjoyed their smoothies too. You can also ordered some food if you're hungry!
label: positive

text: I do not recommend this location. The baristas are rude. Poor service is one thing but outright rude to customers is unacceptable. Especially, when you can get yourself a $7 coffee at any other place in the quarter.
label: negative

text: Came on a date. Service was good, ambience was nice and most of the food was good. However, I am compelled to leave this poor review because of the 20 dollar sardine appetizer. They literally opened a can of sardines, put it on a plate still in the tin and served it. That's it. Not kidding. As my girlfriend noted, it would be no different had they just opened a can of Starkist tuna and served that. Or a can of cat food for that matter. The "chef" should be ashamed of himself. My regret is I didn't send it back immediately. Try Via Locusta next door. They will at least take the pasta out of the box and cook it for you rather than put a box of uncooked Ronzoni on a plate and charge you $20.00 for it.
label: negative

text:
Meh-Underwhelmed although my waiter was super attentive and great
Ambiance uninventive cliched and aged getting close to faded ...
Very unclear what the fuss is about  this place to me just a super average french aspiring  bistro you find anywhere ...
First although place totally empty after 3 seating attempts they kept seating me a black solo female diner in the bleachers - busy corridor aisle ,next to drafty window and restock settings,last next to cashier -WHY????
i have read about restaurants treating diners of a certain hue this way but this is first time in my 50 years  I actually experienced ...
Second food meh / started with escargots which were served like a weird soup in a bowl of strange mushroom liquid and mixed with bella mushrooms - hello for $15 you better give me all escargots with loads of butter /garlic /parsley ...
Second course steak frites steak was cooked perfect but frites frozen seeming average...
Star in the meal  was the tarte tatin which is one of the best I have had in a long time... 
drinks pricey but adequate 
overall would not recommend - this is not true french cuisine by any stretch  - have had loads better food in this part of philly and philly in general ...
Maybe they were better on their glory days but looks like the show is long over
label: negative

text: Not kid friendly, and from the few times I have been there, I get the impression they don't want to be kid friendly. Server tried encouraging us to go somewhere else. Can you believe that? And apparently don't show up between lunch and dinner or they'll get really annoyed and seat you outside at a table with direct sun beating down on you and your kids. Then no one will come ask if you want water.
label: negative

text: I have yet to find a coffee shop as good as Picasso's. Great atmosphere to get coffee with a friend, study or read. Coffee is amazing across the board, staff are friendly and helpful. You have to see for yourself!
label: positive

text: not a single good thing about their donuts, extremely overpriced.
label: negative

text: We have a similar ice cream shop back at home so going in, I knew what to expect and it definitely did not disappoint. They had a ton of flavors and mix-ins to choose from. I went right after lunch so they were not busy at all but I'm not sure if that is the case later in the day. It was overall very good and I would definitely come back. I got a small cheesecake flavored with graham cracker mix in and it was around $6.50 which is on the more expensive side in my opinion so keep that in mind if you're coming here on a budget. Other than that, I would 100% recommend giving this place a try!!
label: positive

text:
Dishes were expensive and quite bland. The rice bowls we had the pineapple chicken and grilled chicken which were ok nothing special much 

For the cost of 5 for takeout I could have had a full service sit down dinner nearby.

Normally the cost is not an issue ... if the food was out of this world I wouldn't have mentioned but it was really much of anything unique we were expecting
label: neutral

text: Amazing street tacos. Way way mote authentic than casa taqueria. Did i say WAY more ? Fresh, packed full of flavor. Meats are high quality. Many times, restaurants will compensate flavor with too much salt, this is the case and many Mexican joints except for this one. They have an incredible three tacos for the price of two on Tuesdays. Run or bike here now. Yum yum tum!!!
label: positive

text: I know Starbucks is a huge chain and they aren't a local place but man do I love grabbing a quick coffee here! They have a drive thru which can run onto the street but hello! There are hardly any drive thrus in Santa Barbara! This is such a perk and I appreciate it! The staff go above and beyond each time I go, whether in person or drive thru. They greet me and if my drink isn't ready right away, they always take the time make small talk. Love their staff! I also appreciate the outdoor seating for when I want to relax and get some Vitamin D. If there is a long line, I'd recommend ordering inside or on the app for a quick pickup. The sugar cookie latte with oat milk is my favorite drink.
label: positive

text: Jamba Juice is great! I always get the Red Gummy but today I decided to try something different and I regret it... I got the Chunky Strawberry and requested 2% Milk instead of Soy Milk. And I asked for blueberries on top as well. When I went in, the employee handed my Bowl. Assuming I paid but I'm a good person to stand there and wait for him to take my payment and he forgot. I could have easily walked away but I didn't.... When I got home I noticed I did not have Blueberries on top of my bowl. And the moment I tasted my Bowl I could tell it was with soy milk and not 2%... immediately that RUINED my entire digestive system and my stomach hurt right away. I'm just a bit disappointed. I guess I should stick with the Red Gummy moving forward :/
label: neutral

text: Went through the drive-thru this morning and asked what kind of muffins they have. The girl replied, "same as we have everyday." Well, I wouldn't know because I don't come to your establishment everyday, and now I have good reason not to.
label: negative

text: We went to tootsies looking for dessert and we were thrilled with everything they had to offer. We chose their ice cream which is all incredible(they let us sample a bunch of flavors). We also had a fresh donut which was delicious. All the workers were extremely nice and helpful. Thank you tootsies.
label: positive

text: I loved this place I was amazed with the chai pancakes and the lavender honey latte and the butterfly matcha was very smooth and very good and beautifully decorated. The service was so sweet everyone was very respectful and sweet!! I would 10/10 recommend this cafe to anyone who was looking for a quick brunch! Good vibes the music was a nice little pick too overall everything was a great experience!
label: positive

text: "CBD and Chill" is displayed in neon lights when you walk into Anzie Blue so I knew I was going to have a good time. This is a true day-to-night transition sort of restaurant since you can get coffee or cocktails, and brunch is served all day. We went on a Sunday for their jazz brunch. The live music was a nice touch and we had a great time. I got the cold brew martini which was yummy and my boyfriend went with a beer. We both enjoyed our drinks and certainly the cocktail was really fun. You can add CBD to any cocktail for $2 as well. For our entrees, I ordered the shrimp and grits and my boyfriend ordered the club ranch with a side of steak fries. The shrimp and grits was so flavorful and the jumbo shrimp was well seasoned. My boyfriend enjoyed the sandwich and fries and said they were delicious. Overall, a win! We will definitely be back.
label: positive

text:
The place is very nice, I like the vibes.

Started with the baba ganoush, fattouch and arnabeet mekle.
Baba ganoush was good, the pita bread was the highlight lol
Fattouch was fine, enough acid to put the taste of some of the rich dishes.
My favorite was the arnabeet mekle, well seasoned and executed.

The kawarma was good, a little too rich. I wish i saved some of the fattouch to help cut the richness of it. It's a bit heavy, I suggest to pair it with the fattouch.

The prawns weren't mine but they were good, make sure you suck the head! thats were all the flavor is at, eat it with the rice. If you like lemon squeeze it all over the prawns and suck the head lol

Overall it was good, I can say I expected a lot more but it was a pleasant experience still
Service was good, and attentive. They were ready to answer any questions and make suggestions

Come try it for yourself, they're doing something right to have high reviews.

The mezcal cocktail was fire

text: Cafe for locals. A bit of a roadside feel.  Not as fancy as the photos suggest.  In a strip mall with ample parking.  But they have wholesome ingredients and homemade bread. text: I can always count on First Watch. There has never been anything I don't like when we eat here. Their waffle and campo is my favorite and I always get the morning meditation juice. Great option for a morning with girlfriends and also hardy enough for my husband too. They have great seasonal specials. We do tend to stick to breakfast so I don't have experience with their lunch options. There is a bit or an odor when you first walk in, but I believe it's due to the open kitchen style. Very clean and well maintained restaurant. Breakfast tacos are great! So is hash when they have it and lemon ricotta pancakes are rich enough to share. Always get the million dollar bacon if on menu!

text: The inside is definitely small, be ready to sit very close to the table next to you. That being the case, we opted for the patio and it was nice out there. The scramble was delicious, plenty of cheese and almost too much meat (surprisingly). The biscuit had a decent flavor I guess, but was definitely too well cooked and slig burnt so it was all dried out and was missing the fluffiness, no butter to be seen. I added avocado - definitely wouldn't recommend that - instead of adding it to my scramble like I asked, they added it as a whole separate side at a steep $3 and sent out one that was a little over its freshness date for sure; not worth the addition at all. Service was good overall but they seemed short handed, our server was trying hard but we didn't even get her name she was so fast every time she came to us and the manager or someone else was the one who brought out our food. Was good enough to give them another try for sure.

text:
I'm from outside New Orleans, but recently moved to Oakland, CA. I frequently Hey! On a monthly basis, as I love specialty coffee and this shop is very consistent and in a few great locations in the city.

My friend Devin, who is a local roaster in Hammond, LA and I had been talking about Yemeni coffee and trying to get our hands on some. For those unfamiliar, Yemen was one do the first places coffee was grown and cultivated. It's also a hard region to export from, modern-day. I was very pleasantly surprised when this showed up at my door, sent from Devin!

I brewed a cup immediately using my Hario V60, and it was fresh and bright. The jasmine came out for me in the tasting notes, giving it a light and almost tea-like body. 

I've always loved this space to frequent, but this was an extra special surprise!
label: positive

text: I love the food here and eat it all the time but the last 3 times I have been here they can't seem to serve the Pho hot. It comes to the table and is warm, not hot whatsoever. On my most recent trip I told them ahead of time that the broth has not been very hot the last couple times that I have ordered it and asked them to put it in the microwave prior to adding the beef if it wasn't going to be hot. I told them I would rather them do that then bring it out to me and then have to go back and put it in the microwave (like we've had to do on 2 other occasions in the past) because when they do that the beef becomes a very odd dried out texture, not to mention it sticks to itself and kind of ruins the dish. Simple request and a fix if they can't serve it correctly to begin with you'd think. Long story short, they add the beef anyway then put it in the microwave and that's how they bring it to the table. So here we are with dry clumped together microwaved beef... I'll try Bamboozle one more time and if they still haven't figured out how to serve hot soup I'll find some place else to order it.

text: The service deserves 5 stars but my drink was a 3.5 so we'll settle with 4 stars. I could tell they were smiling through their masks. I checked out the menu online before going and it's very extensive. They have tons of latte options. They only offer soy milk as a milk alternative so keep that in mind if you prefer/need an alternative. I tried a 10 oz. honey, cinnamon, vanilla latte. One thing I noticed was the high calorie count on each of their drinks. If you want a low cal drink, stick to coffee. The 10 oz. drink was $5.45, which was steep for the size. text:
Finally! Mochi donuts in STL Thank you Pokedoke for bringing these amazing chewy donuts to us

Mochi donuts were originated from from Japan and is one of the star product at Mister Donut in Taiwan. It was super popular since about at least 10 years ago in Taiwan. I never seen these being sold here in the US until recent years. It's becoming a new hit in cities with more Asian populations. I am so super excited that we can finally get them here in St. Louis! 

If you never tried, they are a hybrid of cake donuts and mochi, with a dense and chewy texture. Usually a sugar/flavored glazed will be applied on one side of it. This week, they have Strawberry and Black Sesame and I love them both!!! Not quite as chewy as I would hope but it definitely meets my standard and every bite is so joyful

They have had Matcha, Taro, Cookies and Cream and powdered sugar flavors before. I think only the Delmar and Kirkwood locations have it right now. And they said you can contacted them with few days notice to customize the flavor you like for your event! I will definitely try to get all the flavors in the future

I asked the waitress behind the bar and she said someone will get to me. I waited for 15 minutes before being seated. After which, the waitress just wasn't in a mood. 

Now for the food, we'll the prices are way too high for the quality. Also, at 7 in the morning how are they out of Guava pastries? More surprising is the menu I received was very short and nothing like the menus online for this location in Airside E Delta. Coffee was decent and the Cuban bread was ok. I was expecting melt in your mouth rich buttery deliciousness but it was average bread with so-so butter one could get at Publix. I watched others who waited for 10 to 15 minutes for a table while two waitresses argued about an order a current diner stating he never received an item but had actually ate it. 

Sadly, the reputation fell short due to the service times, quality & availability, and prices.

text:
**Staple for Boba near USF**

Naga Tea sits in the sphere of USF so expect a lot of students whenever you visit. In terms of studying, this place has a lot of space and a good amount of outlets. In terms of drinks, they have a diverse menu of milk teas and fruit teas. It's not the *best* place I've been to for boba or studying, but it's one of the best in the area (for me). 

DRINKS:
Naga Tea's strengths lie in their relatively unique tea flavors, including lavender, brown sugar, pandan, pudding, and even the use of butterfly pea tea (which I haven't seen in other tea places). They have a lot of dairy options, toppings, and sugar substitutes. Finally, like all boba places, you can adjust the sweetness and ice of your drink. 

In terms of quality though, I like it better than the nearby places like Chewy (which is not bad), TeaStori (which doesn't have a sitting space), Kung Fu Tea (big chain), Quickly (big chain), The Boba House (not really fresh). Of course these comments are subjective and based off of my personal visits, but Naga Tea never misses and remains a stable, solid choice for me in terms of drinks. 

One thing I like is that they only have one size - what other places might call a "Large." This inflates the price a little (I mean not by much, $4-$6 for a large drink with toppings is still pretty good), but the simplicity is attractive.

ATMOSPHERE:
For the most part people are usually socializing or studying. The music isn't too loud and, when I go, has an alt/indie/pop flavor. On weekend evenings it can get pretty rowdy since the socializing to studying ratio increases, but I've visited on a weekday evening and you see more people studying. 

Outlets are around the walls but I like the tall (shared) table which has outlets as PART OF the table. The other tables are very large, so you have a spread out study/work space. The benches are quite hard though, lol. The tables and deco have a dark wood tone with some grass, which gives a simple and natural look to the place. 

OVERALL
My visits to Naga Tea have always been pretty successful, unlike other nearby boba places that feel a little mediocre in comparison. I would recommend visiting if you're on the look-out for a new tea place!
label: positive

text:

Another short delay and the food arrived.  The bowl looked good.  The toast was cold - it had been toasted and then when cool, was slathered with butter.  So it was toast with butter sitting on top of it, not melted in to the bread.  My eggs were not over easy; they were over medium to almost hard and they were almost cold.

text:
Frontier Perk Cafe is something I have wanted to try since it opened. Unfortunately, with their hours being 8a-3p Tuesday through Sunday, it is a bit harder to get there, but I can understand why those hours are needed due to the dedication to the fresh drinks and eats.
Upon walking into the cafe, you are greeted with a warm hello. The cafe itself holds an eclectic but new age feel being in Historic St. Charles. It was clean and had the aroma of fresh coffee being made. The ambiance created due to natural lighting and rustic wood reminded me of being in a peaceful cabin. 

Now, we get to the important part: the food. The front counter had quiche, pastries and meat pies to choose from. What I wanted was no longer on the menu (the lavender latte), but when I asked, they were still able to make it but it was not listed as they are making changes and updating their menu.
I also ordered the bagel and lox that comes with cream cheese, smoked salmon, capers and pickled onion. I ordered a breakfast burrito with potatoes and a cold brew for my husband to go for his lunch later on.
The wait was 10 minutes for everything. Not only did it take no time for my order, everything tasted fresh, the bagel was still warm, and you could tell the pickled onions were made recently because of their crisp and sour punch. The lavender latte was warm, creamy and felt like a hug.
The price point for Frontier Perk is affordable to average, depending on what you get, for the fresh ingredients and dedication to making their food perfect. For 4 items, I was able to only spend $35 (that included a $6 tip).
The only thing I would change about Frontier Perk Cafe is that they were open later so I could enjoy their amazing food any time of the day. 
I will now be recommending this place to anyone I know who is looking for a fantastic and welcoming brunch or lunch spot when visiting the St. Louis/St. Charles area.
label: positive
