text:
I have been avoiding this review for some time because I have mixed feelings. The bottom line: the food is good. It's fresh, prepared well, and I love that it's seasonal based on what's growing in their on-site garden. 

I ordered a seasonal arugula salad with roasted squash. It had dried cranberries, goat cheese, apples, and was packed with flavor. The scallops were melt-in-your-mouth delicious and combined with the seasonal salad was a sufficient meal for me. My husband ordered the tomahawk, which had a very flavorful chimichurri and came with mashed potatoes and broccolini. The steak was cooked to perfection and the sides, and especially the chimichurri, complimented the meat very well. The wine selection was strong and we each ordered a glass that complimented our individual meals well. 

Here's the part where I'm struggling and have mixed feelings... 
The name of the restaurant, the atmosphere, the menu offerings, and the food don't complement or support each other. For me, the name "Rattlesnake Club" elicits an atmosphere that feels like a lodge, that's dark inside, with heavy wooden furniture, old school leather chairs, leather menus, a fireplace... almost like an East Coast membership club. Instead, this restaurant has vast ceilings, cold furnishings, metal and concrete, and feels ultra-modern. The food is delicious, there's no denying that, but the menu was boring. In addition, my husband and I both struggled with our food selections because nothing sounded exciting or particularly unique, despite the seasonal menu. We actually contemplated leaving before we even ordered our food because not only was the menu not exciting and the atmosphere odd, but the prices were steep. I want to be clear that I don't have a problem paying for a nice meal; we actually selected this restaurant because we were celebrating something and wanted a nicer meal and experience to commemorate the occasion. It was the steep prices, combined with the unexciting menu, combined with the very cold/harsh atmosphere, and therefore an experience that wasn't worth the price, which therefore leaves me with very mixed feelings and an "ok" rating. Not bad, just average.
label: neutral

text:
Went to the Woman's Exchange today for the first time in 30 years.
1.  Upon entering noticed the tables were at the most 2 feet apart.  They were trying to truly maximize the dining space they had.
2.  The lady that sat us was a " Volunteer" so she sat you at a table and that I believe is all she verbalized she was allowed to do.  Trust me she made this known to all of the diners.  She DEFINITELY DID NOT take orders, didn't know the soup of the day at all and did not offer to go to the kitchen 10 feet away to ask in the kitchen about soup for anyone.  I do think I saw her clear a table.  She was good at making herself look busy as we were there during lunch rush.  By all means I didn't see her volunteer to do anything more than " VOLUNTEER ".  If you are at a philanthropic organization one would believe you to have a personality that would be wanting to help others when they are very busy. The poor waitress was working her tushy off!!!!
3.  The poor waitress was so busy it was at least 15 minutes before she had a chance to get our order.  The " Volunteer" could have simply got our order and give it to the kitchen but the " Volunteer " made it clear to everyone she was a " VOLUNTEER ".  I'm not quite sure what is considered VOLUNTEERING or not.  This waitress was taking care of the ENTIRE room of customers which was easily 15 tables of customers.  I thought that the restaurant was probably breaking some type of OSHA law by having this poor woman taking care of ALL the tables.  I hope the diners left her nice tips because she was working hard.  It was really sad to watch!
4.  Women behind the counter where to go orders were being taken.  One women was taking orders and the other I think 2 were standing around looking busy.  They should have taken 10 steps back to the restaurant area to learn what busy is.
5.  I will say the epic salad was still very delicious.!  So, the cooks in the back get one star and I will give the poor waitress a star.  As the restaurant is rated as a whole they lost  my 3 stars.    The salad  was served with an awful English muffin that was cold with a butter Pat.  I know the history of the Woman's Exchange and this today was a pathetic visual of helping others in need.  The poor waitress was working without any help from people that I'm sure we're on the "special people board"to help for scholarships happen and assist less fortunate people.  I am really trying to use proper etiquette in this review and hold back on inappropriate language.  I rarely write reviews but I was admonished at what I saw in this place called a restaurant especially one with a philanthropic history at its heart!Chopped Salad
label: negative

text:
It's been a long time since we visited, but Corner 17 is always one of our favorite restaurants. We just visited again Dec 2021 with friends. There was a 20 minute wait for a seat at 7pm. They take your phone number and text you when your table is ready. We waited in our car since there were a lot of people waiting at the front door.

We were seated near the window where the chef is making the noodles by hand. He's pulling them to make longer and longer noodles. He's shaving them into the boiling water to make the shaved noodle dishes. He's making the dumplings right in front of us and folding the perfect little purses in just 3 seconds each. BEST SEAT IN THE HOUSE!

We started with the wontons in chili sauce. I was a little worried it would be crazy spicy, but they were perfect and did not light my tongue on fire. I also grabbed a few chicken skewers since they are juicy and covered in a honey sauce. The chicken satay appetizer comes with 3 skewers.

 You can't visit Corner 17 and not get their noodles. We had the Sichuan Spicy Noodles with bok choy and ground pork in a Sichuan spicy sauce. There's extra chili oil on the table if you need even more spice!

I always order the sesame balls with sweet red bean filling. They come out piping hot and squishy. I could eat the whole plate by myself. These are also good when they are room temperature, so grab an order to go.
label: positive

Solid selection of brunch basics but also plenty of more adventurous options to choose from, I'll definitely keep coming back.

text:
After a long day skiing on 12/31/2021, my kids were tired of Casino's food and they wanted to try Taiwanese food while we're in Reno for skiing, they found this 101 Taiwanese restaurant a few blocks from Eldorado. It's quite a walk in the winter cold, but we got there and had a nice change of taste.
The restaurant doesn't have many parking spaces since it shares with a few restaurants, one way in/out.
The food was excellent and the prices were reasonable.
The kids liked the spicy dumplings (wontons?) although to me were over steamed (cooked), the sauce was excellent, they left the broken wraps for me to try.
I ordered the beef stewed noodles and it was good, although it didn't have much fatty brisket or tendon like other Taiwanese restaurants in CA, the soup and noodles were tasty.
My son and daughter liked the wonton and egg dropped noodles, they ate all with lots of chili oil. My son also liked the pork bun which looked quite delicious.
As any other kids, they must have boba milk tea of some kinds and they also liked them. They said they will prefer coming here again when we go skiing in march. For me, the price is right, the food is good and the service is very attentive, it's a thumb up.
label: positive

text: As I drove down Washington avenue, I saw Blondie's currently located in STL to my left and of course I parked and went inside and it was the perfect way to start my day. It gives you that downtown in the city diner vibes sitting there and the second time I sat in a booth further into the restaurant. There are so many cool sitting spots in the restaurant you'll def have endless options and the staff are so down to earth and super helpful. They made sure my entire order was correct and well cooked and was providing great service it was a really good experience both times. The first photo is pictured directly left as I parked and walked inside as the beginning of the blog begins... & the second photo featured is my lovely meal I had the pleasure of indulging in! I will say this has to be the best banana French toast I ever had it was so good and didn't even need any syrup that's how delish it was and the eggs with cheese oh my gawd lol talk about and to top it off turkey sausage and guys when I say you try syrup on that it will completely change your tastebuds and your life lol. text:
I was there for a planned 2 night complimentary stay on 1/09/22 &1/10/22.  I gave them about 9 hours of play on Sun & again on Mon.  On Monday afternoon, the dealer & then the supervisor threatened me with expulsion from the pai gow poker table because I made some mistakes setting my hand.  I pointed out that the mistakes were mine & I did not ask them to return my lost money, I accepted my losses without any complaint.  But I continued to be threatened that I would be asked to leave the table.  So after about the 5th threat from the dealer, she accomplished her apparent goal, I voluntarily left the table (& the casino), then checked out of the hotel a day early (with a free room).  Having made hundreds of casino visits over the years in several states, I have never before been treated rudely by a dealer or a supervisor.  This was the second rude dealer I have encountered at HR Tampa.  I returned to the table after cashing my chips to point out to the dealer that I was far more polite to her than she was to me.  I said nothing when she made 5 or 6 dealing mistakes over the course of 6 hours, including neglecting to pay a bonus hand for a full house, and for neglecting to deal all 7 hands another time. Perhaps I should have recommended to the supervisor that she be expelled from the table & replaced with a more competent dealer.

My suggestion is rather than spend any money for their hotel (over $500 per night), you would be better off using that money to purchase a plane ticket & hotel room at a Vegas casino.  It will likely be less expensive & you will be in an atmosphere where your patronage is appreciated.  This was far & away the worst casino experience I have ever seen.

If it wasn't for the great service I received in the hotel & the restaurant, my 1 star rating would have been lower.  My rating for the casino is below zero stars; the place owes me 3 stars.

Bottom line, church bingo is a better option.
label: negative

It's a casual mix of people there to relax and laugh and people grinding. Note that they have Jazz nights Friday and Saturday night (so it'll be busier and louder) and they close at 6pm on Sundays, Mondays, and Tuesdays...but I know everyone needs a break so I won't complain. 

They know how to make a great latte and a great old fashioned. Cute, lively atmosphere. And everyone is nice. I'll continue to go often.

text:
So there is a reason why this place has really high reviews. Their teas are really good. To add to this, the staff is so extremely friendly. 

Annie Tea House is kind of in an odd shopping center. They are sandwiched between a car/auto parts shop and a medium/high-end furniture store with a pizza restaurant at the end of the plaza. Also, if you drive too fast and miss one of the entrances you have to make 2 U-turns to get back on track on Highway 60. 

I came here after Yelp-stalking my Yelp friend Karen V's reviews. She kind of knows all the cool places and new places to eat in the area. So follow her...just sayin'. 

Anyway, I think I'm a regular customer now because the boba place I used to go to, Quickly Boba Tea, hasn't been walk-in for quite some time and they also don't have the avocado smoothie anymore. So I decided to try a new place and I'm glad I did. Don't get me wrong, Quickly Boba's teas are also spot on, but they may just be losing me in the tug-o-war to find the greatest boba tea place of all time! In the words of the oldest Sunday comic on the planet, Cathy...Ack!

I have tried the taro smoothie with boba, taro coconut smoothie with boba, avocado smoothie with coconut jellies, matcha smoothie with matcha boba, avocado smoothie with boba, avocado smooth with boba and coconut jellies, warm ginger tea latte, iced ginger tea latte, pina colada smoothie with coconut jellies, passionfruit tea and more. I've also tried some of their hot items for a snack to go with my tea and everything is sooo good. My fav cold drinks are the avocado smoothie and the pina colada smoothie. My fav hot drink is the ginger tea latte. The smoothies are thick and not runny which I like because cold things don't stay cold, solid, or semi-solid long in the Florida heat. I can actually take my time sipping on the smoothies, although I don't since they're so good. Then it's all gone and I'm sad. I do think they keep their boba warmed so they don't get all hard like some places have. So you can actually chew them without breaking your teeth. I also think tapioca is gluten-free but don't quote me on that. 

The place is decorated kind of cute so you can hang out and lounge if you want or bring your computer and do some work. 

One note to the world is that if they are busy busy then sometimes it takes 20-30 minutes for the food to be completed because I think they only have one person making food. 

I kind of wish they had a bit more exposure like being able to be found on Grubhub or Ubereats or something so that people further away can experience their teas and food, but maybe they like it just the way it is. So if you're looking for a cool place to relax and want to try a variety of teas, please check out this small business. I think you'll be pleasantly surprised.
label: positive

label: positive

text:
A Santa Barbara staple! I used to go to the downtown location, but after that one closed, I haven't been to Jeannine's since. Man, have I missed it!

You order at the counter and then find a table either indoors or outdoors. The space isn't too big, so if you're with a large group, it may be difficult to find seating. Staff was super friendly.

If you're a sweet brunch person, the banana Kahlua French toast is a MUST! The challah bread is fluffy and not too eggy as some french toasts can be, and the caramelized, Kahlua glazed bananas on top are sweet goodness. The Norwegian Benedict was nice and light with the smoked salmon, tomatoes, and cucumbers. The breakfast burrito was too heavy for me though. I loved the grilled tortilla, but the fillings alone were quite mushy. The crispy home fries on the side and salsa were much needed to add a bit of crunch and to cut the richness of the cheesy eggs and bacon. Nonetheless, Jeannine's honestly has a special place in my heart, and it's because of that banana Kahlua French toast!
label: positive

text: Small unassuming restaurant. It's easy to pass by and is pretty much a to go only place. That being said it's some of the best Mexican I've had in South Jersey. I got the chili relleno and was not dissapointed.
label: positive

text:
The vibe is unmatched. If you want a casual, yet special dinner, the first floor has such a lively crowd. Theres a tasteful bar and seating surrounding it behind their 'market'. 
For more intimate you can head upstairs where there's more seating with dim lighting which sets the vibe for a romantic setting.
The food is 1.thought out 2. Fresh 3. Delicious; there's no other thing to say but worth it.
label: positive

text:
I've never been to this location, but it's close to work so stopped by for lunch. I got the Pesto Chicken Quinoa bowl which is my regular lunch order at First Watch. Unfortunately it was a huge let down this time. There was one very small piece of kale, 3 roasted tomatoes,  and the tiniest drizzle of pesto sauce ...maybe a teaspoon at most. So sadly it was pretty much just grilled chicken and quinoa. The locations in Brentwood have always been great, so I'll stick to those from
now on.
label: negative

text: I love the food here but shocked that employees are not wearing masks especially during the height of the pandemic!!
label: negative

text: What a most fitting name! Evolve.  Excellent experience.  Presentation, preparation and performance, this spot is "evolved".  Found this gem whilst visiting and on my way to the airport.  Tucked away in a strip mall, but worth every turn.  Clean, cultured and cozy.  If you happen to get a chance to share a moment with the owner, she is a very impressive person from whom it's easy to see where this establishment gets its name.
label: positive

text: Ordered donut, gave me wrong donut and when I went back lady gives me bad attitude. Never going to that place and she just throw at me . This Happened many times , you place and order and they end up giving wrong stuff. Last 3 times I never back. How many times they will continue do this? we pay and still get an attitude from them. Unprofessional.
label: negative

text: Had a wonderful breakfast board for brunch. Everything was of very high quality and tasty. Love the creative menu items. Located across the street from the Belcourt so will be pairing a meal at Anzie Blue with a movie again soon!
label: positive

text: I visited the Juice Ranch for a healthy fresh green mermaid juice. I was surprised to see the cashier not wearing a mask and not friendly. I should have read reviews prior to going. While the juice was good and organic, it just made feel uneasy about their hygiene practices during a pandemic. Overall, a mixed experience. A bit pricey, but for organic cold pressed juice in SB about what you would expect to pay. Not sure if I will return.
label: neutral

text: Please avoid this location if possible. They ignored customers for 10 minutes that wanted to place an order. The line was 4 people long. They did not acknowledge anyone. No "we will be right with you" or "sorry for your wait." NOTHING. I'm not sure who is the manager at this location but they are not training their staff properly. Very unprofessional.
label: negative

text:
I was walking past Three Graces one day, after it had closed, and I stopped to look in the window because it's a beautiful space. The woman behind the counter noticed me and invited me in. She warmly told me to take my time looking around and asked me if I'd like an iced coffee, which I thankfully accepted (it was hot as hades). We then chatted for a bit about the shop -- it turned out that she was the owner. When I reached for my wallet to pay, she very sweetly told me that it was on the house and she was just happy that the shop made me so happy, and that she hoped I'd come back another time. 

Now, I would have returned anyway, because the coffee is damn good and, as I mentioned, the space is gorgeous. But I just wanted to tell that story because it's just a good way for people to be together in this oftentimes difficult world.
label: positive

text:
I've been to the one in Broad Ripple (before Covid) and was at the Mass Ave location for lunch with my coworkers. I ordered the Smoked Salmon Hash and it was delicious. My coworkers didn't try it, but were jealous they didn't order it because visually, it looked delicious as well. Our waiter said it was an excellent choice so I assume it's a crowd favorite (or at least, his). I've tried their juices before and I couldn't remember which one was my favorite so I didn't order one this time, but if you're interested, I believe they have juice flights where you can choose and try 5oz of 4 juices. The interior is pleasing as well, and every staff there is very kind.

Can't wait to be back!
label: positive

text: Absolutely one of the first things to do when first stepping foot in New Orleans. This is a New Orleans staple and historic. Line was fairly long but considering the location and the hype it was it was definitely worth the wait. We ordered 3 beignets with a chicory coffee and a frozen chicory coffee. These beignets were gone within minutes. The crunchiness on the outside and the softness on the inside mixed with tons of that powdered sugar was dessert heaven. Just make sure you don't breath in before you take a bite or else you'll inhale all the powdered sugar. And yes I did that. Learned my lesson on the next bites thereafter. The frozen chicory coffee was absolutely delicious as well. Nice and strong coffee and on the thicker side in terms of being frozen. All paired up wonderfully. Highly highly recommend this place.
label: positive

text:
Ordered through Uber Eats.  Food was 45 minutes late, because the restaurant had trouble with the order.  What arrived was mostly inedible.  Part of the order was wrong.  Avocado was black, bacon was either burnt or raw, eggs were rubbery.  

It's bad enough that you can't ask for a meal without bread (for those of us who must eat gluten free), but what does arrive isn't worth the time and money.
label: negative

text:
Best coffee in SB, hands down. The amount of love that husband-and-wife team Chris and Ashley put into this pop-up coffee spot Fri-Sun in Villa Wine Bar is something special. I used to be a vanilla latte kind of girl (which is delicious here) but Chris' skill at brewing and the quality of the Equator coffee they use has turned me into a non-sweetened cafe au lait sipper. Round 2 is usually their chai, which I can't seem to slowly sip because it's so darn good. 
The warm, local vibe Ash and Chris create in their charming shop I swear the Cheers theme song plays in my head every time I walk in. It's that kind of place where, beyond the stellar quality of everything they make, sometimes you just wanna go where everybody knows your name
label: positive

text: Very cute location! Nearby the patco so it's easy to ride and walk right over. The inside is small and cozy with about 8 tables. Depending on covid regulations at the time. But this is my absolute favorite place to get hot chocolate especially the smores
label: positive

text: This place always has reliably good coffee & food. Love to get the specialty latte and they have a very good savory scone with rotating flavors. Possibly the best bagel breakfast sandwich I've ever had - love that jalapeño bacon! They also have good dairy free and vegetarian options. Right now they are order online and pick up only but they were open for dine in for parts of 2021 do I'm hoping they open back up soon.
label: positive

text: Backatown was really cute, and had great service & coffee, however, I didn't love my dish. The grits were a little gritty and the sauce wasn't as warm as it should have been. It almost felt like it was microwaved. While I wasn't a huge fan of the food, it wasn't bad, and I could see the potential. I won't rule it out in the future, but I won't run back either.
label: neutral

text: Very dissapointing. I've been here before; however, this time it just disn't measure. The beignets were luke warm ans doughy. The powdered surgar was clumpy and failed to provide and even covering.
label: negative

text:
Everyone knows when you come to NOLA you just try this place. Even me being a not huge dessert person, I had to give it a try and see what the fuss was all about.  

Wow was there a line.. And I wanted to sit inside (opposed to taking it to go) because it was rainy!  We waited in long line but the turn around was quick!  I did order their cafe au lait along with the beignets.  That combo is just absolutely delicious. Dip the beignets in the coffee and it's just absolutely delicious!
label: positive

text: I'm not always the biggest boba tea fan, but this place is delicious. The boba itself is one of the best I've had. So far the drinks I've had have all been delicious, I especially enjoyed their matcha milk tea. I will definitely be going back.
label: positive

text: Great variety of ice cream and the final product is really good. Need to try their bread and pastries next!
label: positive

text:
Fantastic location, and the outside dining area is awesome.  The outside tables are not spaced too close together so even in covid times you feel comfortable not being crammed together.  The food menu is limited compared to their regular restaurants if you're expecting that, so just be aware.  In particular, this location does not have paella, particularly their ridiculous Paella "A la Valenciana" that I drive an hour to get at the Ybor location.  It is what it is, this location is intended to be a faster experience, not the multi-hour Ybor type setup.  The mojitos are prepared table-side just like in Ybor, and are as delicious as expected.

This is a really great option if you're staying at the Marriott; short walk, much better food, better prices.  Or, enjoy it before a Lightning game so you aren't eating over priced slop at the arena.
label: positive

text: A wine bar in the middle of Santa Barbara that doesn't list a single white wine from California by the glass: not a good start. Food is super limited, overpriced, and cashier (there really is no wait staff) couldn't even describe the few basics, like, What's on the cheese plate? Our group of three left within half an hour because the noise level was too loud for any conversation, but we were told that it was "music night," and DJ didn't care that we couldn't talk. What arrogance
label: negative

text:
Super fresh and refreshing.
We just finished out first ever cleanse.  Not going to lie, I wasn't sure what to expect but I expected some of the juices to taste dirt. None of them tasted any less than delicious! Our favorites : Fresh Air, Lean Elixir, Fat Burner, and Pink were the winners.  Pink is the most drinkable and refreshing.
label: positive

text: The absolute worst!, started taking my order then stopped, took someone else's order then decided to finally take my order over Again!!  Waited almost 15 minutes, finally got my food but no drink! Another 5 minutes to ask me what I'm waiting for, I told them my drink then they blew me off!!  This was around 10:30 at night!  The absolute worst!!!
label: negative

text: Such a nice final brunch in New Orleans. The location is beautiful and not as crowded as the French quarter. Very nice for walking and exploring local stores for tourists. Our waiter was a super helpful and nice young man. I wish I remembered his name because he was very kind and one of the very few people working at the time. Husband and I ordered fried chicken French toast and the mix and match eggs Benedict. I was talked into having the crawfish Benedict, which wasn't my personal favorite but I'm glad I tried. The cold brew was nice here too. Hard to find coffee I like on my trip but this did the trick. Would definitely come back to try more on the menu next time I come to NO.  My only regret was not having mimosas, but mama had to catch a flight
label: positive

text: The workers are very nice and seem like they are the most productive they can be. I recommend ordering a tea latte. My chai tea latte was perfectly steamed. Only downfall is they don't have small sizes, just medium or large sizes. Also seems like they run out of a lot of menu items.
label: positive

text: Pleasantly surprised by this place. All four of us had great meals and although it was the first time in my life eating out on Xmas Eve, It was fun and would go again. The staff clearly enjoy their jobs, as they were so nice and dressed up for the holiday and even took a group photo while we were there. Awesome to see a place that must treat its employees well enough o keep so many happy, even on a holiday.
label: positive

text: Don't go to this site it's all about just getting your money and you do not get any service from them
label: negative

text:
Although this coffee bean is very small and can get quite busy it is still worth the wait. I would recommend getting an iced hazelnut latte, as recommended by one of the baristas. It was very creamy and full of flavor especially when compared to other coffee shops. 

The two girls that were working were extremely welcoming and gave the shop a positive atmosphere. They were eager to help answer any questions as well as give their personal recommendations.
label: positive

text: Delicious and healthy. The salmon, beef, and rosemary chicken have all been excellent. (I've had each multiple times). Also love the sides (charred broccoli, sweet potato, beets and parsnips, rice with parsley). Everything is so good and healthy, too. I've never eaten there, just had it delivered. It's always packaged great and tastes fresh. I seriously am creating a yelp account just so I can say how much I love the food here!!
label: positive

text: Great service and Italian food. They went out of their way to ensure my party was seated even though we had no reservation
label: positive

text: Bagels are very small. Not a very clean establishment and the sanitary situation is to be desired. All of the girls behind the counter are wearing their hair stringing down and it's all over their shoulders as they're fixing the food. Pretty nasty. Also, their hours are so inconsistent!
label: negative

text:
This beautiful coffee shop provides a getaway from the typical. Welcoming and calming atmosphere with exceptional customer service. Try their delicious Yemeni Chai tea & baked goods. We live in SoCal and we're wishing this was our neighborhood coffee shop. 

Even more remarkable, it's a nonprofit organization that helps people in need with their profits. 

I'm finishing this review in my drafts - we visited in 2021. I still miss that amazing chai.
label: positive

text: Charming and delicious place to get a sweet on a cold day out with the adult family doing various things in the city.  Cupcakes and coffee were yummy. But maybe a little on the small size for the $$.
label: positive

text: You don't come to Tucson for Italian Food. That being said, I was pleasantly surprised to find everything consumed to be beyond expectations. Based on my Wednesday, 8 P.M. experience, I will be taking visiting guests to this taste of Italy in downtown Tucson.
label: positive

text: Line for DT was CRAZY. Go inside only to find the employees standing around chatting with no urgency for their job. Do better. Have higher standards for your employees.
label: negative

text: Went today for first time after seeing a news link about this shop, offering a free coffee and pastry with a free Covid test. What a great community event! The setup was well thought out and luckily it wasn't too colds and the coffee was incredible!! Will definitely return for a latte soon!
label: positive

text: Lunch special was great. Food was delicious and came to my table quickly. Had the shrimp Pad Thai with spring rolls and vegetable clear soup. With a Thai iced coffee.
label: positive

text: If you love Cold Brew, you'll love it here. I drink cold coffee all year long, regardless of whether it's snowing, raining, or even when it's freezing. You select your roast, brew method, and your drink style. I love Sumatran coffee and not a lot of coffee shops have it so when I come here, I usually get the Sumatran, Black or Americano, served Cold or Nitro. My apartment also sometimes has Java House come in a coffee truck from time to time. My friends from Chicago, who also almost aways only drink cold coffee, really loved it here and whenever they are in town, they always stop by.
label: positive

text:
For a few years now, whenever I am in the area, I get a pie from here. Today I noticed that they had shrimp as a topping. I was so amped! I ordered my pie and couldn't wait to get home! I live 30 miles away.

Imagine my utter disgust when I opened my box to find a burned cheese pizza with shrimp laid on top after the pizza was burned! 

If you like GOOD shrimp pizza, go to Newks!
label: negative

text: I visited a while ago and forgot to write a review but easily one of the best Bahn Mi's I've tried. Not as authentic maybe, but way more loaded with veggies and flavorful than others you may find in Philly. I love the twist on the original like side trip to Korea. The boba teas I got were 10/10 too. I would come here again, it's pretty far but so worth the trip! Don't wait just go!
label: positive

text: This shop is super clean and well organized. Great atmosphere for studying due to it being extremely quiet. The iced hazelnut latte lacked the hazelnut flavoring which was disappointing, but still not bad. I asked the cashier for her personal recommendation and she did not disappoint! She recommended an iced coconut/vanilla coffee with oat milk and it was so good! You could really taste the coconut. By far my favorite drink I tasted today. The dirty chai was good but it was very strong and kinda of bitter. Overall it's a cute coffee shop with very nice staff and a cute atmosphere
label: neutral

text: Waiting 15-20 minutes for an order placed 30 minutes ago with amble staff is just pathetic. Personally avoiding this location.
label: negative

text:
A friend wanted to meet for lunch and had never had Thai food so I recommended we go to Thai Recipes for their lunch special. It is in a very unassuming strip mall and you would miss it from the street if you aren't looking. There is ample parking and the space is a big open room with plenty of seats. The ambience is rather dark but has a nice feel and is quiet for the amount of tables they can accommodate.

Once you enter they had us sit in any empty table, and immediately we were served a menu, placemat and silverware. The two ladies working were friendly and answer d questions as well as got our selection quickly.

My friend got the shrimp pad Thai and I had drunken noodles either veggies. The soup and veggie roll were served almost immediately, and hot the spot on a cold day. It was a tad spicy for my friend but not overly so and I think she enjoyed it. I loved the soup and could almost just eat that for lunch!

Her reactions to the pad Thai were amazing as she kept proclaiming over the sauce and the type of noodles and all the aspects. Needless to say she loved it! The portion isn't too large but is just right for lunch. The price is around $9 with water and no add ons of meat etc. it is a great deal for what you get and the quality.

My drunken noodles were really Hod if a bit cold and oily. I have a feeling it is hard to know what all will be needed during a lunch special and I got the tail end of a big pot. It was still delicious I think I would just next time either order that for dinner when it is cooked fresh and for lunch get a more typical pad Thai.

But it was still wonderful and a very nice atmosphere, so highly recommend it for a delicious lunch bay a reasonable price.
label: positive

text:
FINALLY. An authentic boba place that is up to par with the ones I'm used to in California. This place is the best combination of tea quality, menu variety, creative drinks, cute packaging, and beautiful atmosphere! 

I got the mochi matcha. I've been dying to try a boba drink with mochi, which has been so hard to hunt down and are usually in a very few trendy boba shops.  It was a matcha milk tea with cheese foam, red bean, and mochi paste. Note that the mochi aspect of the drink is a liquid paste rather than a chewy ball that you typically think of when you hear mochi. I personally loved it but it may not be for anyone. 

They only had one sweetness setting for this particular drink which I think was perfect. 

The shop was beautifully decorated, had ample seating space, had free Wi-Fi, and is a great space to study. 

Not to mention the bear cup the drinks come in are so cute!! Overall I'm glad I finally found a boba place in Tampa that is as delicious as it is instagram worthy.
label: positive

text: Great place for apres..Avocado chicken is so good very flavorful. Season crinkle fries even better!  The staff which are the owners are so nice and always smiling
label: positive

text: A very unassuming storefront location convenient to the highway with excellent service and good food.  Our food came out quickly and the bathrooms were clean. We tried the breakfast sandwich and Tater tot casserole and both were delicious.
label: positive

text: It doesn't look like much when you walk by it, but when you get your food... you're right. It's nothing spectacular and they were out of a lot of things, like hash browns, spinach, and a few others. The coffee was pretty bad, but the music was the only thing that kept me from leaving. The pancakes were alright, but again, nothing special. The service was pretty slow, and usually the food makes up for it, but not in this case. The only thing that was special about this place was their band, but you can listen to them without eating there. If you do go here, if they are playing, tip the band. They deserve it!
label: negative

text: I had no idea this location was now a Clear Sky! Drove by and had to stop for lunch. Everything was great and will be back.
label: positive

text: Excellent food; personable staff. Wonderful choices in cheerful atmosphere. I enjoy their additional specials and desserts!
label: positive

text: If you want the best bubble tea in STL region, hands down the best! Most customizable and delicious boba teas. We had the brown sugar and strawberry milk with cheese foam. So good!
label: positive

text: I wanted to make a point to come on here and make a positive review! I used the Dunkin app to place my order because I had a gift card. When I got there, there was a small line but nothing crazy. Is was a bit longer of a wait than you expect from fast food but I'm a patient person! Once I got to the window, I realized that there were only two workers doing their best! I had to wait at the window for my food as they were pretty overwhelmed! The employee at the window kept apologizing and was so nice! My food came out super fresh so that was a plus! We should all take a step back during these times, really during any times, and understand everyone and business is struggling in different ways. It's always nice to have a positive experience to make your day (:
label: positive

text: Really enjoyed my cardamom latte, subtle cardamom flavor, delicious coffee. We had a chocolate croissant which was flaky and delish, would have been better warmed up though, and a samosa, which was just good, not as good as the reviews hyped me up for, but it was solid. I LOVED the community fridge in front of the cafe. Super cool idea and execution. Really friendly staff at the covid safe plexiglass-window.
label: positive

text: Such a great location! First of all it's absolutely stunning. I love that each new or redone Starbucks has its own personality and character rather then the same thing store after store! The staff here were incredibly helpful and friendly. I wasn't able to pay by QR Code in App unfortunately for this location as they haven't not received updated equipment.
label: positive

text: Had the matcha maiko special. I liked that it was not too sweet. The dessert consisted of matcha ice cream (nice flavor & creamy), matcha cake (was a little dry), mochi (nice and chewy), corn flakes (added a slight crunch), and jelly (not too sweet).
label: positive

text: Great experience at Middle Child today. We ordered the reuben and the surfer which were both excellent. I ate the second half of my surfer at home later and it was even better after sitting for a couple of hours. Great combo of flavors in the surfer, turkey, mayo, some kind of amazing jam and arugula. Yum!!! I like that they checked our vaccine cards in order for us to eat inside. Staff were super friendly as well.
label: positive

text: Coffee was amazing, and the pizza croissants were really good. I also absolutely loved the selection of diverse children's picture books, and the barista was efficient, helpful and applied the Covid safety precautions firmly but in a friendly way. Absolutely recommend this place highly.
label: positive

text:
My wife and I have eaten in many First Watch restaurants, and I have to say that this one is by far the best. Not only that, it's one of the better breakfast spots we've ever enjoyed while in AZ. The staff is great, the establishment is sparkling clean, and the food is top notch. Even the syrup is served warm, as it should be. Our waitress, Tiffany was great. The place appears to get busy. I suspect because of how good it is, but it was a short wait. The staff appears to work very well together and make things happen. Well done First Watch. 

We had Eggs Benedict & the French Toast
label: positive

text:
I have nothing but good things to say about core life eatery! It is always so good and so fresh. Their aesthetic is so vibrant when you walk into the restaurant. It almost is like a chipotle style way to order. Therefore you can kind of scope out what you want. 

I got a bowl filled with quinoa, arugula, spicy broccoli, siracha sprouts, chicken, corn, and much more. Their jalapeño lime dressing is too die for. Perfect amount of spice and lime flavor. 

I also also get the chicken bone broth to sip on and it's perfection. It's a must when you're feeling under the weather or just want to warm up!

I've also gotten the buffalo chicken tacos and OH MY WORD, insane! I don't have a picture because i literally inhaled it in two seconds. They put it on a pita taco and it has changed my life forever. Dramatic, i know.

Overall, you can definitely find something for everyone here, kids included. Healthy yet so delicious!
label: positive

text:
Been going to this wonderful coffee shop since it opened. I love everything j have ever had. When it comes to the coffee it's always hot and full of flavor.

Personally I love the hand pies they have on the menu. I have tried all of them and my personal favorite is the curried chicken with the cheese steak not far behind.

The owner and all the employees have always been wonderful and I have enjoyed sitting outside with my pie and coffee watching the world go by.
label: positive

text: I love this place. The spicy Buffalo is my favorite. The Mac n cheese is nice and creamy and the tomato soup is REALLY good.
label: positive

text: As most places this location is under staffed. I've used the drive thru several times. Generally the service is fairly speedy. The staff is ALWAYS friendly. I really like that they added the extra drive thru chain to keep the lanes separate and reduce line jumpers. My orders have been nearly perfect. The area is clean and tidy. The food is like any other McDonald's so service is the key to this location.
label: positive

text: Welcome Home Cafe has fantastic tasting food with very welcoming staff. This is a new restaurant to the area and it's five star review is on point. Highly recommend their grits and the lemon turmeric juice.
label: positive

text: my girlfriend and I eat here at least twice a week. One of the only tasty vegan breakfast options in the area. The bacon is cooked to perfection everytime and I have to get at least 2 hashbrowns per order (4 total, girlfriend needs at least 2 as well)
label: positive

text:
Awesome vibe and healthy choices.

The staff did a great job on educating me on their locally sourced ingredients. I got the Bolt Jolt as I wanted something healthy with a bit of a pick me up. 

The flavor and the ingredients did not disappoint. Prepared quickly and I was on my way with the rest of my afternoon. I'd like to return and try one of their Açaí Bowls.
label: positive

text: Adorable little cafe. The cats are so sweet and the atmosphere is perfect for studying or just enjoying the calm. The decor is a cottage core vibe. The staff/ owners are the kindest. Food and drink is delicious too. I would recommend this spot to anyone and it's a must go if you are visiting Reno.
label: positive

text: Pure deliciousness! Found this place via Yelp search (of course!) while running errands and so glad I did. I was delighted to see all of the grab & go vegan selections. Everything I have tried so far has been amazing- the chickpea panang curry, the lentil soup, the cilantro yucca salad, the banana muffin... I hope they bring a location to Brandon. Can't wait to go back!
label: positive

text:
You will wait to eat here. It is worth it. Definitely go online and put your name on the wait list before heading over to save yourself some time. 

When you roll up you might miss the place. The street side is non-descript but walk down the alley and you'll be greeted by a cute backyard like atmosphere. (see photo) 

I think the coolest part is the walk up window to order drinks while you wait! (and even if you put your name on the list ahead of time, you will also wait) 

All of the food was great and I even had a breakfast margarita! I was having a difficult time choosing an Eggs Benedict when I noticed the Peacemaker. You could get one each of two different benedicts, so I couldn't pass that up.

There is a lot of good food in New Orleans, but I can see why this is on everyone's list of must eat at places
label: positive

text: Lots of different stores sometimes the line is always too crazy but it's worth it.My favorite is the Beilers Donut!
label: positive

text:
My body feels like the food I ate BAD. $60 spent on a lunch for (2) and we both only enjoyed very little of our meal. We threw the rest away. A classic case of a restaurant with good photography but crappy food. 
Our cheesesteaks were 0.2/10. 
Wings 4/10. 
Milkshake 4/10. 
Fried Oreos 5/10. 
Fries 6/10. 

Little Man's ended up being a BIG disappointment.
label: negative

text: Incredible food! Best omelette I've honestly ever had! Take out tasted as good as eat in which I think can be a challenge! Adorable spot!
label: positive

text: I was very disappointed in their food, service and atmosphere. I adore the actual BBC in Bisbee. The food is fantastic, the atmosphere is perfect and the service reminds me of an old fashioned saucy server who has been with the business for years and all of the staff are like family.. It's wonderful. The new ones are so cookie cutter which leads to inadequate service, compromised food and a corporate atmosphere. Very sad that this was franchised and it ruined the whole idea.
label: negative

text:
What a cute place! It's very colorful inside and they have a lot of artwork on the walls. Every wall, corner, area seems to have its own 'theme.' The corner table we sat at, the walls and shelves were decorated with pigs! Everything was adorable. As for the food, I went for the lunch combination with a half sandwich and soup. I couldn't decide! Everything sounded so good, but I ended up getting the Spinach Melt and the Tomato Basil soup. I think the soup is their specialty since it's always on the menu, but they also have a soup of the day written on a chalkboard. I went with my coworkers and we all did half sandwich + the same tomato soup. Everyone loved the soup. It was very delicious. My coworker and I both got the Spinach Melt and we both liked it. Another got the Egg Salad and she loved it as well. It's her second time ordering it and she liked it both times so yay for consistency. She said she likes it because you can really taste the dill so if you're not a big fan of dill, keep that in mind! The Spinach Melt seems so easy to make, yet, my coworker and I both knew it would not taste as good at home. 

Overall, it feels very comfort food-y and feels like home (taking the words of my coworker). I'd like to bring my friends next time to try other things on the menu.

text:
Overall, I think Gabi delivered a pretty delicious and satisfying restaurant week meal. We were looking for a cute spot to celebrate a friend's birthday and decided to go with Gabi after looking at the majority of favorable yelp reviews online. I think the food was definitely worth it for the price: their portions were huge and we left with very full stomachs. 
Their appetizers were definitely the highlight of the night: my friend and I both got the escargot--highly recommend!! They were doused in a delicious garlic butter sauce that we couldn't get enough of. My other friend got the french onion soup and she seemed to really enjoy it as well!!. 
Our entrées came out next after a bit of a longer wait: I opted for the bistrot steak while my friends each ordered the mushroom risotto and the roasted chicken. I thought my steak was cooked pretty well, albeit on the saltier side. The peppercorn sauce was delicious but also very salty. I appreciated the huge amount of fries they gave us on the side but they were again extremely salty and I found myself reaching for my glass of water over countless times. 
At the end, the three of us decided to each order a different dessert. This was definitely the biggest disappointment of the night, especially since my chocolate mousse was so sweet to the point it was almost inedible and my friend's pot de creme also came out extremely heavy and nauseatingly sweet. Luckily, my other friend enjoyed her poached pear dessert so that was a plus.
To end on a good note, I really enjoyed the service at Gabi. While the food did take pretty long to come out (around 20-25 minutes for our entrée), our waiters were so attentive and kind and constantly made sure to check up on us throughout the night. All in all, I would definitely recommend this place for a date night or a good meal with friends!
label: positive

text:
[Grade: A]

On my journey north, I initially was thinking of getting Philly Cheesesteak in Philly, right? But oddly enough I was craving korean food and luckily I found this place nearby I-95. Although a bit confusing to get to at first (there's like three very close right turns you can take right before it and the GPS had a hard time showing which one it was), I thought it had that great ambience that I've normally encountered in the many korean restaurants I've been to. I especially enjoyed how they had street food and other types of korean food making shows in the background as I love watching those on my own. 

Although they had a wide selection of authentic korean food, I was craving kalbi specifically so I went with the kalbi box. Personally, I thought it tasted as good as I hoped for and maybe a bit better. The kalbi was perfectly cooked with an excellent char while still being super juicy. The japchae was pretty much what I expected, but nothing super crazy tho. What stood out to me was how good the salad was, especially the dressing. I can't quite put my finger on what exactly what is was, but all I know is that it was delicious. Lastly their banchan had an excellent selection of fresh veggies and kimchi. 

Overall, I really enjoyed my meal, and I'm glad I took the time to stop by on my trip. Great food, ambience, and service! I wish I had a chance to try more, especially their korean fried chicken, but until next time I suppose. I'll be sure to stop by again on my next trip!
label: positive
