text: Typical breakfast, not a large menu to accommodate all the people on the weekends. I would prefer to eat at home it's cheaper. I do appreciate our server being extremely courteous. I'd go back with friends only for the flights of mimosas.
label: neutral

text: This place is always out of everything. No pumpkin, no white chocolate etc then you have to sit in line just to leave because of the drive thru! Terrible experience every time I try this location. Save yourself time and frustration, go to another location.
label: negative

text: Visiting arizona for the week, and this is by far the best restaurant i've been to. The service and food is amazing. The vibes are just so awesome here, good music, good people! Definitely will come back next time I'm in Tucson.
label: positive

text: Waited nearly 30 minutes for an açaí bowl only to be told they were out of açaí. Asked about it 15 minutes in and they said it was being prepared. Late for work and hangry. Not cool.
label: negative

text: It's a Wednesday evening and I can't understand why the place is empty..the Cuban sandwich is delicious! My friend has the beans and salad and it's so fresh and good!  Bread is outstanding!
label: positive

text:
I had high hopes for this place and unfortunately my first experience here did not meet the mark. I got the Regular Milk tea, less sweet. I thought since it was just called "Milk Tea" it would be just a plain black tea. But I think they use earl grey tea which had fruity notes and was not my favorite. In addition, I didn't care for they milk they used. I can't put my finger on exactly what it is but it may be too thick or Have an odd aftertaste? A positive however was that the tea was definitely strong and gave me the caffeine I was looking for. 

I would come again to try their fruit teas which look tasty or perhaps another type of milk tea to get a greater sense of what this place is like.
label: neutral

text: The food is always very good  Lyncoya. This afternoon we had a fairly new server his name is Curtis... He was Attentive and he was very friendly  and funny! which are the qualities of a good server...thanks Curtis! Five stars for you too!
label: positive

text: I love this coffee shop. There lattes are excellent whether you get them plain or like some flavor and their croissant sandwich is my absolute favorite. I get it every time.
label: positive

text: It was our first time and the Karen at the counter was short with us and gave my husband attitude.  We ordered a Java Chip and 2 American Sunrise with bacon. She asked what type of bagel 3 consecutive times to my husband who then replied "Sorry it's our first time here" and she passive aggressively pointed at the menu instead of just telling us what type of different bagels they had. I didn't like that she was apologizing to everyone but us over how busy it was. They put soy instead of almond milk in our coffee. First experience rubbed us the wrong way. Even their customers were rude too. We didn't feel welcomed at all, it felt like a Get Out movie. When they called our order to get picked up, one of their customers was muttering something under his breath about us stealing their order. They ordered an American Sunrise with sausage when we ordered bacon. I was overwhelmed by the weird passive animosity, I took my daughter and waited in the car. My husband said another man watched me walk out and said "Wow". This was just all around very weird. I'm upset I paid money for a hard bagel and wrongly made coffee just to get treated like this for being the only Filipina in a room full of entitled boomers.
label: negative

text: Great place for working as they have a lot of seating upstairs and downstairs and it is pretty quiet. If you come on a Saturday during December, they have drink specials where all drinks any size are only $1.
label: positive

text: Very friendly! Food was good. We had the chicken soup, Diego Rivera sandwich, the hummingbird omelette and the oatmeal. The soup and sandwich were awesome!!! Fruit was fresh and I especially love the beet juice drink!
label: positive

text:
One of our favorite stops when we are in Tucson.  Gelatos are fresh and delicious.  Place is clean.  Staff are friendly. 
I took one star off because staff are not wearing masks.  It's important especially with Covid when they're talking and handling food and being around other people all day.
label: positive

text: This is a great coffee shop in the L&L market with a variety of food.  The Kona acai bowl is fresh, delicious, and unique!  They add spirulina which puts and interesting spin on the taste... that is if they remember to add it.  Make sure your acai has that dark green powder at the bottom!  I also loved the Namaro pour over.
label: positive

text:
Pretty cool coffee shop serving the usual combinations of hot or cold coffee. I got the Kyoto slow drip cold coffee and it was delicious !!! The also have a selection of pastries and sandwiches that looked delectable.

This location is split level with plenty of chairs table and lounge like seating. It is on a quiet-ish street off Walnut and one can easily miss it. Prices are competitive compared to other coffee shops in the neighborhood.

I would definitely recommend.
label: positive

text: This location has steadily gone from bad to worse. When I got there, the drive-thru was 3 cars deep at the oder board even though the drive-thru lane itself was empty. So, I decided to go inside the restaurant; the lobby was empty save for one person waiting for his order.  I placed my order: 2 Quarter-pounders, 3 apple pies, a 6-pcs nugget, 1 large french fries and a Strawberry-Banana smoothie. I knew I was going to wait a few minutes but more than half an hour later and after everyone who'd come in after me had already left. I finally asked about my order. It was hastily put together. I had let the lady know I was missing the smoothie (but not her fault, she just started her shift and was the only person expediting orders). The "manager" was hanging around in the parking lot. After I arrived home with my order, I realized they also missed giving me my nuggets and the food was lukewarm, almost cold. NEVER AGAIN! I'm not wasting my hard-earned money ever again at a McDonald's.
label: negative

text: The first breakfast I've had in Tucson Very nice service and location. The menu is better than the other one closer to downtown.
label: positive

text: Definitely the best coffee place in the area. Lots of flavors and cute outside porch seating, even in winter.
label: positive

text: The meatloaf sandwich was a 10/10 phenomenal. I will be stopping here every time I'm in town just for this sandwich!!! The iced Zebra mocha was a great pick me up as well!
label: positive

text:
The highest quality bakery in Nashville. The best people work there and they have unique fresh pastries everyday. Very thankful they exist. Everything is done with excellence! 

Highly recommend trying their carrot cake.
label: positive

text: I love this place. The food always looks so good and tastes so fresh. I got the veggie burger and added cheddar. It came with a kale slaw. It was pretty darn good. I love that they have very comfortable outdoor seating.
label: positive

text:
So this place is the potential of amazing...but the service is a struggle and their menu is challenging. I don't mind paying for good coffee and treats. I personally think their prices are pretty spot on. 
Menu: it is not descriptive or even list things in a way that makes it easy to order. They are happy to give suggestions, but they are usually so busy. There are people waiting so you don't want to stand there as they explain everything. I don't love a corporate structure like Starbucks, but at least I know what I can order. Everything I've had is amazing, but if you have these amazing drinks...put them on a menu so people can order them. 
Service: yes, people are short...I give grace. But very often they just forget to make your order if it isn't coffee. I order, the coffee gets handed off to the second person to make, but anything else is left for the first person to complete...and they don't. They aren't helping other customers or making other drinks. This last visit, I had to go back twice to ask, waited 15 mins, and finally the person making coffee finally did it.

Again, great food and drinks. Lots of room to improve. I'm hopeful and it is a beautiful place!
label: positive

text: Great place for working on your laptop. Good food & beverages with a relaxing environment.
label: positive

text: honestly Im not sure why this place is a 4.5 star rating when this place has fresh and good food, I love their tacos and quesadillas, I am a food lover with my fiancé and both of us love to explore new food places, this is very authentic, highly recommended, the staff is super friendly
label: positive

text: By far best biscuits and gravy ever. Hell yes. As I listen to my friend vomit post bourbon st. night it's still great.
label: positive

text:
A smoothie is a smoothie for the most part, yes? With that being said, this review has nothing to do with the taste and/or quality of my fruity elixir. It does, however, have everything to do with the charming young lady that works there. I have to believe that she is actually going out of her way to be rude! From the time I had to peer my head around the corner to see if anyone was working there, to the time she literally threw a straw at me. Her commitment to nastiness was unflappable. 
Here's where I may lose a few of you... I found myself thinking about little miss sunshine for the better part of the day. I couldn't help thinking that I just caught her at a bad time. Like maybe she had just been told a beloved cat died or something? So the next day I put on my most chipper attitude and I found myself standing in front of the again abandoned kiosk. After craning my neck into the back room, out came my favorite employee with all the zeal of a wet napkin. We'll, how did it go this day? Let's just say, my over-the-top cheerfulness was met with such disdain it made me long for the viper who made my smoothie the previous day. Yikes! As I walked away I couldn't help thinking how lucky we are that she doesn't work at the ear piercing kiosk.
label: negative

text: Good bagels with a multitude of different fixings. They are always busy, which is a good sign! I am not a coffee lover but my husband is and he has been very happy with his choices. I am looking forward to returning and trying the biscuits and gravy!
label: positive

text:
I work from home and am always on the hunt for a good wfh spot for when I need to get out of the house. This spot was recommended by a friend in the area.

My favorite part of the coffee shop was that they had a lot of good seating options and reliable wifi, which made it a great spot to camp out and get some work done during the day. 

One thing I'd note was that the upstairs section was VERY cold when I went (like I was wearing a full winter coat inside cold). I'm not sure if they just didn't have the heat on or what was going on, but something that did detract from the experience, unfortunately. The upstairs seating area was also closed at 3pm, so that is another note to keep in mind.

The latte was good (a little pricey, but I guess most coffee shops are these days) but didn't stay warm for very long. Id recommend requesting them to steam the milk to be extra hot.

Will be back, maybe on a warmer day next time!
label: positive

text: I had Bariatric sleeve surgery several months ago , so when I do go out to eat and sit down , I only finish a portion of the meal....but we were driving by and  my wife made a comment about this restaurant and I had heard good things so I decided to splurge .......I was disappointed ....... My wife wanted a BASKET of hush puppies ( would have been nice if the option to SKIP the cole slaw and substitute for a hush puppy) But to her disappointment it had pieces of CORN in them....we sent those back....she ordered the fried oyster basket and I ( pampering myself) ordered the BLACKENED MAHI MAHI.....again I was disappointed ...... when the order arrived , rather than a FILLET, I received "strips" .......they were not the thick , tender fillet of perfect white meat mahi, they were STRIPS...( my PERSONAL opinion, is twofold, FIRST ,if I were to go back in the kitchen and ask to watch the meal cooked , there would be a 10lb bag that said SYSCO /US FOODS mahe mahe strips , SECOND , I really wish someone would check because I THOROUGHLY doubt that was actual MAHI MAHI ............."In my personal opinion "    First time there.......LAST time there !
label: negative

text: I've been going to Starbucks for years but this one has become the worst. For the past two and a half months they have messed up my order, various drinks off the menu, 12 times which is atleast a 70$ loss for me. You cannot reach this location by phone and you definitely cannot park in this location legally because of the one way and the drive thru cars that ignore traffic laws just to stay next in line. Poor way to start the day.
label: negative

text:
I wanted to like this place, but I just can't.

I ordered a cocktail, two appetizers, and dessert, which is usually a good meal for me.  The portions were so small I had to stop at Popeyes on the way back to my hotel for a chicken biscuit.

The crabmeat cheesecake was good, but bland.  Texture was more like a crabmeat flan than a cheesecake.  Also had the shrimp remoulade, which had a lot of lettuce and three shrimp.  The Bananas Fosters was good.

As for ambiance, I was alone, they sat me at the empty bar at the far end so everyone was passing right behind me all meal.  The bar was empty, so why not seat me more in the middle?
label: neutral

text: Stopped in on a morning of shopping and so glad we did. The breakfast sandwich is the perfect size for a light breakfast. The star of the show for me was the Indian Chai. It was spicy in the best way. The staff was also helpful and kind.
label: positive

text: Very good Asian eatery. Very popular especially with the nearby Washington University students. Wait staff is attentive. Good food. Noodle soup excellent. They pull/make their own noodles. Also serve boba tea snd Asian drinks. Highly recommend this place.
label: positive

text: This location of playa bowls is great; especially Jamie!  She gave expectional customer service with a smile.  Highly recommend visiting this location.
label: positive

text: I'll standing the best company to work for I work there 20 years ago and I miss it Casper company is the best
label: positive

text:
We ended up in PA for a quick weekend trip & came here for lunch , after looking at places for a quick bite. 
I had the Shops in club sandwich , wow it was delicious the combo of the turkey, bacon & avocado in this tasted soooo good nice toasted ciabatta bread lordddd just put it over the top. 
Hubby had the So Long sal , another delicious sandwich! Salami , prosciutto artichoke spread Very good combo as well ... also grabbed a bag of chips the buttermilk nice addition. 
Cute small place , service was quick good prices the sandwiches were amazing & definitely worth a try.
label: positive

text: LOVE the coffee here!  My favorite is the Ginger coffee iced. It has a little spice to it which at first I thought was weird and now I can't get enough of it.  Its so refreshing and consistent every time I order it!  Last time I forgot to request iced coffee and they made it hot. When she handed it to me and saw my face of question, she knew I had meant to get iced and happily remade it for me!  Love going there for a fancy coffee!
label: positive

text: The treats at this bakery are amazing. Delicious!! I have to try everything. The truffles and cupcakes are great. And moist.
label: positive

text: Went today for the first time and it was a great experience! even when they got a little rush, they were very nice. it was super quiet and chill, truly a great place! I had the full cuban sandwich and it was soo delicious! Full Cuban Sandwich
label: positive

text: The food and atmosphere was bar none.  The customer service was the kind that we used to have when the customer was what was important.  Bring back the old days.  Going back and recommend.  Give it a try if you like good food and service.
label: positive

text:
Todays breakfast was very tasty...
The cool laidback vibe of this place and being dog friendly is why we chose this place. You won't be disappointed as their food again was fantastic and the pancakes I had was the best anywhere all kidding aside....
label: positive

text:
I was heading to breakfast at a totally different location, but then something/someone made me take a look at this place I couldn't even pronounce. And I'm COMPLETELY blown away with what I #found. 

Friends! I don't care where you live! You MUST #GetLost at Ante Meridiem soon. 

Do it! This place has only been open 7 months, so you haven't missed much but don't wait. You'll be so happy you took my words no matter WHAT you order. We're talking REAL/AUTHENTIC Mexican and Latin flavors, which means amazing food aromas and vibrant colors. You dig this all and crave it, right?

I would consider this a destination dine if it wasn't 8 minutes from my house. 

 Uh oh. I'm in trouble! 

#Getlostindiana
label: positive

text: This location is wonderful! The menu is fantastic, and the service is top notch! Jamie helped me with my first purchase, she really went out of her way to assist me! There was just so much to pick from and Jamie was so friendly and helpful!  Great customer service!!!
label: positive

text: Amazing restaurant that is one of our staples for comfort food. Super friendly people and the food is some of the best Thai I've ever had, every time.
label: positive

text: This fun shop had good seating, live music on a Saturday, and great cappuccino. Would have easily given 5 stars if we had been acknowledged by the barista, been asked to wait as he was obviously short staffed or maybe even received a smile. I would go back because I like the name, but the coffee is good too.
label: positive

text:
Went to brunch with a friend recently, we sat on their outside patio. It was a beautiful day & the wall of greenery made us feel like we were somewhere else. 
I got a coffee & my friend got an orange juice. The mug I got was big!

I ordered the lemon ricotta pancakes with a side of bacon. My friend got the eggs Benedict with potatoes. 

The pancakes were so fluffy & the lemon flavors really came out with the berry compote. I didn't even touch the syrup they gave me! 

My friend let me know she always got the eggs Benedict & they never disappoint. I will be returning to try their dirty fries, we saw them come out as we we're leaving. Wow, a large bowl of fries topped with  garlic and other stuff that smelled amazing.
label: positive

text: Visiting Java Ship has become a staple in my routine. The owner is very friendly, the drinks are fantastic, the prices are good, and there are a lot of options. I love the chia seed pudding and americano, but there are many other sweet drinks and there is almost always a speciality drink. I could not recommend this strongly enough!
label: positive

text: I absolutely love this Deli! their breakfast burritos are the best I've had and the salsa has just the right amount of spice. I love that they use Boars head meat for their sandwiches (cut thin) and their dutch crunch rolls are always fresh. I wish they were open on the weekends as we don't have much free time during the week to stop by.
label: positive

text: We ordered a 40 count nugget. A 20 count nugget. And a 10 count. 70 nuggets in total. Only got 40 nuggets. Went through the drive through and was asked to wait in a pick up spot. Waited for over 30 mins. The food was good and they did make sure to provide a shit ton of sauces like we had asked.
label: neutral

text: My food I ordered was cold. My biscuits wasn't fresh and that's the best part though I got it on takeout. I wasn't too pleased and I love going here for breakfast.
label: neutral

text: The coffee is great and the atmosphere is amazing. I had the original breakfast sandwich on a bagel with sausage it was just okay. I feel it needed an aioli sauce on it to give it more flavor. My wife had the BEAL-T on a croissant which she thought was amazing it had cream cheese as a sauce.
label: positive

text:
Great food, great customer service! Only problem is, they post a sign 'no mask, no service' but ONLY if someone complains they give a mask. They still wait on people without masks. The people in front of me had no mask, he was coughing and hacking.... said he doesn't believe in shots.... waited in line while this went on, when his turn after 15 minutes of spreading disease, he laughed and said good luck, I have the disease.... the girl inside told me I couldn't ask them to wear masks, then said I shouldn't return. 

Everyday people are dieing, yet Scoopers does not promote masks, once again proving money is more important than community safety.
label: neutral

text: Staff was really patient as I figured out what I wanted. I've never had Poke bowls before. Everything looked fresh and everything was clean. The portions are large. You definitely get your moneys worth. I ordered a pineapple boba drink as well with my meal. Every sip and every last bite of food was absolutely delicious. I really enjoyed my dining experience here.
label: positive

text: This is my new favorite coffee shop and I'm lucky that it is pretty close to home!  I keep it pretty basic with an Iced coffee with oat milk.  Its soo good. Their service is exceptional and everyone is always SO nice!  Highly recommend checking them out!
label: positive

text: Awesome donuts and coffee! Staff is always friendly , place clean, there may be a line but its so worth the wait!
label: positive

text:
Hey! This is TakeFlightFoodie, check out my Instagram for more pics!


I got the French toast and chicken, and it was good. The French Toast was crisp on the outside and gooey in the middle. Well, the bites that was cut from the middle was. The edges of the bread was just crispy. I didn't except for the French toast bites to be like that but it wasn't bad. What I didn't like that the Sweet brat sauce was on the French toast as well. It was an taste I wasn't prepared for. Overall the place is nice, the food not bad just got to know what you like from there.
label: neutral

text: Absolutely perfect. Everything we ordered was bursting with flavor and tasted fresh. The burrata and tomato dish was wonderful. Tons of fresh tomatoes and cheese in a pesto vinaigrette. The adult French toast was also perfection. Sweet savory with a hint of booze. The burger is fantastic as well. Definitely a new favorite for us. The menu is inventive and I'm sure anything you order will be good!
label: positive

text:
We were a "little"'skeptical" at first, small place order at the counter in a hidden strip mall. Wrong, wrong and wrong. The staff is refreshingly upbeat and helpful. Menu choices were good and this is made from scary j Mexican food.
The chips and guacamole were good (just good) but the main dishes were outstanding. The food was fresh, hot and all natural ingredients. Doesn't come out fast but it comes out right.
You would not be disappointed here and there is a nice outside rear patio as well that I noticed after we were finished inside.
label: positive

text:
We flew in to Indianapolis and on the way to our destination, we decided to stop for a quick breakfast. My husband found this place via Google maps, but he didn't really figure out what it was. We didn't know what to expect. 

We thought it was a cafe with just coffee and pastries, but turns out it was a whole restaurant. It was a Tuesday late morning, so despite seeming very busy, we got right in (there was only 2 of us however). We used the menu using the QR code, but I believe they had regular menus as well. Coffee was self serve and they even have to-go cups. Menu had a good balance of breakfast and lunch items. We both enjoyed out dishes, which came out quickly. 

Parking is limited, there is meter parking in front of the building - it took us a minute to figure the meters out!
label: positive

text: When the mans says this place is KOOKY boy oh boy are they right! This place has everything from ice cream, Mexican, Cuban and even hot dogs! The prices are almost as good as the food and the food is amazing! This place has a great atmosphere and over all pretty great vibes. SHELLS?! Yep they sell them! Oh ya did I mention they have a WHOLE TRAIN SET! Yep that's right a train set! If you don't visit this place while in town then you're not living life! Get out there and go to KOOKY COCONUTS
label: positive

text: It is January 2022 and COVID is obviously still around we have been living through a pandemic for 2 years now. I walk into Old Town Coffee eager to order an Iced Vanilla Latte, and to my shock I see the barista without a mask making all the lattes. In my opinion it is just awful that we are years into the pandemic and somehow the owner of a family owned business can't seem to follow a state mandate. Especially now that it is spreading like wildfire. So unfortunately, I can't leave a review on how good the coffee is because I do not trust a barista who is sneezing and making coffee with no masks. If you are very COVID cautious I do not recommend coming here.
label: negative

text: As soon as I saw this place on Instagram I instantly I had to come check it out. This is another awesome and super creative speakeasy in Saint Pete. When you walk in it looks like an old-school Laundromat with washing machines.  They have a good menu but what called my attention was their café con Leche overnight oats, oh my god was this delicious!!! It was the perfect amount of sweet and perfect texture so I ordered a 2nd one. I love the the music in the other rooms as well and the decor just keeps getting better and better. They also have an entire section outside as well where you can sit down and just enjoy the music and view. I can't wait to come back and try some cocktails and more items from their menu. This spot is a must see if you're in the st Pete area!
label: positive

text: Okay came here yesterday after work and let's just say WOW. So i came in around like 330pm and I understand that the buns here legit sell out fast. So went to see if I was lucky to get any. And I was got a total of 5 bun. 2 hot dog buns, 2 egg custard buns and their most delicious pineapple bun. They were the last 5 so I grabbed all of them. All were so fluffy and flavorful, even after a day. Only true place in Reno to get good dim sum. Will forsure try to go in early next time to get more pineapple buns. But overall a gem the reno area has.
label: positive

text: There was a wait time of 30 minutes, but once you're in the service picks up. The food took a while to come out but based on how busy they are it is expected. The chicken and waffles Benedict was delicious but the potatoes were not fully cooked. It is extremely beautiful with a bar and higher seating area as well as outdoor seating.
label: positive

text: What a great little lunch spot! We dropped by one afternoon and were really pleasantly surprised but the quality of the food. Reasonably priced as well, if you factor in the Santa Barbara "tax". The beet salad was really fresh and flavorful, and don't forget to throw an egg on top of that fried rice! Did I mention they serve beer? Not that you would ever have one during lunchtime...
label: positive

text:
Just a lovely, aesthetically pleasing cafe attached to Willa's. I say cafe instead of coffee shop because they're definitely doing more than just coffee and tea! 

I had their house tonic first and sat outside to do some work, the tonic (like a lemongrass lavender soda) was delicious. I also had a strawberry aperol spritz later and it was equally great. My husband tried a few different beers and said they were good, overall, a nice selection of drinkable beers. 

They have a few outdoor tables, which is where We sat. There were several open seats inside as well and it seemed like a very nice place to read a book or do some work as it was very quiet. 

The staff were all friendly and helpful and someone even delivered my cocktail outside when it was ready, which felt like a nice touch since they aren't really a table-service restaurant. 

I'm excited to go back and try some snacks or possibly breakfast or lunch!
label: positive

text: Great service and food! Felt like a local place even though it's a chain. Was really impressed by the pesto chicken bowl I got! Reasonable price, I would definitely be back! The only areas of improvement are small - turkey sausage seems microwaved / not hot and toast was barely toasted (basically warmed) but it's always a crap shoot and like I said, the meal I had was freaking delicious!
label: positive

text:
Stopped here with my better half for a late breakfast. Watching the menus a bit closer now that we are back in Weight Watchers after a COVID pause.

Close to us and recommended by our daughter, we were in the area and decided to stop by. Plenty of parking and easy to find. Lots of patio tables but a bit too chilly for me. Service was quick.

Staying away from all my favorites, I checked the point counts and decided on the Greek Omelette with spinach, tomatoes and Feta cheese, plus I added jalapeños. As a side I went for Sweet Potato Fries. Oh and unbuttered whole wheat toast (but I only ate 1/2 of a slice so most points were the fries. My wife went for, as usual, a Southwest Omelette, also adding jalapeños. I guess I have influenced her over the past 50 years!

Our food came quickly. My wife raved about her omelette and she had sliced tomatoes as a side. Plus she had a piece of marble toast with strawberry jam, to match my fries. My omelette was really good, my first but definitely not my last. The winner of the meal was definitely the Sweet Potato Fries! Hot, crispy outside and soft inside. And unlike others I've had in past, nice big fries. Ummmm ummm, fantastic! 

Great meal, great service. Definitely a spot to come check out.
label: positive

text: I LOVE LOVE LOVE Dutch bros, ALL locations in Reno are great! Very kind energetic staff! They always get my drink hitting the spot lol! I believed the accidently messed up a drink one time but quickly made up for it no questions asked! They also love dogs!!! Take you fur baby.
label: positive

text: this hispanic girl was so rude. i tried to ask a simple question about my order and her response was "i'm making a drink go to the cashier, she will answer you" then rolled her eyes. Very disappointed with this starbucks
label: negative

text:
I've been on hunt for a great study/work spot to hang out in when I need a change of scenery and I got just that. The atmosphere is so inviting and cozy here. There is plenty of seating so you don't have to worry about popping in for work and not being able to find a seat. The noise level was also perfect. I was a little worried that because this is a bigger coffee shop and it's downtown, it may be loud but it really wasn't. 

I ordered a cafe con leche and a Cuban sandwich and everything tasted delicious, flavorful and fresh. They do make the cafe con leche with condensed milk instead of regular milk which resulted in a foamier coffee. However the Cuban sandwich was made to perfection. I can tell it was fresh because the cheese melts away with each bite and each component of the sandwich was crispy and hot just the way I love it! 

All in all, if you're looking for a chill study or workspace, I'd encourage you to stop by. I can't wait to come back!
label: positive

text: Good service and delicious food I love the latte too because of the thick foam. Would definitely go again.
label: positive

text:
Hubby and I stopped in for dinner this week and we're super happy with our whole experience! The restaurant is small but adorable, it feels like your kitchen table at home and the new owners are incredibly friendly. The restaurant was not busy, so service was very fast and the kitchen was quick with getting our dishes out to us also.

Since this was our first visit we ordered all of our Thai food favorites. Green curry was flavorful and full of veggies, larb salad was citrusy and fresh tasting. The tom kha soup was full of goodies, and the pad Thai was not orange, so you can tell it didn't come out of a jar. All of our dishes were beautifully presented and tasted amazing. We will definitely be back more often!
label: positive

text:
I don't understand all the negative reviews. I've been here at least once a week since their opening and they have been nothing but professional with excellent customer service. I wanted to ensure that was clear before I continue. It is possible the negative reviews were one offs and although situational experience is valid I would like to express awe and gratitude at my experiences at Freshii (all six of them) this year. If I could write six reviews and give each review four stars, I would, if only to get their rating a little higher. 

At this point,  I have tried all salads on their menu and they are all great! My favorites are the cobb and the fresh salad. The ceasar can be a little dry but all you have to do is ask for extra dressing and they will kindly provide more. 

My husband is a fan of their soup and pocket combo. The seasonal butternut squash soup is AMAZING, it is a must try if it's available. 

They have a rewards program if you download their app, get points that add up to free items. 

I believe Freshii is a much needed place in our community, healthy take out. We order door dash (because they are available on door dash and uber eats) all the time and they arrive within 30 minutes. 

I grant them four stars because they are a bit expensive and sometimes the food is lukewarm BUT you can ask them to warm it up for you. 

All in all, I am very happy with this place and have brought my mother AND mother in law to eat here (new year new me ,you know #DietTime). They love it as well!
label: positive

text: Give Craft Kafe a shot just so that you can be blown away by the number of gluten-free options they have available daily.  I had no idea one could do so much...and still maintain the deliciousness.

text:

Because of COVID everything was done in the parking lot (although they are open inside too), but the decor and seating areas look cute and cozy.  The event was thoughtfully set up and ran very smoothly! 

The Spanish latte was so lovely, sweet but not too sweet. I regretfully didn't get a picture of the flakey and sweet pastry as it was *ahem* consumed rather quickly. I can, however, attest that it was delicious and I will be returning soon!

text:
After seeing all the raving reviews on box of Cubans, I had to stop by and see what all the hype was about. I have to be honest, I was a little nervous when I realized it was inside a gas station but any worry I had left as soon as I walked through the door.

The warm welcome I got as I walked up to the counter yet could've fooled me into thinking I was at a 5 star restaurant. Everyone wanted to know if I was new to the area, was this my first time visiting? After paying I realized they probably wanted to know because every other order they took seemed like regular customers.....that's how good box of Cubans is! 

I ordered the classic media noche sandwich and cafe con leche. The cafe con leche was missing sugar but once you add in the sugar, it tastes like genuine Cuban coffee. The sandwich was so flavorful and unique. They made it with Hawaiian bread which offered a hint of sweet to go with the savory mojo pork and ham components mmmmm. I can't wait to be their next regular!

text: If you like great coffee, lattes, and a relaxing environment in which to enjoy it, go to Restoration in downtown Clearwater. text: I wish I could give a higher rating than 5 stars. This place is absolutely amazing. I travel to Nashville often for work and I make it a point to eat here every time I'm in town because my hometown in Knoxville doesn't have a place that comes even close. I have had a lot of pho in my day and this is easily the best pho I've ever had. The richness of the broth is so perfect you really don't need to add anything to it. Most pho places I add a lot of chili oil to offset the lack of flavor in the broth, but not here... this is what great pho is supposed to taste like. Fresh, hearty, beefy, rich, packed with umami... it's just the perfect broth that you can tell is made with the upmost care and attention to detail. I will eat here every time I'm in Nashville because it's just that good. Try their special pho and you won't regret it.

text:
The cutest and coolest mom & kids coffee shop nestled in Barrio Hollywood. 

Ambience: Cute, small outdoor patio for seating. It's surrounded by murals by local artists. There is no indoor seating. 
Service: Super friendly and fast, they usually have a long line if you're not there right when it opens
Parking: Very limited parking, so expect to park on the street. You may end up needing to park a few streets over.

Food/Drinks:
-Burritos: You can build your own. It comes with 2 grilled burritos which are super filling. I personally like their chorizo burrito over their sausage burrito since I find the meet to be more flavorful and spicy. Since it's grilled, the outer layer has a nice crunch to it.
-Tamales: Both the green corn tamales and red chili tamales are full of flavor and taste authentic. Their tamales actually have olives in it which I found to be a variable ingredient here in Arizona.
-Lattes: I prefer their lattes iced over hot since Arizona can be sweltering. Grab one of their more unique flavors (dulce de leche or horchesso), it can be pretty sweet but it's so delicious!

It's a cute, locally owned coffee and breakfast shop that is totally worth supporting!! Make sure you grab one of their punch cards too since you'll likely fall in love with the place!
label: positive

If I had to mark one downfall to this place it would be the parking. If you plan to stop by on a weekend, the restaurant fills up within the first couple hours so make sure you're one of the first guests there so you can score good parking because otherwise you might be SOL.

text:
La creperia cafe is one of those hole-in-the-wall places that people go to. When I talk about this place, there's no big hype, but it's like everyone just knows that they have amazing food. It's one of those stable businesses that is enjoyed by locals and tourists alike.

It is located on a busy-ish strip in Ybor, though not too busy during their operating hours. Their crepes are something to look forward to, and you can't go wrong ordering savory or sweet. Pro tip: if you go with a friend, split a savory crepe then split a dessert crepe. Also, get a black coffee to go with the sweet crepes, and get blown away!

The server that we had the last time we were there was very nice and accommodating, and service was quick, too. This was 1pm on a Friday. We people watched from the corner table, and the ambience was just so peaceful that I wish I could just go there everyday and make it my brunch spot. 

5 stars are well-deserved. Will be back.

text:
Restaurant Week review-

Awesome service on this busy, Wednesday night. When we arrived for our 8:00PM reservation, the host informed us that it will be a 10-15 minute wait. We were able to go upstairs to the bar and enjoy an aperol spritz. Once we got seated, we chose our 4 courses! My husband and I picked different items off the menu, so that we could share - highly recommend! For our first course, we shared the meat and cheese platters. When we combined the two, it was like you had a very fancy, complete charcuterie board. My favorite, to my surprise, was the bologna! For our second course, we chose the arancini and the meatballs. The meatballs were something to write home about... authentic, moist, delicious! The sauce on both were very flavorful. For our third course, we shared the gnocchi and pappardelle. The gnocchi had a square of gelato that gave the gnocchi a slightly sweet, and cold burst to the mushroom sauce. If I had to choose, the pappardelle would be ordered again. And for our last course, gelato! How could you not? Get the stracciatella... you will not be disappointed! Thanks for a great evening.
label: positive

text:
I am still a fan of The Cup and always will be! I went in earlier this week for a birthday cupcake and was pleasantly surprised to see how many cupcake options they had! I knew I wanted to order the Confetti cupcake since that's my favorite! But I was also picking up additional cupcakes to share with friends. The girl behind the counter was helpful and understood when I asked for 2 of the cupcakes to be separate from the box. I was also picking up a GF cupcake for a birthday buddy that I needed to drop off later that day! 

While asking for cupcakes, the girl helping me was following along quickly and put the box together pretty easily. She also helped me get 2 "happy birthday" fondant pieces for the birthday cupcakes and I also picked up a pint of their vanilla buttercream. 

The service was super friendly and fast. Very efficient. After I paid, she offered to put everything in a bag for me which was super helpful. Thanks! 

The cupcakes were all delicious when we did enjoy them. My friends and I split each of the cupcakes into 4 pieces and shared them so that we could have a little cupcake sampler-it's my favorite thing to do! I think there were 2 hits of the night: the buckeye was amazing and so was the German chocolate. These are 2 cupcakes you don't want to miss. Enjoy!

text:
Great place to go if you love desserts, and fine to take friends.  Definitely a great date place.  Prices are reasonable.  I didn't know there are now multiple locations, but I shouldn't be surprised.  The artistry with the desserts is impeccable!  A friend from NYC told me that if there is a place like this, the average dessert would be at least $15-20 easily.  Prices are reasonable for what you get.  This is a good place to go for drinks- tea or coffee or other, or dessert.  I didn't know this place sold baguettes in some places, but I wouldn't recommend this place for baguettes in particular as one reviewer said they are expensive.  I'm sure it is good, but you want to save your stomach here for the drinks or the dessert.

The bourbon coffee I had was good, but it didn't stand out for me.  The milk foam rose tea has a slightly "bitter" taste with the sweet, but it worked!  I love "experimental" things like this that work.  It's still as beautiful as all their other selections.

I also got to sample a tangerine cheesecake dessert.  The Orange on it looked like a real orange from the picture and at quick glance.  It is white chocolate on the outside with a cheesecake on the inside.  Seems like it would've taken a bit to make that.  Wow!

A must-visit if you're in the area.

text:
This is my favorite coffee shop in Nashville. 

I don't daydream about breakfast tacos from anywhere else in this city. When women write about tacos in their dating profiles, this is the glorious tortilla-wrapped joy they're talking about. The tortillas and salsa are both homemade and otherworldly. (My usual taco order is egg and potato.) 

They also get baked goods from local bakers (I need to look up who) and all I'm saying is when they have donuts, you need to do yourself a solid and get *at least* one.

Not sure if it's the shape of the room or what, but even when it's busy in there, it's not terribly loud. 

Tempo is what a local morning hangout should be. The coffee is amazing (try the dirty horchata), the music they is always great to work to, and the staff are thoughtful and sweet.

text:
Can't say enough good things about Alessia's!

They have a wide variety of fresh pastries and desserts to try. From assorted flavors of croissants to rich and delicate desserts with the most beautiful detailing, especially the Candy Bar. That is if you're lucky enough to get there to a full case before they sell out! You also can't go wrong with their salted chocolate chip cookie.

However, if you're not in the mood for a pastry, you can sit down for an incredible brunch. 
Enjoy a delicious croque monsieur or quiche with a creamy smooth latte, or even a mimosa!

And because they're always creating new items, you never have to worry that you've already tried everything
label: positive

text: This place is JUMPIN!!!! Whenever their doors are open for business, you can count on them being SUPER busy.  Their food is that good.  I've had their biscuits, eggs, bacon, salads, ....it's all good.  Not one thing on their menu is bad.  Please go try them!  I tried placing a delivery order but found out they don't do delivery anymore because the delivery services weren't getting the food to the customers in a timely fashion so Martha's was getting a lot of complaints, so they stopped using all the food delivery services.  I don't blame them.  But really, great food here!  Parking is a bit of a nuisance, but if you get there during certain late or super early hrs, you will probably find a parking spot.
label: positive

text: My belly is so happy. We had a lamb, beef and chicken dish and every side option for the vegetables. Everything was amazing. I don't live here but my two friends who were with me do and they said they will definitely be back.

It's hard to find one with the perfect cinnamon to bread ratio. When I asked what the difference was between the cinnamon roll and Granny roll was he happily explained. I said I'd have to come back to try it and he gave me one to go! I'll definitely be back! I have found my cinnamon roll Mecca.

text: We took a day trip to Nola today. We ordered 2 King cakes ahead of time & we're ready when we arrived! (They always have a good system on orders/pick up. We have been here several times. 1 king cake was for a friend. We chose the Blueberry king cake. We love blueberry, so why not? The "cake" (bread) was good. The icing & coloring were as expected. I took 1 photo below of a broken piece of this king cake. I am sorry to say We were both disappointed. They have GREAT bakery goods! The blueberry (more a pie filling type- which was ok) was all on the bottom of the king cake. So basically little "cake" on bottom & the little that was there was mushy from the blueberry filling. We should have went traditional. But nevertheless, it will always be a bakery we go to when visiting Nola!

text:

Unfortunately, the macchiato wasn't great. The beans seemed over-roasted and bitter. The macchiato at Sparrow in Naperville, IL blows this one away. However, service was friendly and pricing was reasonable. Plus, plenty of seating inside and out. So I'd say 3.5 stars overall.

text: I do not know how this place got such. High Yelp review. The restaurant itself is super tiny and cold. It was probably warmer outside. Eating our food wasn't enjoyable just because of how cold it was. I got the brown sugar boba. I don't know if it tasted like Soap or what. It wasn't good. Definitely wasn't sweet. If you want some good brown sugar boba, go to Whale Tea. Also, the boba itself wasn't even that good. They just had it in a container. I got the garlic chicken ramen. Absolutely so flavor. The chicken pieces had no flavor. Once I added Sriracha to it, it tasted way better, but I shouldn't have to add that for it to taste good. This place looks like it should be in the food court in the mall. We know the quality there.

text: I've been trying to make my way here since I first saw the king cake beignet on Instagram. I'm happy this place did not disappoint. It was very busy and we barely found a seat. Please keep in mind there is no wait staff. You have to order at the bar. I had the panini which came with truffle fries. The prosciutto had a strong taste which I personally did not care for but if you like prosciutto I'm sure this sandwich would work you. The truffle fries were delicious. I ate them all and they were cold! The king cake beignet had the traditional beignet dough flavor with a sweet glaze. The filling tasted of cream cheese and walnuts. I tried the queso and was surprised it tasted so freshly made. The flatbread was amazing. I'll get it next time I go. I also want to try the beignet flight. This place would make for a cute date.

text:
I'm a bit obsessed with Starbucks' cold brew as it is better than any coffee you can get anywhere else. All of the drink options are great, but a downfall is that they've gotten rid of their best syrup (Sugar Free Cinnamon Dolce). I also can't justify paying so much for a cup of coffee on the regular, but believe me -- when I get the craving, the cold brew is quite a treat!
I also really enjoy their iced chai latte, which has such great flavor! The chai they use is simply perfect. If you want a lower carb/sugar/calorie option , request a brewed chai with sugar free vanilla and half and half. It's just as good, but a bit healthier for those looking!
Finally; huge shout out to the crew at the greenwood Starbucks off of 31. It's super busy and they all are working quickly, efficiently, and with a smile!
label: positive

text:
Words can't begin to describe how lovely Caphe Roasters is!!
If you're a fan of Vietnamese coffees and creative dishes, this is the place for you! I had an egg custard coffee and I enjoyed the sweet creamy custard mixed with the tart and bold coffee flavor. 
In addition to the amazing coffee and food, you'll fall in love with the warm and homey decor. The team at Caphe Roasters did an amazing job creating such an open and friendly space. 
The staff are also extremely warm and inviting, and they're more than happy to answer any questions you may have! 
I also love that Caphe Roasters partners with 12+, a local educational nonprofit. My heart is so full knowing I can support a local Asian American owned business and an educational organization I support and love.

text:
If you have 35-40 minutes to spare in order to wait for a cup of coffee then this is a nice backup option to your homemade cup of coffee. If you don't have that much time, I would steer clear. I literally, waited 25 minutes for an online coffee order that I never received and when I simply asked where I was in line to receive my coffee I was told it was like "mechanics" and they couldn't possibly determine that. Huh? Not exactly sure what that means. It's real simple to say, "you have five or ten people in front of you. So basically, my order was just sitting there in limbo as they continued to take more orders. I just wanted a cup of coffee and honestly didn't mind waiting a little bit for it. I just didn't have 40 minutes of time to spare for just one cup of coffee. I am a full time parent and owner of three separate businesses. People like me need to prioritize our time effectively. 

However, I told the guy working the counter that I wanted a refund because I didn't want to wait if it was going to be another 25 minutes. I just didn't have that kind of time. Especially, since we all had no idea how long it was going to take. He literally refused my refund and said that I need to have "patience." So not only did he not have my coffee ready after nearly thirty minutes, he refused to refund my money like he was some God and expected his peasant child to stand there and wait as she was told. So if my coffee isn't ready and you don't know when it will be ready, why can't I have my money back? Seems like the next logical step in this hourly servers "god complex" problem. Yes? 

And let's be honest, I think waiting 25 minutes for one cup of coffee is patience. Especially, considering I was merely asking for an eta. Which shouldn't be that hard to calculate. It also required a lot of patience for dealing with his shitty attitude in a calm manner while simultaneously being robbed for my five bucks. 

I know a lot of people want to try and control the actions and behaviors of other people by purposefully withholding something that is rightfully theirs or intentionally trying to punish them because they didn't fall in line or accept a lame ass, lazy response from someone that is expected to be knowledgeable and professional. Unfortunately, it seems narcissistic and misogynistic behavior is becoming more common. With that being said, contrary to what this hourly server may believe, I actually have the right to ask for a refund when the wait time is unusually longer than expected. I was polite in my request. It was the server that became unhinged when I didn't accept his half-assed answer to my question. He was Incredibly rude and he literally stole my five dollars. However, with his lazy attitude and their dysfunctional system, this is no surprise to me. 

They could care less if they lose one or two long term customers now. It's no big deal to them. It's nice that you can now treat people with such disrespect when they were just asking a simple question regarding time management. Terrible business morals. You should get rid of your To- Go option, if you expect people to wait 40 minutes for a cup of coffee. Or at least have the common courtesy to refund their money when they have determined that they don't have that much time to wait for it. 

I also have the screen shot notifications of the time I placed the order, to the time I was notified to pick up my order. If you would like me to provide this in order to prove that my statement is true, that can certainly be arranged.

Your hourly server has a solid disrespect for other people's time and is incredibly unprofessional. Not too mention a criminal for stealing my money and refusing to refund me for a product that I never received. He is not a God and his coffee is not God like quality. I will never step foot in your establishment again and I will never speak fondly of your establishment again.

You leave an open opportunity for people to give you bad reviews when you don't have a proper time management system put in place and you haven't trained your hourly staff members to properly respect your patrons time. I understand you're a lot more popular than you were a few years ago but that's still no excuse. You will garner more negative reviews if you don't address this problem. That's a fact.
label: negative

text: I was in New Jersey for some work and wanted to grab some food local food and lo and behold, Pie Lady Cafe. Flakey, buttery, crunchy, sweet, I was shocked by how good it was. Definitely recommend the butterscotch scone. Also, we had their breakfast burrito and it was packed with sausage and egg and also very tasty. Their sandwich, called the red eye, was very filling and tasty too. I honestly wish I was there a bit longer so I could go back and try their famous pie. If I'm near that location, I would def stop by and at the very least grab a scone.

text: Pretty disappointing. I've been wanting to eat here for several years based on what I thought was a great reputation in town.  I don't get over to the Mesa much but when I saw a coupon in a mailed flyer a couple months ago, I clipped it.  It offered a free entree with purchase of one entree and two drinks. Even with a discount of $12, the bill was $53 for lunch (before tip) -- I can eat a great dinner out for less than this -- so I was expecting a very delicious meal.  Both my companion and I felt the food was vastly overpriced for the quality, flavor and even presentation. The quantity was generous enough but the items we ordered (empanadas and the mushroom burger) were both rather bland.  The "burger" was served on a doughy, barely cooked pita-like bread rather than on an appropriate item to hold a messy burger in place like a brioche bun.  It was completely falling apart after a few bites... which would have been okay to eat with a fork except it was just not flavorful or complex given the list of ingredients. The most interesting item was the ketchup made from beets.  Also the side fries weren't thoroughly cooked except for the smallest ones.  Definitely not a $24 item! The empanadas were somewhat better but again, not a 'wow' and not even a 'hey, really tasty.' Just bland and again, not worth $24.  The half-off discount didn't even cover the $13 cost of a chai tea and lemonade (that was mostly ice of course).  All in all, very disappointing food and therefore completely overpriced.  We took the food to go so I cannot really comment on the service. When I read another lengthy review (12/5/21), I now see that the prices have been jacked and the quality has been wavering. I totally understand the precarious state of the restaurant business right now during covid but I cannot recommend this restaurant at this time.
label: negative

text: We stopped in to get beverages before our Saturday evening church service. After being endlessly disappointed by both national coffee shops in town we wanted to give our dollars to a local place instead. My husband and I tried "energy bombs" which were a balanced, non-jitter inducing refreshing drink. I found my Dragonfruit Lemonade drink to be tasty and the energy and focus it gave me lasted for hours. Our kids had protein shakes which they really enjoyed. They were topped with whipped cream but were tasty with minimal sugar. They also have protein coffee, aloe juices, and even snack bites. The young guy at the counter was very helpful and explained the difference between all the drinks. The shop has a clearly displayed menu with all prices listed and even seasonal rotations. The price may have been a little higher than coffee would have been, but we enjoyed our drinks and were happy to support a local place with an emphasis on health.

text:
5 stars immediately. I've been in the Tampa area for most of my life. I frequented the city quite often in my older teen years and I've been familiar with OE being a semi-occasional meeting spot for my friends but I've actually restrained from eating there for the thought of it being too expensive. Usually I would just buy a book or two, grab a coffee a tea or peruse the storefront or other an activity. But as age was settling in (i'm only 24 so for the older readers please don't shy away from me speaking about my age, quite aware I am but a baby but I digress), It came the time for me to settle down a bit and the morning of our reservation I actually had gotten my civil marriage and when we got there the staff was very well involved in everything. They made an exception to one of their rules and In order to preserve the confidence that I want to keep with them I don't want to divulge what rule it was that was broken. But they made the day feel as special as it was meant to be. 

The atmosphere is what you would expect from OE, it just radiates luxury and even excess but it is undeniably beautiful. The food was exquisite. I posted the Hummus picture and i'm not even a frequent hummus consumer but it was so good we ordered another. Then I got some breakfast tacos and it was fantastic. I paid for alcohol and breakfast/brunch for 9 or so people with drinks and coffee and it was still significantly cheaper than I expected. Please do your favor and come to the restaurant. Even if you're passing through, buy a book, buy a tea or a coffee. Sit and breath and take in the atmosphere. If you're in the area and you have a job interview get your shoes cleaned, they have someone for that too. Just come in.
label: positive

text:

Also the service was great too Max was our server and he was super attentive, knowledgeable about the menu and very efficient. We didn't have to wait long to get help. We were checked on pretty often without feeling rushed.

text:
If you are in the mood of having breakfast, lunch or dinner in a "dinner" Resturant setup this is the place for you!

As soon as you walk in you are great, walked to your table or booth and a waitress introduces her/himself.

Staff members are all very profesional, using their uniforms and name tags at all times.

Been here a couple of times and I have to honestly say I love it! Our last waitress was Autum and she was GREAT! Love her energy, professionalism and her upbeat, lively and positive attitude!

text:
We finally came to Willa's for dinner. Their dinner menu options are relatively few and simple. For entrees you'll find things like steak frites, pork schnitzel, salmon, one pasta dish, and their famous rotisserie chicken which I've not had but heard it's pretty good. 

We started with a few snacks including the focaccia, cheese and jambon de Paris. This was the perfect amount of food to start our meal. We also tried the pork schnitzel, served with greens and a mustard sauce, and the steak frites accompanied by chimichurri. The food was simple but so good, the flavors were really on point. 

Their wine list is very nice, offering lots of old world wine options both by the bottle and glass. Their nonalcoholic drinks are also good, we tried the Willa's tonic and soft sangria, both slightly on the sweet side at first but very refreshing.

To end our meal we had their warm, gooey, delicious chocolate chip cookie and the amaro-gato (soft serve ice cream with a shot of amaro instead of espresso). This latter dessert item was a nice surprise, the vanilla soft serve had a slight cereal taste to it which paired so well with the bitterness of the amaro. 

I absolutely love this restaurant and cannot recommend it enough but I do wish they added a rotating entree and dessert special to their current menu.
label: positive

text: Okay but not amazing. Got the AZ special which tasted decent but was a bit dry for my taste (a little heavy on the bread). The ratio of meat (2-3slices total?):bread/other fillings was a bit low compared to what I'm used to (in nyc, etc.) Would definitely recommend either adding a sauce (or maybe putting in a bit more pate) and/or additional layers of meat since the pork belly and ham are quite thin. This could also be because of the meat in this version - my friend got the beef, which definitely looked like it had more meat in it/tasted a little more balanced. The ube snow that I got looked really cool/interesting but tasted quite odd (would not recommend) - perhaps it's the syrup that they used(?) - it tasted a little medicinal. Everything was super cheap & affordable tho!

text:
I wanted to celebrate my department's achievements over 2021 so looked to Yelp for a cupcake place. Found this gem and based on the reviews I just had to try them. Originally went on a Monday only to be disappointed because they were closed. So we went back on Tuesday. We were the only customers and greeted as soon as we walked in. She asked how we learned about them which I obviously explained that I found her on Yelp and then we got to talking about events and all sorts of Yelp stuff. 

We opted for the 18 count of cupcakes and just had her give us a variety of each. I ended up having 2 for myself-- the hot fudge sundae and the fudge brownie. I love that these aren't huge so it's just enough for you sweet tooth! Both of these were delicious!! I think I enjoyed the hot fudge more. The other thing I love is that they are not overly sweet and the icing is perfect   I'm usually the one that scrapes off icing because it's too sweet however not these. My staff loved them! 

My only drawback here was they didn't take Amex abs she couldn't give an itemized receipt so keep that in mind since my corporate card is Amex and I have to have the itemized receipt to claim for refund. Soo I just paid out of pocket. Will I return....ummm heck yea!!

text:
I was looking for something lite and sweet and came across this establishment. 
I was able to find street parking directly in front of the business.  I don't think finding street parking will be hard as there is typically space.  
We were greeted by a young man at the register who showed us the menu behind him and in the counter.  Everything was so colorful and there were so many options I couldn't choose. Selecting a crepe was the easy part because I had no clue what some things were on the menu for the dessert bowls.  The young man behind the register was very patient with me as I had a few questions about some of the ingredients since I hadn't heard of them before.  Boy, he was so amazing!!! Whatever it was that I ordered was great. It had the mango slushee which was sweet and tasted like mango puréed juice, mangos, strawberries and other fruits (I feel like I tasted grapes that had been chopped up but whatever they were they were sweet and good). There were also small boba balls (not sure of exact name) but everything about this dessert was amazing.
label: positive

text: If there was ever a reason that Yelp should bend the rules and allow for 6 out of 5 stars, this spot would justify it! We stumbled on The Tattooed Pig. It's in a completely unassuming, almost invisible little shopping center, tucked off to the side. But from the moment you see the designs on the food trucks and branding on the storefront, it starts to click that these guys aren't f***ing around. It's a small seating area, but enough for a handful of families to sit cozily. The decor, art on the walls, and photos of what I assume are friends and family tell you that they're passionate about what they do, but also would never be constrained to any box. Sometimes people get too broad and lose focus and make lots of things poorly. But that is NOT The case here. Whether you go with the fried chicken, a specialty burrito, or pulled pork, it's easy to connect the dots and appreciate a consistent commitment to quality, flavor, and presentation. We will be coming back often. Oh...and the servings are very generous, order light or bring a big appetite.
label: positive

text:
My first time experience having rolled icecream was really good. You get to see how they make it behind the glass. From the moment it gets flattened, cut up and rolled with the other ingredients was cool to see when it was arranged in the cup.

You can either have a cup to yourself or share it- be prepared to be filled from the dessert. We chose to have two rolled icecream orders to  walk around the mall, and hold while you enjoy the fresh tastes.

Friendly service, wait patiently, all major credit cards are accepted. Mr. Wish offers drinks and other items on the menu. When we don't get rolled icecream, we have gotten a Mango slush drink or bubble tea to quench our thirst.

Mr. Wish is located in the lower level. If you take the escalator and walk down further, you'll spot the sign. King of Prussia mall is a big mall, go enjoy some icecream!
label: positive

text: I'm so thrilled to discover this wonderful French  pastry!!!! text:
The ice cream du jour downtown was busy but a tad understaffed, with only two people working. The one who served me got my ice cream, left me to wait at the register a bit to serve someone else, and then asked me how it was despite the fact that it was visibly untouched; she was a tad busy. I used to think understaffed businesses just had owners who were being cheap, but in the COVID era who knows.

I might have thought that my flavor choices of chocolate raspberry truffle and mint white chocolate oreo were unique, but I was bemused to read someone else's review where they ordered the same combination. These must be winning flavors. I actually liked the truffle even better than my mint chip but both were abundant in complexity. It was a mild winter day, but I did consider their large selection of specialty hot chocolates as well. They didn't seem to have much in the way of non-dairy frozen desserts, but their beverage selection offered multiple alternative milks so at least there's something if you need. I guess because they're open late downtown they see themselves as a bit of a convenience store as they had Tylenol and a few other random things for sale at the register.

It wasn't quite so good that I would rule out ever trying another dessert in Nashville, but their flavor selection is more than the basics and it is good.
label: positive

text: All Star Bagel is a newer and  welcome addition to the area, conveniently located on Route 206. The in-house made bagels are some of my favorite. Great texture and flavor. They also offer a variety of made-to-order breakfast and deli sandwiches. The store is clean and service is courteous and efficient. text: I don't know how to review this gas station worse than I am about to and I just want to say that I downloaded yelp specifically to warn you all. So the first time I came here I thought maybe it was a fluke. I went to one pump tried to pay by my card and the pump said to see the cashier. So I'm stubborn and just decided to drive around to another pump and use a different card. Same thing happens so I walk in and go to pay and the cashier says yea our pumps never work. So I chalk this up as a random occurrence. Well today my husband went to the same gas station and same thing happens to him so he goes inside says "please put 30 on pump___" goes back out and the station pumps three dollars and then stops so he goes back inside and tells the cashier- "hey I thought I said put thirty on pump ____" and the cashier goes oh yea sorry about that I'll do it now. So husband goes back out to the pump- still not working waits goes back inside and asks the cashier what the deal is. So then the cashier says oh... I thought you said $3... all I'm saying is save yourself some time and annoyance and go either a street up or somewhere else because this gas station will just give you problems... and you always have to go inside to pay. Your girls in a rush sometimes I just want to pump my gas and go.

text:
This was a spot that I wanted to check out because I saw some pictures on Instagram. Provider is located in the same building as Tinker Coffee! There's a private parking lot and I was able to find a spot. 

The coffee shop is not that big but they have adequate seating. I was able to find seating at the bar. I went on a Saturday afternoon so I'm not sure how busy it usually is or what their peak hours are. I was facing the cute "Nice Weather" neon sign. The coffee shop has nice natural lighting. 

I ordered a hot Miel latte (which is seasonal). All of the lattes are $7 (which is on the pricier side in my opinion). 

I enjoyed my latte but it was on the sweeter side. It was also garnished with something - I'm not really sure what.... I'm moderately allergic to nuts and it seemed like maybe my latte might have been garnished with some kind of nut? 

I ended up not finishing the latte because it was a lot (oz wise) and a little too sweet for me. 

Overall, I would definitely come back and try something else! I enjoyed the vibes and ambiance.

text:
I went to Heartbreaker on a recommendation from a friend. I think the name should be wallet-breaker! Don't get me wrong the food was very good but for the money I feel a little filched. The "Breakup Burger" was no more than a double slider plus. I thought that the job that was done with the fries was good! They're very crispy and salted to perfection. I also enjoyed the Chocolate Shake for $6.00! It was 12oz of chocolate heaven!
In total. The patties on the burger were homemade but really the only thing that was different about this burger was the spicy mustard. Fries are fries and the shake was extra chocolatey.

I spent $50.00 for two lunches! In retrospect I think I over tipped. You know how all that's reported in the news is how the wait staff is struggling at restaurants. I thought I would be nice and give a good tip. $10.80 on a bill of $39.20. I would expect to at least be asked how I was enjoying the food, NOTHING. I tipped before I received my food because it's counter service. The staff was more focused on getting out to the picnic table to smoke.

I'm really mixed on how I feel. I feel taken for granted and a$$ fucked at the same time! 

I recommend Heartbreaker just take plenty of money and low expectations with regard to portions and service.
label: positive

text:
I recently came to French Truck Coffee during a trip to New Orleans.  I actually saw this place on the way to get a slice of cake at Bakery Bar down the street and decided I'd bring the cake here and pair it with some coffee.  Upon entry, I liked the place's simple and clean aesthetic.  There were an adequete amount of tables to sit at.  The guy who took my order seemed very nice and friendly as well, which is always a big plus. 

I ended up just getting a small coffee ($2.50).  I think it probably came out to $2.75 after tax.  I think there were 3 types of roasts to choose from: medium, dark, and chicory.  I just got the medium roast but looks like chicory is their specialty so too bad I didn't try that one.  I'm not a coffee expert, but I felt their medium roast was excellent.  It was buttery smooth going down my throat and noticably better than any other chain coffee I've had.  

I think the combination of all things here make it deserving of a 5 star rating.  If I'm ever in New Orleans again I'll drop by to try the chicory.
label: positive

text:
How I Discovered Wow Wow Lemonade Stand
One day I stumbled across a picture of the most beautiful lemonades on Reno's Yelp Instagram account (I highly suggest you follow it, especially if you're a foodie like myself, as they post lots of information on local eats on the daily, especially popular and new ones).

Having full trust in Michael T.'s  (Yelp Community Manager) recommendations, I knew I had to make a trip out to Mae Anne and get my fill for a drink near and dear to my heart.  If you grew up with Hot Dog on a Stick, you'd probably be in the same club.

Decor
I entered the building and immediately was blown away by how much thought and care went into the creation of this charming, warm, and welcoming shop.

Mason jar lights, wood tables, farmhouse chairs, decorative Hawaiian-themed art lining the walls, and a nice and bright professional looking kitchen lined with white subway tiles is what you'll find when you visit the shop.

Discovering Amazing Lemonades
I took a moment to appreciate the decor, then found myself examining an extensive menu of many items, including their popular hand-crafted lemonades that come in so many unique flavors.  Watermelon Basil, Lavender Blackberry, Butterfly Mojito, and Strawberry Mint anyone?
After having one of their flights, or tastings of their drinks, I knew that this shop would be here to stay.  If I could describe their lemonades in one word, gourmet would be it.

Everything was incredibly fresh and flavorful & I have no doubt the other flavors would not dissapoint.  Try all of their flavors to discover your favorites.

Fresh herbs, homemade purees made from fresh, high quality ingredients, and additional ingredients like teas, spirulina, charcoal, and butterfly pea flowers help take the beloved lemonade to another level, a level worth exploring, savoring, and appreciating.

Final Thoughts
If you're looking for some Aloha magic, including friendly service, beautifully crafted, well-balanced, and refreshing drinks made with love, & other items to help nourish your body & soul, you'll definitely find it all here.  I always look forward to my next visit.

I've been to Kauai, one of Hawaii's islands where Jurassic Park was filmed, and cannot forget how amazing it was.  

Epic scenery and the friendliest faces were two of the biggest factors that helped build my love for this island.

Wow Wow Lemonade brings me back to the island and for that, I'm forever thankful to have them here.



NOTES
-They have an app that helps you keep track of reward points (links above).  Save up bigger points for bigger rewards.
-online ordering available on app and on website
-Outdoor (weather permitting) and indoor seating
-Flights (samplers) available for purchase for those wanting to try more than one flavor, but in smaller quantities.  Choose three
-Their drinks are visually appealing and make for a fun experience, one for all ages to enjoy.
-found in Ridgeview Plaza with other stores including Safeway, Petsmart, Kohl's, UPS, & more
-Buy a Mason jar (with lid and straw) and get refills at 10% off each time (My kids love drinking smoothies out of them at home.  Their jars are sturdy and cute.)
-Instagram worthy shots of food and location
-Eco-friendly with sustainable practices
label: positive

text:
Look, I get it. You have brunch fatigue. I have it too. I'm tired of having to pay $45 just to day-drink and eat eggs that are cooked a bit better than I could cook them at home. I understand the feeling of trepidation when someone says "Let's try this new brunch place!" because 8 times out of 10 they will have the same variety of american breakfast that's upcharged due to having a bit of seafood on it and it'll be ok but somehow more expensive than lunch??? 

So I'd get it if you swore off brunch places forever. But before you do... can I recommend you a brunch place? It's called Bearcat. It serves "food coma," "I can't believe I ate so much," "I want this to be my last meal" food. It's the same price as every other brunch place except you won't be annoyed when the bill comes.

Caveat:
It's definitely a pain as they do not take reservations and they're always packed. Today I waited 45mins to be seated at the bar with my two friends which I was not expecting and was a bit disappointed at because I wanted to get a table and be able to look my friends in the eye and hear what they were saying. They really should ask if the bar is ok before seating you there.

All that aside, everything else about my experience was perfect just like last time. The staff was very kind and attentive and the food was incredible. I got the chai pancakes because they are the best pancakes I've ever had and may ever have in my life. I have no idea what you would even have to do to make pancakes this good... get the chai ones!!! The upcharge is worth it!!! I also got the seafood pot pie.... My god.... It was totally full of seafood, not skimpy at all, and the scallops tasted high quality and were cooked to perfection. I'm not sure what the "catch of the day" was that was in there but I scarfed it down. The lobster sauce was delicate, the veggies were perfectly cooked, and the presentation was lovely. My cappuccino was lovely as well. And they serve you your food on the most beautiful custom made plates with hand-thrown pottery cups that have their adorable little cat logo on them...... !!!!!!!!! The attention to detail is so appreciated. I wish I could eat here every day.
label: positive
