text:
I am out of carrots. I am out of sticks. This location has time and again proven that they are utterly incapable of producing the most basic of coffee drinks. I order an "iced coffee with cream, sugar, and a shot of espresso" every time. They repeat the order back correctly to me. Yet somehow they manage to omit the sweetener on an infuriatingly regular basis. 

I do not like the taste of their coffee, which is the whole reason I get an iced coffee with sugar so that it shocks my taste buds and I am blessed to drink a tasteless, sugary caffeinated drink. The lack of sugar makes the drink absolutely horrid and I cannot understand why I keep thinking they'll get it right this time. I keep giving them the benefit of the doubt but not anymore. Not again. 

I am dumbfounded. Bewildered. Utterly aghast at the surgeon like precision in which they dupe me into thinking they did it right this time. It is unfathomable and inexcusable and I will never make the mistake again.
label: negative

text:
I wish you could give two sets of star reviews, but since you cant I will give this a 3.   What I would like to do is give a 1 star and a 5 star.
    
           My 5 star review would be the place, (interior design) and the food.  It is very nice on the inside,I went there for lunch on a weekday because I was treating my elderly neighbor lady, and we wanted to see everything which we did. 
           The food was served hot, fresh, and plated beautifully. My burger was to be cooked med rare, and it was perfect,my neighbor wanted hers med well,and she said it was the best burger she ate.

       The 1 star review is about pricing.  We ordered Gator Bites to start,  then we each had a sportster burger that comes with pub chips, or you can pay for a substitution of fries which we did on each plate. I had a water, she an iced tea. This came out to $49 +change not including tip.
            Seriously?????   I knew it would be a little pricey being a new place, but I can see why it appeared empty. 
                 I am willing to bet a dinner date in the evening while eating off the dinner menu will easily be over $100 not including an alcohol beverage.  Im not knockin this place, just giving my view.  This is a one n done place for me.  
           Oh one thing I did not like was what they do to gouge you a bit more. Burgers come with pub chips for a reason, and thats being people would rather have fries.
           Not only do you pay for a substitutuon, but what they gave you is equivilent to a small portion at any FF restaurant.  We are talking about potatos here. They shouldnt make themselves look bad by being cheap on the fries especially after you over paid for them along with over paying for the burger.  Heck when you go to Five Guys they give you a bunch and thats included.
label: neutral

text:
Atmosphere: Wow, much nicer inside than I expected. The interior is very clean and classy. Plenty of seating and lots of space. Quiet enough to have conversations and this would be a great place to take a group of friends.  

Food: We have been looking for a Ramen place for a while and so far this is the only one in the area that we know of. The Ramen was excellent, the broth was flavorful, the pork cuts were great and the eggs were also good. I am not a fan of soft boiled eggs but their soft boiled eggs were cooked a little more firm which I appreciated. We frequent this place regularly now. 

Service: Great service. You order from the counter and then someone brings out your food. Most people leave there dishes at the table but  some walk them over the trash area where they have spot for you to leave them. 

Overall: Again, not many Ramen spots in the area but this one is certainly worth your time. We are regulars here.
label: positive

text:
Boba tea has been my latest obsession for the last month. As such, I've been making my rounds at various shops offering my new favorite delicacy. I've noticed most places add a TON of sugar and that's saying a lot coming from me as a dessert connoisseur. I've developed the habit of asking for less sugar as a result so today I ordered a medium milk tea with Oreo bits, less ice, less sugar and boba pearls. 

I liked the way they cooked their boba as it was softer than others I've had. On the other hand, the tea taste was very strong and I wasn't used to that. It seems they have a different tea to milk ratio than other places I've tried. 

At any rate, I may go back and try something different, definitely not so bad that I'd write it off. It's also a lot closer to me than other boba spots. 

One last thing I'll add is their menu is very confusing. It would benefit from some simplification.
label: neutral

text:
Super cute place! I thought we had the wrong restaurant walking through the door because you see advertisements for Mexican food BUT once you see the menu, it makes sense. We didn't linger here for very long due to this being an impromptu visit but I highly recommend this place . I ordered the Indy's Biscuit Combo. It's a half order of biscuits and sausage gravy, two eggs how you like them, two sausage links, two pieces of bacon and a serving hash browns. My fiancé ordered the Creamy French Toast. That consisted of French toast stuffed with cream cheese and topped with a vanilla icing, strawberries, blueberries and bananas.. and of course, whipped cream. I loved the options that were available for breakfast ! Especially all the Mexican inspired breakfast food. The staff were very friendly and super efficient. I don't think it was even 10 mins before our food arrived . My dish was delicious and I very rarely clean my plate. Needless to say there were no leftovers to be found lol. My fiancé also enjoyed his meal as well. Mainly because it wasn't overly sweet and the portions were just enough without being overwhelming . Oh! And they have mimosas . Had I known that, the stay might have been a little longer lol . Definitely on the top
of the list for next time. Price wise, it was more than fair . 24 and some change for everything!

text:
I was craving banh mi one night, but one of our favorite spots for some reason didn't offer it for delivery so we decided to try mopho. This review is purely on the food and not service - we got our food via delivery. The prices were a little more than typical viet pho spots. 

I didn't realize mopho was very strong fusion (almost seemed like Indian or some other fusion with Vietnamese) - thought it would still be leaning towards more Vietnamese. The beef pho had a little different flavor than viet style broth and the beef was very thick and chewy. The pho noodles were thicker as well. The pork banh mi had a strong curry flavor and was almost too spicy. I will say the taro bubble tea was delicious. I am not 100% sure we'd eat here again but I have heard that their wings are really good. Mopho just didn't fill our Vietnamese craving like others do.

I started off with a red eye with almond milk. The coffee definitely hit the spot. The only thing was that the coffee had some type of separation with the coffee and the milk
so that was concerning.

text:
Bywater Bakery is one of the places that you absolutely have to visit when it is King Cake season! They are also one of the few Bakeries that do King Cake by the slice in addition to buying an entire, whole king cake. My friends and I were feeling adventurous and we all ordered different slices so that we can try all of the different flavors. In addition to the the sweet flavors, they also have savory flavors as well. Sweet flavors are $6 a slice and savory ones are $8. 

It may sound a bit interesting to have an Artichoke and Spinach King cake, but it was actually pretty decent. The Artichoke and Spinach King Cake was a creamy and had tiny bit of spice to it. We also wanted to try the crawfish, but they were out and I hope there is a chance to try it in the future. 

As for the sweet flavors, my friends and I ordered slices of the praline (the praline flavor is so New Orleans), lemon (it comes with a nice cream on the inside), cinnamon bourbon (which actually is vegan!), cheesecake and the Chantilly.  The Chantilly cake was so perfectly made - it looks heavy, but actually tastes light. I also really enjoyed the Lemon and Praline ones. 

There are a few more flavors I have yet to try, but it was so much fun to try the diverse flavors this way and I recommend it if you get a chance. The bakery had a line when we were there and more and more people continue to come throughout the day, but it continues to move quite quickly and it is definitely is a place you should stop by if given the chance.
label: positive

text:
The last month or two of visits I've notice a bit of a change in the consistency of my drinks. On a positive note, I'm finally getting a jasmine milk tea without the green tea topper. Some days it was green, sometimes it was just the plain ol milk tea you'd expect.  The reason for my update is that I order a large and spend $7 with tip, but what I'm getting is a barely topped off tea with a lot of ice and regulating levels of sweetness. They're shaking their drinks to mix and putting a bunch of ice and showing that it's topped off because the foam is almost to the top but that's a bit shady cause you're not actually getting any tea and I see that there is tea left every time in the shaker. 

$7 dollars for boba in Reno is expensive in my opinion and I'm not even getting organic tea. If you're in the Bay Area with the vast options, this is overpriced even by the standards there. 

I'm hope this gets fixed and I start seeing value to continue my business here and not take it to the other boba shops in town.

text:
This was my first time at any Cafe Patachou location. I had brunch plans with friends and decided to arrive a few minutes early to put our name in (since they don't do reservations) but they were able to seat us right away on a Saturday morning. However, it was still busy and waitstaff was pretty slow to come around to take our orders, get our checks etc. we spent a while there just waiting on our checks. Which is okay, but don't expect to stop in for a quick brunch here. 

I started off with the hot chocolate. I'll be honest, it was the worst hot chocolate I've ever had. Half the cup was whipped cream and the hot coco was basically just lukewarm milk. It wasn't chocolately at all. But then I got the croissant French toast and it was to die for! Super delicious. I've never heard of French toast with a croissant but I think it's my new favorite! 

Overall a pretty good experience at Cafe Patachou.

text:
My friends and I found this place on Tiktoc, and let me tell you, it was worth the trip! We were in town celebrating a birthday and decided to give Green Eggs a try. Upon arrival, we were greeted and sat immediately. We started with a carafe of Peach Bellini, which was great. We ordered The Oreo French Toast, Apple Pie French toast and Pecan French toast, along with Shrimp and Grits. Now, I had to send the shrimp and grits back, because they weren't hot. They also only put five shrimp in the entire dish, and I expected a few more. After sending the shrimp and grits back, I decided to order the Red Velvet pancakes, which took a long time to come because they are made to order. By the time they came, my friends were already finished eating! The Red Velvet Pancakes were hot and huge, but definitely lacked flavor-- like it tasted like some plain batter, that's it! 

Let's get into the French Toast... The French toast was absolutely AMAZING!!! Each one we ordered was packed with flavor! The Oreo was my absolute favorite! It was made with Challah bread, and had Oreo stuffed inside. Talk about a foodgasim!!! If you come here, you MUST try the FRENCH TOAST! It's probably legit the best French toast I've had. 

In addition to the food, the service was great as well. The manager was very nice and understanding and took care of the issues we had. If and when I'm back in the area again, I will definitely be back!

text:
1/14/22

If it wasn't for the two minor issues, this would have been a 5 Star experience.
Can I put 4.9 stars? LOL.

The 2 minor issues: 10 cent credit card charge, but did not take cash.
Outdated menu on window/door

Quality/Quantity -
Wow, I am NOT a tea drinker, but I would drink this!
Great flavors, you can ask and custom probably any type of tea preference.
They offer samples so customers can choose what tea they like best.

Atmosphere -
Student oriented, probably 90% of the customers were college students.
Large indoor space, with some outdoor seating.
Street metered parking - $1 an hour.
There were too many choices on the menu, especially for us, who are not tea drinkers, but the team was very knowledgeable and made the process quick and easy.
They also offer beer and wine - see attached menu.

Service - Very patient with us, as my daughter likes to ask many questions.

Price -
Very affordable, I paid $3.74
1st refill = $1.10
2nd refill = Free
Not sure if I just missed the sign, but customers should know that there is a ten cent charge for using a credit card.
I personally prefer if you just add that to the already reasonable price, as I do not like to feel "nickel and dimed." Additionally, it is your choice to NOT accept cash.

FYI - you have an outdated menu posted on your window/door with the wrong prices, I highly suggest you update and/or just cut off the price part of the menu; see attached picture (the prices are different).

Owner Suggestion - Congrats on your 2 locations.
This location by the University is very popular
Good website for buying tea, but not really applicable to the retail location...?
I like the pop-up for 10% off.
I also like the pop-ups from all of your positive comments from your customers, but it can get annoying after a while...
Also, a great informative story of how you started in Granada, Spain.

Good customer service for your tea program, cut and pasted from website:

"Satisfied or refunded.
We guarantee that our teas will leave our facility in peak condition. If you are unsatisfied with your products we will gladly issue a store credit for your purchase price, excluding original shipping charges, on merchandise returned to us within 30 days of delivery."

text:
Updating my review within the same day, because the Sabrina's UCity team did contact me today to refund me my money, and also offered for me to come in again soon, so they can set things right. Thank you John M for reaching out and staying true to your word.  I can appreciate when a business seeks to resolve issues in a timely manner.

---

Pains me to write this, because I am a lover of Sabrina's through and through. I've been to multiple locations and I generally love their food. Unfortunately, this is more of a customer support issue, and I am not sure who the blame lies with more: Toast, which is the app they use for Online Ordering, or Sabrina's. Since the Toast app literally has no customer service feature and and is the absolute worst, I'm inclined to place the blame there, but I don't really think Sabrina's was helpful.

Long story short, in my haste I ordered breakfast from this location when I wanted to put the order in at the Wynnewood Sabrina's. Seems like something that should be remedied through the Toast app, right? Wrong. There was no way to cancel the order. So I resigned myself to calling this Sabrina's location and having them cancel the order manually. Bright and early I called and was told they couldn't even find the order in the system. So now I'm side-eying Toast. At this point the host remarked that he wasn't even sure how I made an after-hours order. Well I did. And if that's an issue for the restaurant to control, they need to not even offer evening orders for the next day. Anyway, since they couldn't find my order in the system, he stated I should be OK to re-order at Wynnewood. That wasn't really a satisfying answer for me, since my checking account showed a pending charge for the Sabrina's UCity order. Therefore, I was hesitant to be double-charged both at this location and the Wynnewood location. The next host I spoke to allegedly found my order, but said she wasn't sure how to cancel orders. Ugh. So, I gave her some time, and I called back (mind you she said she would call me, but I couldn't trust that). This time I was told my order was finally voided. OK, good.

I order for pick-up at the Wynnewood location. Easy-peasy. Great service and the food was terrific. A day later I find out I was charged for my food at this location! What was the whole confirmation that my order was voided? I was under the impression that I would be refunded. At this point, I'm not even sure what the restaurant is going to do, and I guess I'm just out almost $50. I hardly know what to say, except that Toast is terrible and the Sabrina's employees would do best to learn their online ordering system and how to rectify orders properly.U
label: positive

text:
This might be my new favorite coffee shop in Indy! I'm on a quest to try them all and was super impressed by Kaffeine. Close to the Lockerbie Square historic area and the Bottleworks district, it's located in an excellent part with lots of fun goings-on nearby, like a little oasis. The parking way easy (they have some of their own spots and other spots can be easily found nearby - I have often used the nearby parking when I can't find a spot for other places), and the inside was open and spacious with smaller tables, tall tables and bar seating, and one large 6-person table, avoiding the cramped feeling some coffee shops can bring. It appears to be set up in an old warehouse, which is something I love (I'm all about repurposing and would love to know the history of this shop)! The music wasn't too loud and many of my own favorite songs were playing.

I try to get a simple almond milk latte at each shop to allow best comparison and really enjoyed this. The presentation was beautiful and the coffee itself was high-quality with good roasted flavor. I also noticed that the almond milk they use is unsweetened - major points for me as many shops use sweetened almond milk as default, which is not my personal preference. They also have many fun and iced drink options (menu pictured)! If you're going for fancy pastries or treats, this might not be your place. I typically go for just the coffee and was impressed by the snacks they offered based on personal preferences - lots of sparkling water, some fresh options, and many types of packaged bars that would be suitable for a wide variety of dietary preferences (this can also be hard to find in coffee shops)!

Open from 6 AM-6 PM M-Sa and 8 AM-2 PM Su, their hours are some of the best in Indy, too. Coming from a college campus, I forgot that many coffee shops don't stay open later and are completely closed on Saturdays - this was not the case with Kaffeine. 

Overall this was my favorite shop that I've been to in my time living in Indianapolis. What pushed it to a favorite was bonus points in many areas of personal preference, but I could see just about anyone enjoying this shop. Good job, Kaffeine!
label: positive

text:
It was an hour and a half before the restaurant closed, we came in to order two bowls. They only had enough white rice for one bowl and said they were not making any more rice for the day. Are you really telling me at a pokey restaurant you're not gonna make any more fresh rice, and it's an hour and a half before you close??

We had already paid, so we had decided to use the brown rice instead. That was a big mistake, since brown rice on a good day is usually quite dry. I had the chicken bowl, with brown rice and it was not very good. The mangoes were over ripe which really overpowered the whole dish. text: This with no exaggeration was the worst restaurant experience I have ever had.  From start to finish!  The employees should not be working here and honestly should never be in hospitality.  We arrive at 7pm on 1/15/22 for a party of 8.  They told us it would be 45 minute wait.  We were ok because we knew it was busy because of a huge event at the convention this weekend.  After about an hour we went make to front desk and they said maybe another 10 minutes.  20 minutes later they said their kitchen was backed up and they were all going to take their breaks first...ok?  These girls were the most unprofessional workers ever.  Drinking, swearing and having an 'I don't care attitude'.  Finally it's about 8:30'and they said since their kitchen is backed up and we had kids with us they will not seat us!!!!  Talk about discrimination.  Our kids were starving and we waited over an hour and half!!!!  We would have left but everything else in the area was closing or booked that late.  We finally get the manager and she is the nicest person in the world.  Gets us a table and says will rush.  Server was nice and a sweatheart.  But it doesn't stop here.  10:15 all our food comes in a to go bag from the kitchen manager and said we had to leave because minors not allowed.  Both the server and manager of the location were in shock and they said they were embarrassed.  I just wanted to get the heck out of there.  I have never felt so disrespected in my entire life.  I get that it turns into a bar after 10 but they made my kids and family feel like absolute shit.  Manager was nice but needs to own it.  Her server was great and I still tipped her 25%.  The people at the front desk should not be in the service, hospitality business or anything that is client facing.  They were horrible and honestly the most unprofessional people I've seen.  Horrible and the WORST experience ever!   I hope everyone reads this and make sure they know what happens when you let crappy people be the face of your company!  Our kids ate dinner in a Uber car after waiting for 4 plus hours!  Shame on you!!!
label: negative

text:
I would give this restaurant zero stars if I could. The food was served rotten, cold, and the service was terrible. When we confronted the owner she was unapologetic and rude. We can hear the chef swearing in the kitchen behind us. The service was slow. We had two diabetics in our party and asked for Diet Coke and they brought us regular coke (very dangerous). Half the menu wasn't available. No silver wear until after the food was served. The food was half the portions than usual for the same price. All of the dishes that were supposed to be hot were cold. Service was atrocious. One of the salads was served with rotten lettuce. Little to no dressing on all of the salads. Only one of the terrible dishes was taken off the ticket where I would argue they all should have been.
I don't write reviews, but this was too bad not to write about. My family is Italian and used to love this restaurant as we have been going here for years, but regrettably this was our last time. Kitchen nightmares would have been very apt for this restaurant. #GordonRamsayComeHelp

text:
I stopped in to grab some Pho after reading some great things about this new business, and I am glad I did. I ordered shrimp Pho with some tofu to go, and the bag had what I needed in terms of condiments. Service was friendly and my order was out lightning fast. I felt like the bag was kind of small and thought I'd be disappointed but that was not the case. Everything was packed tightly, and the portions were quite generous.  The broth was still piping hot when I got home and when all of the ingredients were in the bowl it was a pretty big serving. There were 6 large shrimp (two biters) and 8-10 squares of tofu along with plenty of rice noodles. Also included was a packet that contained bean sprouts, cilantro, basil, and jalapeno slices. Everything was fresh. The broth was flavorful and delicious. 

As a celiac, I won't be trying much of anything else, but I will come back for the Pho!
label: positive

text: Very cute breakfast spot! So my BF and I checked out Perenn over the weekend. We got here at about 10, so the line was nearly out the door, and curled through the front area. There's a lot of inside and outside seating, so that's nice! The line moved relatively quickly, and it was interesting to take a look at everything they had for sale. I'd say the inside definitely gives Magnolia vibes, very cute.  The staff was also very friendly and super efficient in getting tables cleaned off and ready for the next patrons. My BF and I got chai tea latte, americano, soft scramble on the croissant, bacon and eggs meal, danish, and the crème brûlée pastry. From my end, the chai tea latte was delish! Unsure if they only have one size, but now I wish I asked for a bigger size. Very flavorful, creamy, and just a hint of something else I can't pinpoint that made it delectable. I'm not a huge breakfast person, but I enjoyed the scramble on the croissant, tasty. The crème Brûlée pastry was pretty good. I'd describe it as a croissant filled with donut custard. It's definitely filled to the brim with custard, so that was a bit much, but it tasted very good. My BF was a little put off by his toast and didn't eat it, I guess it had a smoky flavor to it. Unsure if that's added on purpose or just a byproduct of the grill. We did get a good amount of items, but the total was just over $60. The food was good and drinks were great, but it's a little pricey for my budget, especially for breakfast items. Definitely can't be a regular occurrence. I'll probably be back for a delish chai tea though!

text:
We walked into the cafe in the direction of the counter and as we stood there, there were two people behind the counter - young man and older woman. Neither really spoke to us for few minutes as we awkwardly stood in pretty tight enclosed space. At some point the woman standing there writing in her notepad looked up and asked if we were being helped which we obviously weren't as she and the other guy that could've spoken to us. She asked for our vaccine cards before anything (which isn't a problem until a bit later) and then told us there were counter seats in the back or we can just wait for one of the tables to be cleaned and we can sit there. We opted to wait and we're seated shortly after. While we waited for someone to take our order, about two more crowds walked in and were politely greeted immediately and told them someone would be with them in one minute. Right after, another group walked in and after being greeted, they were advised to have a seat and would be asked to show their cards when someone came to the table.

My partner and I immediately noticed a large difference in customer care between us and the groups following us; I'm not one to say this often but it definitely felt like it was because we're are people of color.
We decided not to eat there and didn't even bother to try their food based on their service. I'm a big fan of small cafe's but this was disrespectful for my own taste - Philly has much better places!
label: negative

text: I visited Lightbox Cafe last night with my best friend on a frigid night with temperatures in the teens. From the moment we walked through the door of the colorful, cozy space, we felt a genuine sense of warmth and welcoming from the owners. We seated ourselves in one of the corner window cubbies, and co-owner Jen came right over to personally welcome us and suggest menu items. I ordered the breakfast scramble, and the roll, scramble, and especially the little crispy potatoes that come on the side were so filling and delicious. My best friend ordered an avocado toast and enjoyed it equally; and we both sipped on warm golden milk kava. I even bought a to-go double apple cider kava as a gift for my neighbor. Lightbox is a plant-based cafe that provides its customers with healthy, filling options; a booze-free environment and alternative to bar culture; and an authentic sense of community. Furthermore, it's owned and operated by two incredible people who are deeply invested in the neighborhood. In this time of pandemic -- when we're all feeling a bit discombobulated and occasionally all alone in this -- Lightbox provides sanctuary from not just the bitter cold; but also from the uncertainty that exists outside of its door.
label: positive

text:
This was such a disappointment and didn't have to be.  Saturday afternoon, about 1 or so (so mostly after the "lunch rush.")  Pull into the parking lot and first thought was it sure smelled good, which I hoped meant good things for a restaurant that was recommended to me.  Seating was quick, restaurant was about 1/2 or better full.
Then I ordered.  Combo plate, ribs and brisket.  With beans and potato salad, and a Dr. Pepper.  Was asked if I wanted mild, medium, or hot BBQ sauce. 
 Never been faced with that choice before (note to self and others:  ask for sauce on side, you'll see why in a minute).  Waitress was pleasant enough.   Waited.  Waited some more.
Meal comes.
The hottest (warmest!) thing on the plate was the beans.  Ribs on top of brisket.  All pretty heavily coated in sauce.  Ribs tasted ok  (although I wouldn't call them championship quality).  But they were cool.  Definitely not hot.  Meat was so cool the sauce had the look of "I've been on here a while."  If you know BBQ you know what I mean.  What I found particularly mystifying was the 4th rib.  You know the end of the rack that is usually thinner than the rest?  And turns into a chunk of slag unless you really know how to cook ribs?  Yeah, they served me that.  A solid chunk of slag (and cool).
The brisket.  Ah, the brisket.  It tasted ok.....but was cooler than the ribs.  I really think if the brisket was warm, and had the sauce on the side, it might have been pretty dang good.  But, as it were, it was bleh.
The potato salad was your typical mass-produced, over mustardized (which is hard for me to say because I like mustard) potato cream like you'd find in a grocery store deli...in other words, bleh.
The beans, as I mentioned earlier, were the warmest thing served to me.  And they too were just ho-hum.  Kind of a weird flavor, actually.
Now I'll admit that I am real particular about how I like my beans and potato salad.  This definitely wasn't it.
You may be asking why I didn't say something to the waitress about the quality of the meal I was served....well, I would have, if I had gotten more than a fly-by "everything ok?" while I was eating.  I would think she would have noticed (and asked) that I didn't eat much when she dropped the check and scooped the plate.  But no.
So, $30 later (including tip) I left, very unsatisfied and still hungry.
Would I give them another chance? Sure, the brisket had potential.  I'd try it again just for that.
Otherwise, not so much.
label: negative

text: FitLife Foods has been a staple of mine for healthy work food ever since I tried it at my job in St. Anthony's six years ago, and they seem to get better and better the more they expand (although they have closed alot of locations). The menu items do come and go, as they switch up their offerings depending on what's in season, but I always manage to find something I like. Some of the offerings they have dont even sound healthy, but shockingly, they are. I'm talking about their meatloaf (which they improved immensely, and I'm a sucker for their cauliflower mash) bbq beef with mac n cheese, and pumpkin cheesecake. That's right. Pumpkin. Cheesecake. So light, creamy, and rich, it actually manages to RIVAL its unhealthy full-fat counterpart. It provides a nice hearty three forkfulls (cause i may be kinda a pig, but not a slob) and finish great with black coffee. My other fave is their bbq beef mac and cheese. Tender, sweet tasting, hearty pulled bbq beef with burnt edges and creamy mac and cheese fills me up and satisfies me. This dish is so good it has me wondering what the point of a cheat day is if my normal work food is just as good if not at times better???!! They also have fresh breakfast entrees that I like to get for my Sunday "morning brunch" even though im not brunching and im going to work, but im not feeling bloated and weighed down. There's a large selection to choose from there. Love their sweet potato pancakes with bacon the most, along with their french toast and banana oatmeal bake (which is rather unique and original!). The new packaging makes the food last forever, which is a big plus, and you still get a discount for bringing clean packages to recycle. What's NOT to love???

text:
With covid and work and life, it has been a moment since I did any international travel. On the bright side, the food scene in Philadelphia is pretty diverse, and Gran Caffe L'Aquila gives a sampling of Italy in Philadelphia. 

An Italian restaurant means Italian food, but Gran Caffe L'Aquila is pretty serious about the Italian theme and diversifies its business to be more than just a restaurant. The dine-in menu has enough of a selection on there, but there is also a weekly city menu on top of it that rotates and showcases a different city every week. If you want to take a slice of Italy home, there is a little Italian market inside the restaurant and online store too. If you are not looking for a full meal, this is a cafe and gelato shop as well. 

The restaurant looks small, but there is plenty of space between the two floors and the outdoor space. I recommend making a reservation, but the bar is open seating so you can always try your luck there if you stop by when it is busy.

The food is pretty good. The torta granchio, crab cake, was delicate and so tasty. The insalta di mare, chilled seafood salad, did not skimp. The panini and pastas hit the spot. I cannot comment on the coffee since I do not drink coffee, but the gelato is great, and the way it is served with the little heart-shaped wafer is so cute.  

Alcohol goes great with a meal. The wine list is super long. There is a good list of cocktails. There is a selection of Italian beers too so you will find more than just Peroni here. Try the Gran Gianduiotto, made in collaboration with Pennsylvania's Levante Brewing Company if you like a stout.

There is a lot going on at Gran Caffe L'Aquila. Whether it is a meal, household items, coffee, gelato, or an alcoholic drink, they have got you covered. I have no idea how authentic this place is since I am not Italian, but it is a nice experience regardless.
label: positive

text:

The five of us were able to play boardgames and comfortably chat with our friends. There was also another table close by that were also playing games! We also had a 3 month old with us and she didn't seem to mind either :P They do not have a baby changing table nor do they have high chairs, so I wouldn't say this establishment is baby friendly, but it is large enough to bring in your stroller or car seat. 

We were not able to enjoy the outdoor seating, but from what we saw, it was cute and intimate! Each seating area is portioned off with plastic and boards finished off with lights! I imagine this would be a cute place to take a date after dinner! Would definitely come back :)

This burrito is going to be my go to moving forward! The burrito was flavorful and the meat was cooked perfectly. I appreciated all the sauces and the cheese sides because I love to layer all of them on after each bite. The rice itself is also so good! 

My husband got the fish taco, shrimp and al pastor and he said all of them were tasty but al pastor was his favorite. 

The chips were also so tasty and made at the restaurant you could tell. The guacamole was so fresh and perfect amount of seasoning. 

All in all this was a great delivery experience and I can't wait to try more things on the menu!

Don't get me wrong, I don't mind taking it out, but if the menu doesn't say that there is blood in the food, others might be a little grossed out. Despite that, the food was delicious; although not as spicy as many bun Bo hues I've had in my area. My bf liked his pho and was pleasantly surprised at the big portion sizes. text:
This place is great! I love the pink and green interior and the stone corner cashier stand. The old tile and tin ceilings are also gorgeous. I believe some elements of the interior are left from the space previously being a jewelry store - how cool! I didn't see any old pictures of the store but if the owners have any it could be a great way to decorate. I love the corner it sits on in Fountain Square, right across from the "you are beautiful" sign.

There is window bar seating, tall tables, wall/booth and shorter tables in the room in the back. Also outdoor options. Unfortunately it was a little crowded when I went (around 1 PM on a Sunday, so no surprise) and I got very lucky to be able to find a seat and a parking space in the back. The back room could potentially benefit from a larger table or more smaller tables in the center, but I can see how this might be intentionally left clear for functional purposes. I did notice that the bathrooms are very clean! I love the flamingo wallpaper.

My almond milk latte was good! It came in a paper disposable cup, which had a cute design, but I'd prefer a "for here" or reusable cup option for sustainability purposes. They sell cute merchandise up front, I'd like to get one of the blue travel mugs next time I go. They also sell pastries, though a fresh option like apple or banana would be nice, too. Struggled deciding between 4 and 5 stars, if I could do 4.5 I would. Pleased with this shop, would go here more for socializing than getting a bunch of work done. Glad I finally got to try!
label: positive

text:

To add an egg to the skillet is quite expensive, but it was enjoyed. I thought the portion on it was small for the price. The empinadas were absolutely adored. Fluffy and tasty. The chicken and waffles was kind of dry and really needed the aeoli sauce that our disappearing waitress forgot. Also. She abandoned us half way through the meal, and another waiter came at the very end, but the initial waitress never said anything. We saw her still walking around, so I'm not sure what happened. text:
This is an awesome shop. They have exotic coffee and bagel sandwiches. They have a small dining area and a lounge area across the hall, with couches and games, that you can drink and eat. I saw many people ordering cold brew flights. They were very popular and looked savory. The shop also offers free wifi like most coffee shops.

I ordered the Rappin' Rashad. It took about 10 to 15 minutes for it to be prepared. It came out hot and fresh. It was a delicious bagel sandwich. I would recommend it.

There is a decent sized parking lot there. The staff and ownership there is very friendly. They offer a discount for police officers, firefighters, and military/veterans. They also give a discount Brownsburg Community School students, teachers and staff.

My only gripe is that the food seems a bit overpriced. I do not have much to compare it to, but it is higher than the few that I do.

This is a nice spot to catch breakfast or lunch. I did enjoy my sandwich and the shop's ambiance and vibe. This place is definitely worth a visit.

We have had missing drinks, missing hash browns, missing icecreams, ive had to keep telling the young girl this and that is missing after the 3rd time she got pissy at me for it... always waiting bayed and they delete your order and they dont give you what they want because they want their monthly bonuses rather happy returning customers.
label: negative

text:
their coffee is great. their gespochio [i can't spell it, or much else] was too. they think people only order cold soup in the summer
I don't want to write more. what makes you think that long is better. good coffee good soup. what else do you want
label: positive

text: My husband is Palestinian and loved the great selection of sweets that reminded him of home. The gentleman working there gave us a free sample of the hazelnut baklava and it was so GOOD. He was very professional and helped us find exactly what we were looking for. Will be going back!
label: positive

text: Fantastic independently owned coffee shop with an engaging staff. Great place to relax, and enjoy a freshly ground cup of espresso or a customized coffee. They make all of their syrups in-house. There's also an area in the back to unwind. Jillian and Thomas are friendly and hospitable. Stop in for one.
label: positive

text:
Came here on a whim the other morning. Location is kind of in the cut  near UT. The staff was warm and greeted us when we walked in. Inside there's sufficient sitting space if you wanted to come here to chill or knock out some work. 

On to the real reason you're here...

My husband ordered a regular hot latte and the egg & bacon breakfast sandwich gave it a 10/10. I didn't try either. As for me, I'm an iced latte/coffee kind of woman thus I ordered the iced caramel latte with oat milk. It was delish, can't complain. I also had the buttered Cuban toast. It was okay as expected. This is a coffee shop, not a Hispanic bakery so ya get what ya get.

All in all the visit was pleasant. Truthfully nothing is calling my name to come back here anytime soon but I do recommend stopping by if you're in the area. Support local
label: positive

text:
One word: FAST. Our food came out fast, faster than I was expecting since we went there on a Friday night and they were busy with takeouts.

Process: you walked in and order by the monitor yourself. Go to the cashier and pay. 

Ambiance: go in a little further and you fill find seating area. Quaint and cozy. 

Taste: i would like to have more flavor in my burger and to me it was a bit blend. Their m2o sauce is just a regular mayo+ketchup and a bit of spicy sauce- nothing out of the ordinary 

Service: great. Nice people making our food.
label: positive

text: I highly recommend this place first of all because I was super well treated over there! Smily people, amazing environment and you can feel God's presence in that place!! Second because it's delicious :)
label: positive

text:
After one or two drinks I quickly became hooked on the coffee here! The drinks are balanced and delicious and, in my opinion, some of the best coffee in the valley. Unfortunately, the drive through is killer. Wait times are usually a little high- which I understand; quality takes time- but I waited probably close to 20 minutes in line this morning. I recognize the challenges of food service, especially on a Sunday morning during the pandemic, but it just wasn't worth it for such a long line- and then when we got to the window, we weren't acknowledged for even a minute or two more. A little disappointed in the service this morning. 

Tl;dr- great coffee, get ready for a wait. And avoid the drive through!
label: neutral

text: We recently went back to work in office and my coworker found this coffee shop near our job site. We needed a pick-me-up on a Monday afternoon. I placed our order online (suggested by my coworker) and it was ready in about 20-25 minutes. When I arrived, there were only 2 customers doing their thing in the small shop. There were about 5-6 tables. The variety of flavors you can add to your latte or iced coffee drinks is vast! I ordered a large, hot latte with lavender honey flavor. It was delicious! Perfect coffee strength & not overly sweet. Great first impression! My coworker ordered a large sugar free vanilla latte and she said it was very good. They also offer ground coffee to brew at home. This is gourmet coffee and although I cannot afford to come here every day, I will be returning on a weekly or bi-weekly basis. I'm so happy to have found a new coffee shop for the new year 2022.
label: positive

text: Fresh fantastic! I'm so glad we don't have to drive all of the way to Carrollwood for organic juices and cleanses.
label: positive

text: Cute coffee shop! I was on a trip to Nashville but needed a place to work one afternoon. It was a Monday so it wasn't crazy busy and it's so cute in here! The staff was super friendly and the coffee was great. Highly recommend for your morning cup of coffee :)
label: positive

text: Wow, we stumbled upon this gem on an overnight adventure in Tampa this weekend. Searched 'coffee nearby' and this place pulled up. We didn't realized it was housed within a little piece of Tampa history called Pickford's Counter or Pickford's Sundries, where I ended up blowing way too much money on gifts for all sorts of occasions. The espresso was delicious and we ended up buying a bag to take home. They had a small and delicious breakfast menu. What really stood out, through, was the hospitality. There were 3 people working there, all who were calm, kind, and authentic in a way that's hard to find these days. They took COVID precautions by wearing masks and keeping the front door open for extra ventilation. We can't wait to return next time we're in the area.
label: positive

text:
One of my go to favs in the city.  Definitely my Chinatown favorite.

But don't go here expecting to eat traditional American Style Chinese food. It's all about the noodle soups (I recommend the House Special noodle soup or the Roast Duck wonton noodle) and the duck  and pork dishes.

Personally I suggest the duck/pork and green vegetable and rice dish over the more expensive Peking Duck meal.  Also, try the house Special pan fried noodle dish.

This place is always busy from 11am until 11pm.  But the turnover is quick and usually there is not a long wait.
label: positive

text: Worst McDonald's ever. Every time we get stuff from here it's either messed up or they forget stuff. This trip they almost didn't even give us all our food. My husband had to tell them they forgot our food. Didn't even give the toys they're supposed to be giving. We got Pokémon stuff. We were expecting Sing 2 or Encanto toys.  My daughter's cheeseburger in her happy meal had onions on it. Never once in all the times we've gotten a cheeseburger happy meal has there been onions on the burgers. And they didn't even give us her juice with her happy meal. Seriously if I could give 0 stars I would!!
label: negative

text: Cute restaurant. The decor is worth the visit. Food was sub par. Ordered normal breakfast food. Nothing special. Service was a little slow but it was due to lack of employees. Only gave 4 stars since the food wasnt great.
label: positive

text: Buenavida has a short and simple menu. I had a veggie burrito and my partner had a chicken burrito, both very good. We got beans and rice on the side instead of the chips. It's a nice environment, I'll be back :)!
label: positive

text:
Okay - seriously. Do they hate me? I love their food. So... as mentioned before, I keep going... everyone is typically nice. BUT it always takes forever to get my food! I'm talking like an hour or more! I went tonight and was told "10 more minutes" 3 different times. ...it was for a Greek salad and hummus. 

Listen. I love you guys. I visit y'all a lot. Your food is absolutely delicious.  Plz tell me what I did to piss you off. I just wanna eat good food.
label: positive

text: We've been going to Kooky Coconut's for ice-cream for forever. I had read that the Cuban sandwiches were delicious so we just had to try. They did not disappoint! They were one of the best Cuban sandwiches we have ever had! We are from S. Florida where we ate a lot of cuban food and honestly- this sandwich tops them all! We always have to bring company to Kooky's when they are in town! Can't brag about the sandwich and not have everyone try one! So far...all guests have rated them a 10 out of 10!!!
label: positive

text:
All I got to say is them Collins sisters be slinging some tasty brews out the machine. Really gunna miss Jackson though. 
But this girl Meg been pumping out some sus drinks...disappointing. Tough..
label: positive

text: Nom Wah is a chinatown hidden gem! Best soup dumplings in philly! Scallion pancake is to die for, it's crispy and flavorful. Generals chicken is crispy and not over battered! I would skip the chicken and cabbage dumpling as their doughy/ flavorless. I would definitely recommend Nom Wah!
label: positive

text: Great food and drinks with a great service! We had a great time at Napoleon house, I'll go again in my next trip to New Orleans
label: positive

text:
They got taken over by new management and the food is SO bad now :( completely flavorless. The Tofu Satay used to be so amazing and now it's soggy unseasoned tofu:( the gai chicken is the same so little flavor:(  
Literally want my $25 dollars back. Before I would've spent that on the amazing food but this is BAD
label: negative

text:
Corner 17 on Delmar in the Loop has hand pulled noodles, soup, stir fry, ride dishes, home made bao and dumplings, to bubble tea and desserts. The large menu has enough variety from vegetarian friendly dishes to seafood, duck, pork, chicken- it's hard to make a decision. ( Try the 3 cup chicken! :p ) 

The restaurant is large for the loop. It is split, so it is take-out friendly and plentiful for dine-in. If you dine-in they have a large window into the "behind the scenes" how they hand pull the noodles or hand craft their other menu items. Btw they do not hold back on the portion size for noodles.. I always share my soup orders with someone. 

I could imagine returning alone or with a large group of friends, this restaurant is good for either occasion. (:
label: positive

text: Ordered a chicken satay and it is not the same as it was with the previous owners. The chicken is so bland and very chewy and not the def at all as it was before. And the portions of the chicken are very small and they give skinny little pieces of chicken. That's was not at all what they gave us. The peanut sauce was also extremely bland and watered down. 100% never coming again ever with this new ownership. A disappointment to what Yu De Thai was previously. Definitely a shame for me to not be able to go here again ever.
label: negative

text: Wonderfully prepared fresh food, attentive wait staff and a great, cozy atmosphere. Highly recommended!!
label: positive

text:
I don't know what all the bad reviews are for. This place is great! First time here, so far it's amazing.
Friendly staff, great food, hot coffee. What more could you ask for?
It's busy! So yes you'll wait, but not an overwhelmingly long amount of time.
Come in. Get the Ricotta Toast. It's not too sweet or heavy.
label: positive

text:
I like how they allow you to purchase drinks outside before you come in. The bloody Mary's were great and had lots of toppings!

The Peacemaker allows you to mix up multiple benedicts. That's a game changer! Love it!

The crawfish is fresh and perfectly cooked. Great service even at a super busy time!
label: positive

text: Great vendors and so much fun to walk around and explore. Definitely stop by for some ice cream and donuts!
label: positive

text:
It's a smaller sized Thai restaurant (about 6ish tables). Better call if party size more than 4 people.

They are also open on Sunday and they were busy. There is one server person doing it seems everything and the 2 chefs cooking in the back.

Got the panang curry with extra shrimp (total of 8 tail on shrimps). It had green beans and some slivers of green bell peppers, but no other veggies like carrots or onions. Curry sauce very fragrant with basil and lemongrass flavors.

About $5 for Thai tea.

Was not asked about what heat level we wanted for curry but  Mr Bland ate the curry we received slowly and drank his water. In fact, every table asked for a refill of water.

Also got the green papaya salad that came with about 4 shrimps, too!

Do a Yelp check-in!
label: positive

text: Such a yummy lunch! Amazing customer service! They forgot to put chicken on my salad & gave me a whole other salad with chicken! Definitely will be back!
label: positive

text:
I have frequented the original Ms. Saigon many times before,  but it was time to try this downtown "branch". I also tried to expand beyond my normal Pho order by ordering Bon Bo Hue (spicy beef noodle soup with vermicelli noodles with sliced broiled beef and Vietnamese ham). 

I expected rich flavor from chile paste and shrimp paste, and I found this broth lacking in complexity and richness. The broth did have a kick from black peppery spice not from the complex broth.  I ordered large thinking there would be some leftover, but perhaps this is post covid portion - it would be considered small by any means. 

Ms. Saigon has served me well in the past with delicious pho consistently. I can't help but to feel that this downtown location and Bon Bo Hue missed the mark. I will give it another shot in the future.
label: neutral

text: I try a donut in every city I visit. The donuts here are out of this world. Might just be the best donut I've ever had. They are unreal.
label: positive

text: The worst McDonalds I have ever been to. There are regularly groups of gang members hanging out inside and negotiating drug deals (even though it is "closed" for eat in). The employees have bad attitudes and always get orders wrong. The place has trash strewn on the floor. A discredit to the franchise.
label: negative

text:
Wow! Hands down, the best Cuban sandwich in Florida. I live in Miami, and have been to many little Cuban bakeries and famous restaraunts and not even one of them came close to this one.
The bread is toasted to perfection, yet soft on the inside, filled with an abundant amount of pork, ham and cheese. I ordered a half size and it left me wanting more. My boyfriend ordered a media noche, wow it was good also. I ordered a guava and cheese pastelito. I have never tasted a better one, it was soft with just enough sweetness but not overwhelming. 
This is a must go place!!!! Don't miss it. They know how to do sandwiches.
label: positive

text: Absolutely Delicious Food!!!!!!! Well worth the wait for the delicious homemade food. CANT SAY ENOUGH 10/10
label: positive

text: Great brunch place. Don't sleep on this NOlA eatery. But I would suggest not walking in without calling ahead to be placed on the waitlist.
label: positive

text: Upon visiting Tampa, I was looking for a quick bite and to stay on the healthy side. If that's what you're looking for this place is for you. Everything was so fresh. I got the pesto chicken wrap. Perfect size and not overfilling.
label: positive

text: This cafe is great! The atmosphere is relaxing and calm, beautifully decorated. The owners are super friendly, they have a nice selection of teas, coffee and snacks and the kitties are adorable. We plan to come back often!
label: positive

text: Mind boggling delicious! My friends and I went there and we all utterly adored what we ordered. The Tikki Masala was spectacular and the cheese naan nearly stole the show! I'm super excited to go back and even more confused about how this place only has 3 stars!
label: positive

text: I love LA cafe. The food is amazing. The ribs are spot on! Not a very expensive place and the food is very very delicious. Great for all ages. They do have some front spot parking but have additional parking in a lot across the street
label: positive

text: Had an excellent latte and London fog here. The baristas were so nice. The coffee is the best I've had in Reno. The only bad thing is the basement is closed Mondays, so I couldn't get another coffee.
label: positive

text: Went to both the flagship location in Fishtown and Rittenhouse square original store. The drip coffee and latte were both very flavorful. Great atmosphere at both spots for studying or hanging out with friends!
label: positive

text:
Cherry Espresso Bar is located inside a building that is more than 100 years old. New Orleans coffee shops are often special places and this is another unique spot to enjoy your coffee and tea. This location next to Wisner Park was previously the old Laurel Street Fire Station and then it later served as a New Orleans Recreation Department Community Center before the city auctioned it off which led to us being able to enjoy some great coffee at Cherry Espresso Bar.   They serve some great coffee, tea and other tasty beverages along with a selection of baked goods.  This is a great spot to enjoy your caffeine and it has a good variety of seating options both inside and outside.  When you find yourself in the mood for some tasty hot beverages you should check this place out.

My video review of Cherry Espresso Bar is here:
https://youtu.be/iV1AQiz7wc8
label: positive

text: Here's your Starbucks alternative, don't get me wrong I'm a card carrying member of Starbucks, that's quaint and chilled.  They play GREAT old school soul music that provides a welcoming vibe. It's a bit off the beaten path but that mean it probably won't get huge crowds.  This is a spot most tourist will miss which is also a good thing.  Ben's a great host too boot!
label: positive

text: Just like their North Wales location, very underwhelming.   Even for take away, the wait is entirely too long. The food is good for the most part, but sometimes it's just "too much".  Far too sweet.    Since we had such a bad experience with service dining in person in NW we have never bothered here.   We come back because it's convenient when you have an itch for breakfast.   The prices were high before the supply chain issues, so that isn't a great excuse.   Breakfast for 3.5 people shouldn't be $70
label: neutral

text: Really cool coffee spot if you can find it. It is in The Basement aka the West Elm building. Loved my Golden Hour latte. The espresso and syrups were on point. Baristas were very friendly. Note: The Basement is closed on Mondays.
label: positive

text:
I got to try MOTW yesterday with my fiance and we both loved it! The barista was super helpful and polite. He walked us through the specialty and seasonal menu and gave us personal recommendations that ended up being perfect. He even checked in with us later and chatted for a bit which isn't something you necessarily expect at a coffee shop. We tried the cardamom latte and the Biscoff latte as well as their honeycomb bread and red velvet muffin. It was all very very good, but especially the cardamom latte. This was definitely the best cardamom latte I've ever had.

We will definitely be back and I highly recommend giving MOTW a try.
label: positive

text: pretty much live for my morning coffee and am a die hard Illy fan. But the Glory roast is even better Best Ive tasted. The coffee shop itself is cute, I love the positive merch. People working there are laid back, very nice. My new place to go every morning when I'm in town.
label: positive

text: I have been coming here for a few months and omg it's delicious! My bf is not vegan but he loves the vegan wings and the corn soup... he said it was the best soup he's ever had :) Cant wait to try everything else on the menu! Definitely recommend to anyone in the area looking for good service and yummy food!
label: positive

text: Rooster is always good.  Sometimes crowded but this time we got right in.  Crepe was amazing.
label: positive

text: Such a delish little treat here in SB. Would definitely recommend the Turkish delight -- JUST LIKE NARNIA! Must stop in SB
label: positive

text: I cannot stress this place enough! The employees, the atmosphere, and most important the food!! I liked this place so much the first time I came, I came here for my 21st birthday. Everyone was beyond sweet. They got the restaurant to sing happy birthday and gave me my first legal shot!! Lol ! We both got the alligator po boys with the fried deviled eggs and they were both amazing. I also got a blood bag sangria drink, which was also delicious! They are very strict with covid vaccines/ negative test so if you are thinking about coming here, this is your sign !

text:
Celebrated my Birthday w/Sunday Brunch @ Messina's Runway Cafe. The Perfect setting for an intimate group. The Ambiance, Food and Staff were spot on. 
Highly Satisfied!
label: positive

text:
This place is the worst if you are looking for a coffee to go.   They force you to order through a touch screen and they've only got one of them.  So get ready to stand in line for 10-15 minutes while someone in front of you tries to use the clunky interface to order breakfast for their whole family.  You'd think you might be able to say "Hey, can I have a cappuccino to go?" to one of the 3-4 people standing unoccupied behind the counter, but nope, you must use the screen to order.

The place looks like Cape May ice cream parlor and certainly wouldn't be somewhere I'd ever consider going for food in an area with a plethora of restaurants where the staff will take your order in person.
label: negative

text: Eggshell is quaint and welcoming. The flavors of the food are phenomenal and the shashuka is easily my favorite brunch dish of my life. Food is made to order and the servers are helpful with questions about the menu. The coffee is incredible. Portions are smaller when compared to other brunch spots with similar prices, however the quality and taste of the food more than makes up for it. Try Eggshell and you won't be disappointed!! I felt like I was transported to a European country and really enjoyed my experience here.
label: positive

text: Have this place in mind anytime you are even remotely near.    Worth the 30+ mile drive from my neck of the woods!   The food...always fresh, full of flavor  and healthy....then stay for the fantastic teas hot and cold, milk tea, boba and  no powders....all fresh brewed.  Heck go only for the peace & tranquility of a beautiful hot tea!  Rejuvenation is at hand.
label: positive

text: I love their branding. The strawberry syrup was really nice quality. So aesthetically beautiful.
label: positive

text: You see a place with positive reviews for their "mission" but not so much for the coffee or food. Just know that's the kind of place it is - heavy on the religion being in your face.
label: negative

text: Went here to buy chips and salsa for a friend on 1/11/22. I told her most of them are close to two weeks old and she just shrugged her shoulders. text: This place was an amazing brunch experience! A group of us went during our girls weekend and we were not disappointed! We did wait a little over an hour but they have the bar set up so you can walk up and order drinks while you wait. The food was very good and service was great too. Definitely recommend a trip here!

text: I have come her many times and everything I've ever had is delicious!! Although I do think it is a bit over priced. For the drink and creme brûlée it was $14.44. I enjoy what I get every time I go but I don't go often because it is expensive for the amount/what you get.
label: positive

text: Great beet salad and pizza , chips are pretty darn good. Only down fall is the wait over an hour for our food. I understand it being busy , but that's a long time to wait for your food. Totally recommend this place awsome good
label: positive

text: We enjoyed the Germantown Cafe. It was slightly pricey for the amount of food you get. I had the French toast, and it was great but not enough of you are really hungry. The bacon was decent. Friends with us had the crab cake Benedict and said they were very good. If you are with a large party, they charge you 24% for gratuity, so be prepared.

text: Honestly, this is my favorite coffee shop in town. They have two locations in Sparks: at the marina and by vista. I usually order an iced matcha with oat milk, an iced chai with oat milk, or a regular vanilla latte. They offer other non-dairy options, which is always a plus. I've never had a bad experience here, their staff is super friendly and helpful, plus service is usually fast.

text: This specific location is just horrible with everything they all have attitudes and don't make the smoothies right or know how to fully cook meat. Miss girl me and my smoothie with no gloves on
label: negative

text: The best neighborhood spot! Great iced coffee and lattes and a friendly vibe
label: positive

text:
During COVID times, I am shocked and taken aback that almost all of their staff (like 5 out of 6) who are in the kitchen either blatantly have their mask down or their noses clearly poking out.  Only the main waitress and cashier were wearing their mask.  

By contrast, the huge kitchen staff of The Lark next door are fully masked - no loose masking, no noses poking out, and absolutely no masks simply pulled down, like the staff at Lucky Penny.

text:
Food was cold, order wrong . Waiter in a dirty shirt really bad experience.
Stake was cold because they've waited for a toast to cook!
label: negative

text: I found out about la segunda because my boyfriend doordashes and I'm telling you I have never ate from a Cuban spot that was that good and I'm Cuban myself. I've never had a Cuban toast better than here thank you so much for your delicious food !!!
label: positive

text:
If you have ever done a candle making experience before this place will be a HUGE disappointment. 

From the rude staff to the inaccurate website description this was all around a pretty disappointing experience. 

From the time we walked in the staff looked like we were bothering them patronizing the business. After getting checked in we were lead to a table and then just left there to figure it out. When trying to ask questions the staff literally just ignored us and walked away. This happened multiple times by different staff members who when I tried to speak to them literally walked away and disappeared in the back. The place wasn't even busy. The tables were more than half empty. 

We brought wine but were never offered any cups. No water. No one was even working the "coffee bar". 

At the table there are paper instructions on how to make your candle and you are pretty much just left to figure everything out yourself. 

Online they say they have 40 different fragrances. That's a huge lie. There were maybe a dozen to choose from unless they were hidden elsewhere but none of the staff let me ask any questions. There were a good amount of vessels to choose from but if I'm making a candle I'm a bit more interested in the scent than the container so that wasn't super impressive. 

Once you figure out what you need to do the process literally takes like 10 mins and at the end is the only time the staff will interact with you as they immediately take your supplies and try and rush you out. 

Also you don't get to leave with your candle. It takes 3 hours to cool and you have to come back later to get it which is inconvenient. 

There's better candle making and BYOB experiences in Philly. Don't waste your money here.
label: negative

text: Amazing family-owned cafe, incredible healthy food, a ton of craft beer options, and friendly service. Highly recommend!
label: positive

text: GREAT BRUNCH! Warm, bright atmosphere. Servers were awesome and gave us a free latte that was made by mistake that they didn't want to go to waste. You can't go wrong with the menu items- they're flavorful and artful. A great experience!
label: positive

text: Call ahead to get added to the waitlist. Plenty of outdoor seating while you wait. Outdoor heaters if it's chilly. Bar seating also available. Staff was very helpful. Chilaquiles were just a bit spicy but so good! Egg was perfectly done, chips were drenched with sauce but still crispy adding to the texture as you bite into this cheesy deliciousness. The bubble waffle was just the right enough of sweetness to counterbalance the spice. Perfect breakfast!
label: positive

text: The staff is always friendly. They probably have the best latte in Nashville. The place is clean. It is conveniently located near my work. On the other hand, sometimes the latte quality is inconsistent.
label: positive

text: Great coffee spot in Philly! They have fun menu items and a good spot to pop in and out of. They also have a great deal with beans- buy a bag and get a free drink!
label: positive

text: Too much ice in my boba. The flavor was okay. For a snow it's usually smooth but it was more of a slush.
label: negative

text: A casual quick spot to grab sandwiches, juice and coffee. Stopped by and grabbed the Lechon sandwich which was AMAZING. had a strong garlic/onion flavor and the bread was perfectly crisp on the exterior yet still soft on the interior with pulled pork. Ordered by the side window then self seated on the inside to eat, but also had a few tables for sidewalk seating as well. Looking forward to returning to grab a snack when I'm in the St. Petersburg area.
label: positive

text:
Stopped at Willa Jean for a cute and quick solo brunch! I grabbed a seat at the bar as the actual dining area was pretty full on a late Monday morning. I opted to start with a frosé which was really good! I ordered my food and it promptly came, I was so excited! I had the Willa Jean breakfast which includes 2 sausage links, 2 strips of bacon, cheese grits, eggs, and a biscuit. The grits and eggs were soooo good! The sausage was good also, but the bacon was a bitttttt too salty for me. Would I recommend this dish? Yes. 

After eating my meal, I couldn't help but feel pressured to indulge in something sweet, as their pastries and bakery items were very visually pleasing. I originally ordered banana pudding which was not good at all. The pudding part was greatly lacking flavor. Luckily, I was able to exchange it and had the milk and cookies which were delicious! I typically do not like to drink milk, but the milk they serve is magically delicious. 

I also would like to rave about Camille, the server at the bar. She has such a kind and genuine spirit and exudes such grace and positive energy. If you have the chance to be served by her, your experience will be enhanced. 

Overall, Willa Jean is cute, and the food is good enough to come back for a quick bite!
label: positive

text: I sometimes door dash in my spare time and I went to this location before to pick up some food the drive thru line was super long so I parked and walked in the store (the lobby door was unlocked) soon as I walk in I'm bombarded with question "what you doin in here?" "How you get in here" "you know the lobby closed huh" I answer, I'm picking up a order for doordash, the door was unlocked, no I didn't know the lobby was closed cause the door is unlocked" once I get through the McDonald's interrogation I ask the lady who's talking to me if she can hand me the food because it's sitting right there on the counter and she started to hand it to me then all of a sudden the manager comes all the way from the office to snatch the bag out of my hand and tells me I need to go through the drive thru. But sir... I've already touched the food so whatever it is you're trying to do to abide by safety protocols is already out the window. Long story short instead of arguing with this man I go back outside get in the drive thru line and wait a extra 15 minutes to pick up a order that was already in my hands. I avoid this McDonald's as much as I can. Since I've been in New Orleans the last five years this is hands down the rudest McDonald's out here.
label: negative

text: overpriced, paid $7 for small coffee that tasted GRAINY and UNTASTEFUL. as quoted by my friend "i would rather drink black coffee with boogers." my other friend got a caramel iced latte, a basic right!? you think that ANY coffee shop could master this....but no! such a disappointment. the ice cubes & aesthetic were the only good things.

text: Good coffee in an interesting location. Definitely go if you have a sweet tooth. Their seasonal flavors are really intense. I tried the rosemary latte and there was massive flavor only matched by the sweetness. Not even sure how their beans are but if you want a treat that also happens to be caffeinated this is the spot.

text: Awesome bagels and breakfast sandwiches! The pic speaks for itself. Super tasty and lots of options. The store is small and not any seating for indoor dining but a great option for on the go breakfast or brunch.
label: positive

text: Had coffee several times here and breakfast once in Jan of 21' while on vacation. Very friendly staff! Breakfast was awesome! Coffee was very good! I would definitely recommend for breakfast!
label: positive

text: Came here for lunch yesterday. Was fairly busy - had to wait a little bit to place our order. Ordered the Pollo Asado Sandwich. I ate every bite...considered ordering a second one to go. Perfect amount of spice and seasoning. All of the flavors worked so well together. Also had the Mixed Berry Fresca which was also refreshing. 10/10
label: positive

text: I love Everything about Bearcat Cafe. The ambiance, but mostly the food. Always hot, fresh & Tasty.
label: positive

text: Great café con leche! This place is conveniently located and clean. Their coffee and pastries are delicious. They're open early in the morning until the late evening. Parking is easy to get and the staff is always welcoming
label: positive

text: The food was good and the service was decent. It was busy, but overall good experience!
label: positive

text: Love this little bit of Paris on the streets of Tampa. Food we had was great both the Strasbourg crepes and the Parisienne baguette sandwich. The sidewalk tables mimic a Paris cafe although it's not so much people watching as "car watching" given they look at the street!
label: positive

text:
Amazing! I can't wait to return. Our mochas were perfect--not to sweet and smooth.

Our Savory crepe was chicken, spinach,  and bacon with cream sauce. I'm so glad I took a chance on a small French cafe in Tampa. I've been to Montreal and enjoy French cuisine. 

The baguette and brie was so tasty. I send a kiss to the Chef! Thank you for being our highlighted meal of Tampa. I will see you again.

Prices and portions are reasonable.  Actually I think it was unpriced based on the quality of the food. I can't wait to eat there again!

Customer service was mediocre but I don't let that influence my experience. It was a cold and rainy Sunday morning. The host/server was from Poland (didn't get her name) but she wasn't thrilled to see us walk in. We were greeted very underwhelming. I don't internalize bad customer service but I still notice it.

Parking is okay. We arrived at 7:30am so the streets were clear. I know how crazy it gets Downtown so I anticipate walking a block or 2 on my next visit.
label: positive

text:
I LOVE boba and I LOVE this place! My first job was at a boba shop in California and where I'm from there are about 20 boba shops in my medium sized  town. I've had boba all over the world and this boba spot is pretty good! 

I've had the Thai tea, brown sugar milk tea, lychee iced tea, and Taro milk tea all delicious! The teas have a authentic sweet and and hint of herbal flavor to them. I also got boba in all of the drinks. The boba is made the proper way! They soak their boba in a brown sugar mixture and keep it warm before serving. The texture is perfect being soft and chewy. 

I've tried all of the boba places in Boise and this is definitely the best one!
label: positive

text: Wife and I were looking for an intimate place to enjoy the evening. FOUND IT! Ambience was great, food was delicious and more than could eat. Service......AMAZING!!!! Our waiter, Curtis, was perfect for us and the evening. Very personal, funny and met every need. We will be back!! Hopefully we will have Curtis as our waiter again!!!

text: I used to love this location more but lately for the past 2yrs, it seems to me that their selections have gotten less available. I still love their pastries and cakes, but certain ones just not always available. Their cakes are not too sweet and very delicious. I love their cheesecake too! They recently added mochi donut as well. I ordered a coffee frap the other day when I stop by and also got a few pastries for my daughter and a personal size cheesecake. text: I really liked this coffee shop! I just got a generic vanilla latte to be safe and I loved it. A little sweeter than usual but it's okay. I came around 5:40 so I got my drink made in minutes so it was a quick in and out. Located next to a lot of cute shops too!

text:
When in Rome....am I right..

text:

Maybe it's because if new staff constantly or I don't know what. My last time in, they insisted they made my smoothie with peanut butter but clearly had not. Now, I got a smoothie for me and my wife and he only made enough for about 75% of each 20oz. Cup and didn't make them right at all. What we got was a ton of ice and not thoroughly blended so we basically chewed out smoothies.

text: All of the regular donuts have some kind of cream on the top layer. We even brought them back to my boyfriend who has a huge sweet tooth and he was disgusted but the texture. Also, they added so much salt to every single item we got , including the cookies. text:

I ordered the Spanish latte (12 oz - $5). I liked it better than the Spanish latte at Bovaconti Coffee - and that's saying a lot because that's one of my favorite coffee shops in Indy! 

The barista, Reagan, was super nice and informed me that the Spanish latte was one of their more popular drinks. 

I will say that seating was kind of limited, so my friend and I just sat the bar where people picked up their drinks. Maybe it was quite busy because of the holiday. 

Overall, I would definitely come back and check out some of the other drinks on their menu.

text: For thanksgiving 2021, I visited the Ft Lauderdale/Hollywood, FL location. It was first time visiting any of the or properties. I would suggest bringing wipes and hand sanitizer to do a quick over in your room & to wipe the slots & tables and I'm not saying this because of COVID, some people are straight up nasty and to protect yourself from getting sick & ruining your time...it's worth taking a little extra precautions. The smoking is thick; however, they do have a non-smoking zone I stumbled across (the place is big) and a non-smoking zone on the 2nd floor which was great (tables close down, not sure what time). text:
Great brunch spot. There's a wide range of more unique brunch items outside of just eggs and potatoes and everything is made with a little more pizazz than the average diner. There was constantly a wait because of how popular it is.

Chicken & waffles - nice and crispy chicken that's not too dry and crispy waffle. It's served in a bowl which makes cutting the waffles hard and the syrup drain to the bottom waffles so I would've preferred a plate.  Recommend pouring the syrup as you eat to not make the waffles too soggy. Syrup is not very viscous but tastes good. Decent portion size but not to make you overly bloated or with leftovers to take home. 

Staff was relatively attentive despite it being very busy. Not super personable but not an issue. 

For those with Covid concerns - they check for vaccination BUT the seating is pretty close to one another so not the best distancing arrangement. Also the booths are small so even sitting with your party feels somewhat squished.

Service: 4.5/5
Food: 5/5
Ambience 4/5

text: I go to the eegees on st marys maybe twice a week or so. They're great there. The employees are friendly. The food is almost always on point. text:
