label: positive

text:
I was born and raised in Hong Kong and my parent are from China so I get to know many fish from both side with their real taste for many many years before I moved to American. I love Dim Sum 點心, When I was in Hong Kong I go yum cha 飲茶 for dim sum with my family every Sunday as tradition. I usually go to Philly Chinatown to get real Chinese food, I was excited to see a dim sum place opened up close to where I live and had to try it. First of all there is not a dim sum variety having the restaurant name as dim sum court, it should focus more on dim sum as it main course before any other entrees, I recommend expending more, my favorite dim sum dish like chicken feet and sticky rice with lotus leaf is missing, hope they add it one day. Second I think the owner maybe from China as I can tell from the menu are based in China as it follow the cuisine from China and Shanghai. Anyway enough talking, today I order a few dish to try. Steam Pork Bun 菜肉包, BBQ Pork Bun 叉燒包, Pan Fried Pork Bun 生煎包, Yang Chow Fried Rice 揚州炒飯, and General Tso Chicken 左宗棠雞. 
1. Steam pork bun is on point, the look, size, and taste is good. I recommend this bun, but I don't think it should cost 2.5 a piece, maybe it's inflation.
2. BBQ Pork Bun is also on point, but I think this is a mini version because the regular one is usually bigger. The pork is lean and without fat which I like, sauce and taste is good, the bun itself is soft and good. I recommend this.
3. Pan Fried Pork Bun, this is a Shanghai dish, what I received could be bigger, usually it is bigger size in Shanghai. The taste and look is good, I recommend this.
4. Yang Chow Fried Rice, this dish is missing the main ingredient BBQ pork (Char Siu 叉燒), instead it used shredded pork which is ok if you don't know, to me this is more like the American version of house special fried rice, also it's missing green pea. It need more taste, it's missing its fragrance. I probably won't get it again.
5. General Tso Chicken, no doubt this is an American dish and I don't care much for it, but my wife is American and she love this dish so she got it, the chicken just a little smaller than most Chinese restaurants, but it looks ok and my wife say it's good and it's spicy. I don't think any Chinese restaurants can mess it up but I recently got one from a dim sum place up in this area and it's awful. 
Overall I can recommend this place and will continue to try different dish like soup dumplings and beef chow fun. The price is ok because you can't find a place that serve dim sum up in this area if you are craving them. My order is for takeout and not dine in, so I don't know how the service is.
label: positive

text: It's hard to mess up a Dunkin.  But this is the worst one I've ever been in.  Asked for a sourdough breakfast sandwich with sausage.  Got a sourdough sandwich with bacon.  Asked for a powdered sugar donut ( pictured on the menu), told they don't have powdered sugar donuts.....actually felt scolded for asking.  My wife got a toasted bagel....charred toasted like a hockey puck.  The staff was not friendly.  This place is nasty Andrew bad!

text:
I finally went to try out this place after seeing all the posts on Instagram.  I called ahead of time on a Saturday just to check that they still had lots of selection.  I was informed that they only make the big tarts in the morning and sell out as the day goes by.  The minis are always available, they are $4.50 each or 6 for $25.00.  Since I was also buying some as a gift, it went for the 6 pack and 2 extra minis.

The tarts look really good however, I think they look better than they taste.  I chose the ones that looked fresh as some flavours were starting to dry out.  There were lots of flavours so it was hard to choose.  The mango was nice, fresh and fruity tasting.  The tiramisu was also nice with that kinda coffee taste.  I was surprised by the crust, it wasn't too hard but looked grainy with a slight beige colour.

There were 3 big tarts available for purchase when I arrived at 5 pm.  I thought they were a bit pricey though.

I won't be running back here to try other flavours but I think the mini box is a great gift idea.  The packaging is cute.

text:
SB Roasting Co. makes some great coffee.  I'd stop by their lower off State Street location on occasion to grab a cup of coffee.  Used to use their coupon card but most of the employees did not know how to use their system.

After multiple times of too much time and energy waiting for people to figure out what they were doing finally reverted to auto-bill-ship 5 lb bags.  

It was fine for 2-3 years and this year January 2022 they screwed up and shipped the wrong item 2 times in a row.  

So now I have to get a return address label a second time, print it out, tape the box up, tape the label on and drive it over to UPS.  I've canceled all future auto ship orders with SB Roasting Co.  They've lost a long time customer.

Good bye and good riddance you're personnel have lost a long time loyal customer.  There's other coffee companies that actually care and take care of their customers.

text:
Our first visit to this newish (five weeks) cafe.  Located in a strip mall so there is ample parking. Was surprised to walk in and see a collection of Frida Kahlo prints and photographs hanging on the dining room walls. The cafe also has a decent sized patio available.  We walked in for breakfast around 8:00 AM and were immediately seated.  Our server was a delightful young lady who was very knowledgeable about the menu items and how those items are prepared.  My wife ordered the Hummingbird Omelete, which comes as a large omelet with spinach, tomatoes, onions, and provolone cheese.  It was accompanied by hashbrowns and grilled sourdough toast.
I ordered the Hornitos French toast, which is your basic french toast but made with an egg batter that includes a splash of Hornitos Reposado tequila, accompanied by either link sausage or bacon and sliced melon.  Everything was delicious!  We'll definitely add Frida's to our list of go-to  restaurants.
label: positive

text:
I've been following Middle Child for years on the internet, ogling the breakfast sandwiches and dreaming of the day I would finally put one in my face. Historically, when I am this excited about food, I end up disappointed. Reality simply cannot live up to the unreasonably high expectations I've developed. I finally had the opportunity to go to Middle Child yesterday, and I am very pleased to report that it truly was as delicious as I'd imagined. Big fluffy eggs, perfectly toasty bread. I got a hash brown on the side and it was wonderful. I can't wait to go back again so I can try a phoagie or a charred on. Thank you for not disappointing me, Middle Child.

Important details: we ordered online and arrived just as it was being wrapped up - about 40 minutes on a Sunday morning. You can also order & wait, you can take it home, or you can eat it inside at a table. Or, if you're us, you get ready to take it with you but are too excited to wait and end up eating it in the car.

text: I had a horrible experience at this restaurant. From the very beginning before our party even got to the restaurant we had to move tables because of gigantic bugs that were on the window pane. Secondly, no one apologized about the matter not even a manager. So fast forward, I go to Ricks a lot, I get the same entree every time which is Jumbo shrimp scampi.. Well this time the shrimp were not deveined. Which is disgusting. So I asked the server to take a look and could I please order something else. I had to wait even longer to eat when everyone else had finished for my new entree.. Well when it was time to pay I asked the server could I speak to a manager . I am a server and I've worked in the food industry a long time. I should receive a discount , a desert , apology, or something.  When the manager walked up to me, he was rude , he didn't introduced himself, he did not ask what was wrong, he automatically assumed I was just complaining.. he said didn't we give you more food lol his exact words and walked off, I was in shock , so I asked to speak with another manager who was also rude, not one manager apologized to me for giving me dirty food. Not one offered a soup, salad, or dessert. When I voiced that to her...We had a cake on the table bc of our party , she said" don't you have cake lol". Why would I offer dessert.." I was appalled.. This was the worst service in my life. One argued with me and said they always have clean shrimp, but the server had to tell her the shrimp were dirty.  I was racial profiled by every manager in that establishment.  They even called the cops on me because they assumed I was not paying which was so embarrassing.  I spend good money every time I'm there. I will never step back in that restaurant.  Each manager was laughing and giggling with all the Caucasian tables and walking by interacting  , but the African Americans didn't even get that. Ridiculous!!

text:
Is someone pulling your leg?  Denny's is a step above Waffle House which isn't saying much. I Hop has gone downhill but a step or two above Denny's which is not saying much.

The only good place to get breakfast in Santa Barbara is Jeanine's.  

Best  in SBC is Ellen's Pancake House in Buellton about a 35 minute gorgeous ocean and scenic drive north up 101 just off the freeway.  

They are the best of the best in SBC.  Quality and amount of food is huge... prices fair.  Expect a line of people waiting to get a table or counter seat that are locals not tourists.  Prepare to go out for a walk or hike.  One doesn't go away hungry. Staff are fantastic and many have worked there for years.  Exceptional service. Unlike most breakfast lunch restaurants family owned and run onsite and cooks and servers that have worked there for decades.

Several of our favorites are chicken fried steak and eggs with thick apple cured bacon bits added to gravy with homemade biscuits and gravy, NY steak and eggs, thick ham steak and eggs, 3, 6 or 12 egg omelettes made any way one likes.  Never, ever forget the thin Danish pancakes that can be ordered with fruit.  They are truly a delicacy and come with powdered sugar dusting.  Just a little butter and real maple syrup is all they need.  If one wants them a tad sweeter the preserves they offer will put it over the top.  Prepare for a to go box.  Local ranchers and farmers eat here for breakfast starting at about 6am as they need a hearty breakfast to make it to lunch or dinner.
label: negative

text:
Indoor seating and outdoor patio available.   

When we arrived on this Sunday morning around 10am, we were seated immediately (inside).  I enjoyed the chorizo and chile omelet (which could have been ordered as a fritatta).  I had potatoes and tortillas with my order.  The coffee is delicious and they leave the carafe for coffee and water on your table.  My little enjoyed the Belgian waffle which came with compote and butter.  Added bacon and potatoes.  Sarah our server placed the order as the Trifecta for pricing purposes.  My little gobbled it up.

There are tables, half booths, and booths. 

From some of my previous visits:  The Benedicts are super yummy.  I am not a fan of the million dollar bacon, because I prefer crisp bacon.  I ordered it previously and when my server saw that I did not eat more than the first taste, they offered me something else instead.

Come a little earlier on the weekends; apparently everyone sleeps in:  There were people waiting when we left.  They gave their name and number to the host(ess) and were notified by text message when their table was ready.

Looking forward to trying the Purple Haze (a lemonade with lavender and more).

Their usual fragrances along the wall are all named after Philadelphia neighborhoods - which I thought was a nice touch. 

We also tried the seltzer experience, where you can pick a fruit flavor to be added to a seltzer and have something to drink while making candles. They offered edible straws for the seltzers too. They're also BYO. Looking forward to coming back in the future to make another candle!

text:
I don't know how many times I have passed Daily Eats and been in the area but had never stopped to eat. It is a known staple in Soho so I have zero excuses. 

I love that their menu is all day and when looking over everything their French toast sounded good even though it was lunch time. I just love breakfast so I can eat it at any time of the day. Personally, I do not eat that much per meal and my eyes are definitely bigger than my stomach so it was great to see that I could get a half order (2 halves) which is really the perfect amount for me. 

I also ordered their sangria which tasted like fall in a mason jar glass. It was a spiced apple with grannie smith apple pieces and a cinnamon stick garnishment. 

There was a slight miscommunication with my waiter while ordering and he had originally asked me how I wanted my grits with the French toast, which I was didn't think came with it since I was only getting the half order, but he said it did. I really don't eat grits but since he said they came with it, I said I'd have it with cheese. When the food came out, he also brought the bill and I saw that the grits were added onto it. I spoke with him about it since I mentioned it during the ordering process and even said I don't eat them but since they came with it, I'd try them with cheese. He quickly apologized for the misunderstanding and took them away and off the bill for me. If it had been an item that I enjoy, I would have just kept them and ate them it grits are just not my thing. His attitude and quick actions were super appreciated and conveyed several times.
label: positive

text: I stumbled upon this place on my way home after a long run. I was already on my way elsewhere and i decided to make a u-turn and try this place. I'm so glad I did. They have so many options for smoothies, juices and food as well. I usually fast until late in the afternoon so I opted for just a juice. I ordered the anti-inflammatory juice and it was so refreshing. I ordered and the cashier said to find a seat and they'd bring my order to me. I thought that was very kind. There is a lot of indoor seating and plenty of outdoor seating as well. They have cute palapa seating areas and a corn hole game. While waiting for my juice I noticed there is a bike trail and there were a lot of bike riders passing by and some stopped here at the cruising smoothie after. The name of this place then made sense. The parking situation was confusing. I had to enter through a dirt dead end road to park behind the business. I noticed they also have a drive thru which is pretty cool. Overall, I had a great experience! Friendly  and prompt service. I will definitely be coming back!

text:
My friend and I had a very unpleasant first experience here, after shopping at target we decided to check this store out. Granted there was only one girl working there, it felt as she did not want to be there whatsoever. 
Since it was our first time there, we had no idea what to order, and when it was are time to order she very snappy and it felt as if she was rushing us. We had no idea what was good. So we ordered whatever we saw first and that was it. 
She made the drinks really fast and moved us along, not saying anything. I said thank you and she actually ignored me. 
The green machine was really good but the boba was really hard and very plastic like. 
My friend got the almond flavored milk tea and it was sooo bad, it was sickly sweet and it had white clumps in it. So I patiently waited until the girl was finishing up with a customer to tell her about  the drink. I said this exactly," my friend did not like this drink I think somethings wrong with it, is there anything you could do, i don't mind paying for another one." And she said she get me after the next customer. So I go to throw the drink away and wait behind the next customer. Mind you after the next customer was a group of 8 school aged girls, so I waited and after she finished the customer in front of me, without even looking at me, she goes on to the group of girls and take their orders. I was directly in her line of sight but she chose to ignore me. So I took my friend and we left. I don't think I will ever go back.
label: negative

text:
I ventured down to St Charles Main Street for last some minute stocking stuffer shopping. I made a commitment last year to do my gifting from local places and my stockings still had plenty of room to fill. 

I really hadn't planned to buy spices...but on the day I went most stores didn't open until noon which I didn't realize and this store was open. So I ventured in. It is tiny and at first it was a bit overwhelming trying to see what all was in there... but as people started leaving I ended up with the store to myself. 

They have SO MUCH STUFF here! Spices, gifts, soup mixes, jams, hot sauces, premade trinkets, tea items, etc. I enjoyed that they had quite a few neat gift items already packaged including a "items from MO" box. 

I ended up hitting the spices and purchased multiple for stocking stuffers and to use for myself. We've already used the taco seasoning and the ranch mix I purchased and we loved them both. Great flavor! The spices are packaged in multiple size offerings and were reasonably priced. 

I also gifted a St Joe's multipurpose style seasoning and the receiver used it on veggies and chicken and said they really liked it. 

This will definitely be a store that I can utilize throughout the year and especially for gifts.
label: positive

text:

text: Don't go to this Starbucks. Pregnant with my third kid and barely sleeping with bad pregnancy brain. I stopped by the Starbucks while walking my dogs because my daughter was hungry. I went inside to get her a snack. Being human and a tired, sleep deprived mom I accidentally walked off without my dogs. Realizing this shortly after, I called Starbucks in a panic & about in tears and told them I was not far away and was literally sprinting back to the store.  I asked if they could make sure no one steals them or anything. They said okay I see them and sure. WELL After this they called animal control and the sheriffs department on me. The sheriff told me after seeing my dogs she realized this wasn't really an appropriate call & that I was obviously a sleep deprived mom. She said she could tell I felt terrible and that my dogs were extremely well behaved and well cared for. She then told me to leave before animal control got there. Luckily, the sheriff was so understanding and kind. I will never return to this location. In the past they have done annoying things like mess up my drinks etc but I understand that mistakes happen and have never gotten upset. Now, I'm MAD. Support a local coffee shop instead. I am so easy to please this is my FIRST negative review. I usually only review things I love.

text:
I Am A Biscuit Slut for Willa Jean - direct quote from our dope waitress who shall remain nameless (she knows who she is!). 

This spot hits for sure. We love another well no in place, also in the French quarter but we wanted to try something different and we are so glad we did! Walked in before I got too busy and we were seated immediately after they checked our vaccine cards. The menu is not vast but the items listed demands your attention. The first day we went, I ordered the hangover bowl and my wife had the avocado toast, preluded by the monkey bread and artichoke dip. All were absolutely fantastic. The monkey bread has crunchy caramelized toffee bits and the dip was so cheesy and gooey - what a delicious delight. The egg in both main dishes were cooked to perfection and they are NOT shy with the avocado! Also the matcha vanilla latte is insaneeeeeeee! 

We returned the next morning because, why not?  The way was annoying because it was cold AND windy but worth the wait. This time We started with the banana breas which was exquisite! And I had the tabasco honey biscuit - it was so amazing it was damn near rude! I mean wow! The edges were crisp but dense and doughy and the chicken was insane. I added and egg but it certainly didnt need it! My wife had the bbq toast which she enjoyed but I thought it was just ok in comparison to the biscuit! The absolute star however was the sausage gravy! I have only tried sausage gravy twice previously and both have been disappointments but this?!?! Was absolutely mind blowing Lee delicious. It was chunky but somehow smoothand bursting with flavor with a lovely kick of spice - I mean absolutely insane. I also had a strawberry bellini which was surprisingly delish and of course another matcha latte. 

This is a special spot from the warmth of the folks working there to the lovingly made food. Next time Im in Nawlins, I will make a beeline straight for WIlla Jean without hesitation!
label: positive

text:
Roco's aka SB Roasting Co ratings of quality craft coffee are 80 out of about 90 points quality in coffee.  That is night and day better than most coffee house chains that at best might be 50 out of 90.  So that's actually very good.

We had been on auto ship-bill 5 LB bags for years and so far this year have shipped the wrong product twice which is why they have dropped from 4-5 stars to a 1 star review.  Having to tape up packages, print out labels and take them to UPS two times in a row to return them is a waste of time and energy.  Will give it one last try.  If they get it wrong 3 times in a row will stop doing business with them.

From what I've recently discovered there are 90 point craft coffee makers out there that cost a little bit more for higher quality online that auto ship-invoice several times a year.  They're sending me a sample to try.  

Great personal service and if it turns out to be better will go with this company and cancel with ROCO.  After years of being a loyal customer disappointed and will update once I try another supplier.  A company has to look in the mirror when they drive customers away to seek out better and more reliable coffee companies.
label: negative

text:
I went to NOLA for a while and having eaten so much fried foods, I had needed something that is not as fatty and with a fresh taste. I was pleased to having found a place that serve pho here. 

The place is a family owned and the owner/server was funny. We ordered Pho, a couple tea and juice. 
The pho was pretty decent for the region given that there is not much competition (I think there are only like 4 or 5 Vietnamese spots in the area). It has a refreshing mild taste. 
I thought the price of the pho was fair; however, the Bahn mi was pretty pricey. Bahn mi at this place was similarly priced as the pho. Most places I have been to, Bahn mi at the same location is 20-25% less than pho. 
The juice was definitely not fresh squeeze which is what I expected and they are not falsely advertising. The Vietnamese iced coffee is also overpriced. 

Overall, for the region I think the place is a solid 4 stars and you will not regret trying it. However, If the place was located in any other major cities, I would give them 3 stars ( I just have a very picky taste).

text:
Fantastic!

This is a review from a donut snob. Seriously. I've eaten a lot of donuts in my travels and most are disappointing. I have a single, favorite donut shop in California, but living on the East Coast now, that's rather moot. 

I saw a review of Federal Donuts a while back and have followed them on Facebook for several years. However, we live about five hours away so it was never really practical to visit. In a stroke of luck, I happened to attend a meeting that took me near Philadelphia, so I decided to drive out of the way and stop by the closest Federal Donuts.

This location is literally a hole in the wall...I would never have known the location was there unless my nav system said it was. Purely a residential neighborhood; I didn't see another business anywhere. I thought it was closed at first, but I parked, knocked on the window, and sure enough - I was in luck! They were open and the rest is history.

Because I wanted to try everything, I ordered a half dozen of their hot and fresh, a half dozen of their fancy, and two chicken sandwiches (I had a couple of my kids with me - I'm not as much of a glutton as I appear! LOL) 

So how were they? We tried the hot and fresh donuts while they were still hot. I have to say - they were really, really good. Unfortunately, I didn't get a photo of their menu, but the donut with the cinnamon sugar coating was the best of the three, even though all of them were great. I really liked the texture, the flavor, the amount of sugar coating. Overall, they were really, really great. Better than any other donut I've had on the East Coast, hands down.

I then went on to try the chicken sandwich. I loved the crunch of the fried batter. I've seen a couple of complaints that the chicken seemed dry; it didn't seem that way to me. My only complaint was that it did seem quite salty - and I'm typically one who likes extra salt! Would I eat another one, absolutely! Would I recommend cutting back on the salt a bit? Yep, I think that would help. I did eat part of the second sandwich and it seemed less salty, so maybe the first one was a bit of a fluke. Still...would I eat another? You bet!

We waited till we got home to try the fancy donuts. The snowball was by far the favorite by both taste and texture. It was a hands-down winner. The rest were really good. The donuts disappeared in this order: snowball, peanut butter cup (that was really good), banana (surprisingly good!), apple pie, blueberry (I liked it; my teenagers didn't as much), and then the chocolate cream one. I think that's the name...it was chocolate coated with a yellow dollop in the middle. The texture was great; the flavor was lacking, in my opinion (my kids liked it though). 

Overall, I really think these are the best donuts on the East Coast. Or at least the best donuts I've had an opportunity to try. The texture is amazing, and for the most part, the flavors are really fantastic. The chicken sandwich was a bit salty for my taste, but I'd definitely eat it again for certain. 

My recommendation: if you haven't tried Federal Donuts, you need to! I most highly recommend them and support them as a non-chain local business too. I really wish they'd open a shop closer to me because I'd be there as a regular (then again...my waistline might not appreciate that...LOL). Anyway, the bottom line is this: I most highly recommend Federal Donuts and if I'm within an hour or two of Philly in the future, I'll make the drive and pick up some!
label: positive

text: I have been going here for almost 10 years now. I used to go all the time when I was in high school around here. They were one of the first spots to master gluten free wraps and food. text: I am so happy that I got a chance to check this place out. I ordered the chicken and pork sliders with a side of fries. The fires were really good and had a yummy seasoning on it. I also loved that it had slaw in it. It added a crunch and creaminess to the slider. I liked the pork slider. The pork was a little dry for me, but still very flavorful and yummy. The sliders were tiny, so if you are hungry I would say order more than 2 sliders. If you are looking for a snack, I would say two sliders is perfect enough.

I went around 1pm for a smoothie and it was very busy with doordash pickups, high school kids hanging, and gym people. I waited only a few minutes for my smoothie and it tasted great. 

Prices are real high here for plenty of reasons I'm sure. But if I feel like treating myself, there are no other smoothie places that have as much fresh ingredients nearby so this is worth it for me occasionally.

text:
Rooster is definitely our go to brunch place in St. Louis. There are long waits on the weekend but it's definitely worth the wait. I wish you could make reservations in advance but maybe they'll add that in the future. 

Our favorite thing on the menu is definitely the brunch burger. You get two biscuit sandwiches with your choice of sausage (chicken, pork, vegan) and how you want your eggs cooked. We opt for chicken and scrambled. It is delicious with the rooster mayo and the hearty breakfast potatoes are a nice pairing. This time, I tried one of the scrambles with avocado, chicken sausage and pico de gallo. I opted to replace the white cheddar with goat cheese which I really enjoyed. Some other great options on the menu include the sweet crepes and nutella french toast. Definitely try it out if you are int he area!
label: positive

text:
Had lunch here today and was pleasantly surprised. My husband mentioned this place to me before since he said he tried the Kyle Burger and liked it. Since we had to do some errands at Fedex (right beside it), we decided to have lunch here. I had the kale and caesar salad and Kyle Burger while my husband had tomato soup and Buffalo Chicken Sandwich. We were pleased with our orders and the serving was just right.

In their menu, you could create your perfect meal by selecting any 2 options for $13 (upgrades are available for an additional cost -just check the menu). They call this Pair It!
These are the options for Pair It!
1. Burger or chicken sandwich
2. Salad
3. Bowl of soup
4. Beverage

This place is clean and the interior is nice. We will definitely come back to this place especially if we have burger cravings!
label: positive

text:

text:
I really enjoy Honeygrow in general, and this location is decent! Clean, order is pretty much always correct, and they are pretty efficient with service, plus a friendly staff. 

I almost always do the build your own stir fry. My favorite stir fry items are:
- egg white noodles
- scrambled egg
- roasted shrimp 
- roasted tofu
- red coconut curry sauce
- jalapeños 
- scallions
- mushrooms
- pineapple
- bell peppers
- spinach
- roasted garlic
- fried shallots
- chili flakes
- fresh lime


I mix up the main protein and always add an egg unless I really am not in the mood for egg. However, I usually go with shrimp and egg the majority of the time. 
I like how if I get extra sauce, this location adds it on the side, so I can add it as I see fit. 

I have tried the pre-selected stir fry bowls, and they are delicious, but I tend to prefer my own creation, so that is what I normally go with. 

I've had the salads. And they are good, but the stir fry is my favorite, so that is what I normally stick with.

I also really like the honeybar items! The fruit is always super fresh. You can't go wrong with any of these options, the pre-selected or the build your own. 

Portions are pretty big, I can often get 2 lunches or a lunch and dinner out of the stir fry. Price is not bad at all for the amount of food you get.
label: positive

text:
I mainly stopped by Cartel to try the vegan pastries, but I showed up too late in the day and missed out. I ended up buying their coffee mug, dark roast and a cappuccino with oat milk.

The dark roast is one of the best coffees I have had to date. When consumed hot, it's got a great chocolatey favor, and very smooth. When consumed cold, the coffee tastes like a high end toffee candy. So good. 
Although I shy away from spending too much money on coffee, this has been added to my list of guilty pleasures.

I'm not sure what beans they use for the cappuccino, but mine tasted light, a little fruity and also smooth. The oat milk was very frothy. I don't usually like coffee with fruity notes, but this one wasn't too acidic. I would order this again.
label: positive

text: Great breakfast/brunch spot just off State Street in Media Borough. Good coffee, omelets, pancakes, French toast, etc. Favorites include their scratch biscuits and maple bacon!

Also, they don't offer gluten free bread for the sandwiches anymore and they used to have Native Bread for them. They have a gluten free bagel but I was told I couldn't get it for a sandwich. Only with a smear. Makes no sense.

Great coffee but won't plan to eat here anymore.

text: I love Cafe Ynez. First of all, I believe it's woman owned. Second, they have vegan and vegetarian things. I often get the cauliflower tacos. Amazing. text:
I don't have a total count on how many breweries in Philly and suburbs, but there are a lot. Roots Down is hands down one of the best ones and one of my favorites.

Sometimes I'm lucky and they'd have 3 or 4 IPAs with different sub-styles on tap, but their flagship IPA Bine is delicious!

I love their beer so much, not only because they have a large lineup with every style of brews, but the most interesting, innovative, intriguing, impressive beer that always rotate.

Let the server know what your preferences or "beer profiles" are, because they will make sure to find a beer that you are looking for, regardless you're looking for from conservative to cutting edge, mild or exciting, they will have something for you.

The food is equally innovative, and ever changing.

The space is big, but can still get very crowded during weekends. Not a surprise because it's a fun place with great beer and food. Highly recommended!
label: positive

text:
Keto or paleo diet? Not the place unless going majoooor cheat style. 

Super cute setting - tight spaced and packed NYC style where you are basically 2 inches from the table next to you and eating with strangers.  Food was amazing - I'll get to that after grievances.

Hate to bring it up since service is struggling every where but service was struggling big time.  We were pressured to order our meal not even 1 minute into sitting down. Friend pointed out we had just sat down and needed some time. And again not even 2 minutes later right on top us to order. Ordered Coffee - wasn't asked if we needed anything with it & it was brought without silverware, spoons, or any sugar option - which turns out they only have ONE sugar option of raw sugar, and it seemed quiet a shock we would ask if there were other options or if we could have a spoon for the coffee...so much that we were brought one spoon for two people for two coffees when we pointed out no one brought us a utensils set up.  It was really bizarre response.  And for a brunch place?

Food itself was absolutely stunning and well crafted, and I'm giving this an extra star completely on the food.  That said - Side of bacon was not- cold and not cooked enough, but banana bread, shaksuka, etc was all really impressive.  Seems this place has a hard time with the little things, but main menu items are really great.
label: positive

text:
I really didn't think I could finish two scoops of ice cream by myself, but the ice cream is that good I did. My empty cup a compliment. Clean and bright interior, some finishing touches going in currently. Wonderful and helpful staff. The manager here was very accommodating and knowledgeable about the product and business. I'm glad to see enthusiasm in a place I believe is much needed ice cream spot here in the Bay Area. 

It's kind hard to pull off Key Lime ice cream. Excellent here. I guess you can describe it...you feel like you're eating real key lime pie you made...mixed with delicious ice cream. The chocolate cookie crumble was an unexpected perfect combo with the key lime. If you've ever had chocolate dipped key lime pie in Key West...it's exactly this, but x10 better. Chocolate cookie ice cream was wonderful by itself too. 

Too finish. I think the best part about the ice cream was, it's a clean taste. Usually I'm hunting down water after ice cream to cleanse my palate, but somehow here I didn't have to. 

Lovely place. Can't wait to be back. 3 locations too.

text: Listen! This place was amazing. I've had several breakfast/brunch spots in the Tampa/Brandon/Riverview area and this place by far exceeds all my expectations. My friend and I wanted to support a local Black-owned business for MLK Day so we were pleased they were open. The staff is so diverse and very professional. They even have their hometowns on their name tags which was a cute convo starter. We were immediately seated and once we took one look at the menu, we knew we needed a bigger table to share entrees & they happily accommodated us. We ordered a bit of everything honestly. Literally everything was amazing, from the shrimp & grits to the mimosas, to the waffle & that Philly cheesesteak eggs Benedict... everything was cooked and seasoned to perfection. I was thoroughly impressed the shrimp and grits was loaded with shrimp and the sauce was so flavorful. My friend had the loaded potato bowl and said the potatoes were crispy and pleasant. The wait staff weren't the only friendly faces, the manager came out and greeted us, even one of the bus boys came by to see how we enjoyed our meal. Oh and let me not forget that hazelnut praline coffee, my my my... they hit my spirit deep down and had me dancing and eating the entire meal. The decor was beautiful diner style and I can definitely see myself as a regular customer. I hope they start a customer program because they are definitely my new brunch/breakfast spot. Great job with the creative & flavorful menu. Definitely a local gem that I just might visit every week, at least once!
label: positive

text:

text: The Spinach Florentine Eggs Benedict were the BEST! French Toast slice on the side was crisp with light softness after the exterior crunch. Great service and eclectic vibe with all walks of life and dress codes enjoying meals with laughter.

text:
The host noted that this corner space has held a number of different businesses, and I recall there once being a Latin restaurant I liked less than this one. I hope this one sticks. Offering some fresh, interesting flavors, it brings something that New Orleans doesn't have too much of. I thought a peanut butter horchata sounded a bit odd, but it was a robust mix of flavors that really worked. Striving to eat healthy things at least on occasion during my visit, I got a yogurt bowl, which had an impressive variety of fruit, granola, and flower petals. The mix was a bit too sour and tart for me-a few bites with dried fig really worked, but I needed a bit more of that or a banana or something to balance it out. Still, I was quite satisfied.

They had some good vegetarian and a few vegan options; ideally I'd like to see them do some pupusas without meat, and clearer labeling (are the beans vegetarian?), but they're ahead of the curve for New Orleans and it appears to be quality food in a very pleasant Bywater setting.
label: positive

I met up with my sister for a morning bite and this place was a half way point for both of us so we sat quickly and luckily there was no one except us.

Our server was super nice when we had some questions about the menu explained to us what the had seasonally and what there best menu items were because she explained that they were changing the menu soon and opening more bar options drinks as well. 

We finally figured out what we wanted and ordered our drinks came out and the waiting game began while we waited we were playing games with our kiddos and just chatting maybe 20min go by and we realized that the food wasn't ready we waited another 10 minutes and asked one of the servers if she could get our server to check on our food she came out and assured us that the food will be ready shortly that they were having an issue in the back so of course at this point our youngest we're getting antsy finally after 5 more minutes the food was out.

Everything looked good but it all comes down the the taste. The apple fritter French toast was the best thing on the table, the scrambled eggs were fine, the sausage was interesting its not traditional it has a lot of spices and made by hand. The biscuits and gravy were terrible unfortunately it was pure pepper all the way through, and the bacon was cooked perfectly but very salty. I would give this place another try but hopefully with a little faster food to table time.
label: positive

text: Wow! What an amazing bakery. Absolutely incredible food and a beautiful atmosphere. Delicious breakfast, the croque madam and breakfast croissant are great but look out for specials, the duck hash is one of the tastiest things I've ever eaten. Pastries are easily the best in Santa Barbara, from simple croissants to decadent and elaborate cakes, the signature candy bar and the pistachio cream croissant have been the most memorable. Seating is a challenge on the weekends so be prepared to wait if you come around breakfast time but once you get one of their seasonal mimosas or a champagne spritz you'll be enjoying yourself before you know it. Still need to try the high tea!
label: positive

text:
Maybe we came on an off day, but we're used to lighter, tender, more flakey croissants. The multiple layers of laminated dough were clearly there, but we found the texture to be heavier and more bread-like than what we are used to. The baklava croissant had a small amount of filling that tasted of nuts and honey. Chocolate croissant had 2 bars of chocolate in the center, but the heavy bread-like texture took away from our enjoyment of the chocolate. Both croissants were gorgeous to look at as were the rest of their pastries. 

UPDATE: The owner personally reached out and was so kind as to make things right for us. We were so appreciative of her generous offer, but unfortunately we were there just for the day and had already left. It's rare to find an owner who is willing to go the extra mile.
label: neutral

text:
Perfection!! My boyfriend and I came to Bree'osh for a day trip to Santa Barbara from LA and this was our first stop. It was everything we wanted and more! We got a croissant, the Bree'Osh Breakfast Sandwich, and a Three Egg Omelette with mushrooms, gruyere, and spinach. The croissant was delicate, crispy on the outside, and airy on the inside with just enough softness and chew - simple done right! The omelette was also done very well; they were extremely generous with the mushrooms and it was well flavored. The egg was soft and buttery. The bree'osh breakfast sandwich was my favorite!! As someone who loves burgers, this was exactly what I wanted for brunch. It was so tasty and very filling, and the brioche bread was amazing! I wish the egg was a little bit saltier, but it was not hard to add salt myself. 

Overall, 5 stars for sure! We can't wait to come back!

text:
I spent 6 years in S.E. Asia in the 60/70's. Bangkok and Saigon completely seduced me with their food back then. I have been disappointed many times here in the U.S. with attempts at their food but I was not disappointed at Saigon Basil.  I would have been even happier if I could have had more types of "street food" but I have never found but 2 places in the U.S. that truly offer such "street food" variety. I recommend communication of your spice and "heat" desires. Their menu descriptions of "heat" are accurate!
They will adapt to greater "heat"  if you communicate what you want and allow time for the customization. Overall, I could have closed my eyes and dreamed I was back where food was incredible.
label: positive

text:
I cannot believe I've been working in Ardmore for 3 years and have JUST finally experienced this *adorable* French cafe. To be fair, I've just become a parking expert to the area, and can honestly admit that's probably a huge reason why I've never paid a visit.

The shop is super clean and HUGE! A great alternative to post up for a coffee meeting as opposed to Starbucks, which is always heavily populated.

They serve your usual cafe beverage options: drip coffees, lattes, mochas, teas, flavored syrups, and even smoothies. I kept it simple and got a vanilla latte with almond milk. It was superb! And extra hot (just how I like it!)

Additionally, there are tons of French nibbles and even vinaigrettes and othered bottle items to choose from. Great place to pickup a gift for those foodies in your life. 

I couldn't leave without a to-go back of treats. So I got the chocolate croissant and the apple turnover because it was just so pretty to look at in the case. The croissant was definitely my favorite of the two...but that's really just because I prefer the sweetness of chocolate over apple. I still suggest trying both!

Can't wait to return (and most likely become a regular lol).
label: positive

text:
After giving a 3 star review I had to revist and give them another try. I've been here several times before my 3 star review and wasn't disappointed so let's give her another roll.
  Wednesday morning on my day off I'm here inside Cafe Central at the Row. Very calm morning not too many diners. Nice and quite just how I like it.  I was seated outside in a plush comfy booth towards the patio area.  On the wall i notice an old school keno board hangin which brings back memories of when a nice lady would walk around offering Keno sheets with those little black crayons. Saying ,"keno...kenooo.....keno." lol am i old enough to say?" the good old days."The one thing I love about this place. Rain sleep or snow they are the very few in town that are open 24/7.  Whenever you're in doubt, Cafe Central can provide you with those late night and early morning eats.
  It looks like their menu changed slightly. Being Indecisive I read thru it fifty seven eleven times and can't decide breakfast, lunch or dinner. I love eating breakfast for dinner so I'll probably save that for later and go with dinner for breakfast. caution, their entire menu is not available till after 11am. Being it's only 10min away from 11 my nice server Suzanne allows me to order Lazagna for the taste buds to enjoy.  Garlic bread is served with my dish to top it off and to wash it down I went  with a fancy bottomless ice water. Oh yea babeeeyyyy....
   I ordered my food at 10:51am anxiously waiting It arrives to my surprise at 11:07. Not bad considering  it's not like a eggs and bacon, Wham bam, thanks man. The food looks amazing! I'm impressed. I can smell the combination of garlic bread and Lazagna and can't wait to dig in. After playing a round of COD, eating and in-between writing this review i decide yea, this dish taste so good and is definitely worth 20 dollars. The consistency of the pastas textures were on point. I hate having pasta that's too soft or soft and hard or even having burnt ends from being cooked improperly. Not the case here. The lazagna was topped off with cheeses,then delicious meat sauce, mixed in below was pasta cheese and more sauce. The ingredients blended in well and complimented each other nicely with the garlic bread. Classic, delicious and attractive.  Very good dish I'd order it again. Thank you Suzzane for the text book execution of good service. Now that's what really makes  a good dish great!!! Grazie
label: positive

text:
I've been wanting to visit Willa Jean for the longest time, and boy am I glad I finally did. I was on a bit of a time crunch, so I just grabbed a croissant and biscuit to-go. Both were OUTSTANDING. 

Y'all, their food is legit. The croissant? Absolute perfection and exactly what pastries should be. Not gonna lie, I've been eating a lot of mediocre croissants at bakeries and coffee shops throughout the city, but Willa Jean's croissant is as good as what I've eaten in France. text:
Do not let the name of this place fool you - it's not all just from "the garden", i.e - greens. I had an amazing BLT Smash with egg. It's black pepper bacon, lettuce, tomato, and avocado mash, and I added an over-easy egg! Okay, that made it incredibly messy, but really, messy sandwiches are the best. The bread had a great crust and was nice and chewy, making the mess easier to hold onto. The fries were crispy and the perfect complement (I swapped out my greens for them). 

I also got a Hoosier Heater cold-pressed juice with it, and it was delightful and refreshing. I could taste the flavor of the jalapeno rather than the spice from it which was nice (although I do love spicy). I also can't wait to try some of their cocktails made with those fresh juices. 

They currently use Toast to allow for contact-less checkout, and I am all about that (mostly because of convenience)! I can't wait to come back and try more of their options which tend toward brunch-y! I seriously had a hard time choosing my lunch!
label: positive

I got the number 7 they don't have a menu that's easy to read. You enter and it's always busy. You grab a number and wait for them to call on you to order and then they will call you again when your food is ready. You can pay anywhere such as before you enter the patio area or when you leave the store. The sandwich was delicious, I would've gotten less sun dried tomatoes the vinegar taste is usually my favorite thing but it wasn't suiting to my palate today. The meats were mouth watering and salty. This sandwich was huge and easily could be split between two people for about $9.00. You definitely won't want to share any of it because it's so good. 

I accompanied my sandwich with a latte with almond milk made by Melanie and she was very kind. The coffee was good and you can opt to eat at the coffee bar and have it served in a ceramic mug. 

Anticipate heavy foot traffic & good food.

text:
I used to be a regular at blueprint Delmar, a daily patron and would even attend their special events excitedly. However, at one of their after hours coffee tasting events I was targeted and harassed by a worker who was sexist, believe it or not! 

I sat through the event with a woman friend of mine, and when I went to use the restroom, one of their workers who is also a woman decided to whisper to my friend that she should be wary of me as a person and that she didn't trust me. For some context I had had ZERO negative experiences here prior and had spent many many hours there enjoying time with both men and women friends... so for their worker to had the audacity to impose on my social circuit with their unwarranted suspicion was absolutely astounding to me.

My friend told me that this happened as soon as we left the event. She was very uncomfortable with the fact that the worker chose to say that and quickly reassured me that she didn't have any concerns about my integrity, thank goodness.

So at this point my heart is racing, like borderline anxiety attack, and I'm absolutely confused about why I was targeted and why this had happened after I had spent so much time there, even time ordering from and small talking with this exact worker! I decide to go back in in order to get some clarification and correct the situation, hoping to put my anxiety to rest and also offer some kind of reassurance of my integrity to the person who was suspicious of me.

I calmly walked up to her and said hey I'd like to speak for a second. She immediately dismissed me and said she did not want to talk to me. At this point I literally do start having an anxiety attack and my heart is racing very fast. I kept my tone very calm and maintained courtesy, but I also got very excited internally and said "why would you say something like that to my friend when I went to the bathroom?!" 

She gave me little to no response and just said 'she's seen me talk to women before' (?!) and that she didn't trust me basically. My jaw drops at hearing this because I am very courteous and respectful in all my relations and I anxiously asked her to clarify, but to no avail...

She expressed she felt she had done nothing wrong in saying this to my friend when I walked away. At this point I feel so hurt and so confused and betrayed- I leave shortly after a very short and fruitless exchange with the worker with my heart in my stomach and my anxiety flying sky high.

A few days later I spoke with 2 of the owners there and explained the situation to them and they had absolutely NOTHING to say nor any goal to rectify the situation!! They didn't seem to care in the least and even went so far as to say they were going to 'side with their worker' regardless of the situations specifics and they asked me to not come back while she works there!! At this point I'm offended beyond belief and astounded at the ridiculous judgment call of their owners.

I quit going to blueprint at this point, but the story doesn't entirely end here. Fast forward like a year, maybe 1.5 years, and I walk back in the same store attempting to see if we can clarify and make amends but I don't even get to that point as I can see as soon as I walk in that the same woman who mischievously whispered to my friend was behind the counter and had been promoted to manager! She noticed me immediately and said I wasn't welcome there! It was embarrassing and another big anxiety explosion for me... 

I was hoping the owners would have corrected this, and I even waited a year after the last altercation hoping that one of them would speak with me or offer some clarification since we lived in the same neighborhood and would cross paths now and then. But they've never said a word to me since...

The fact that their owners deemed this appropriate, and felt it was wise to promote this individual, and also felt that making amends with me on a human level wasn't necessary has left a horribly bad taste in my mouth regarding blueprint coffee and I encourage anyone who takes sexism seriously to quit supporting them.
label: negative

Alessia is an inspiration and living proof that if you put your mind to something, stay at it even when it's hard, dreams really do come true! I could not be more proud of her and accomplishing her goal. You did it girl!

text:
The Mud House is doing fine without my review, but I'll throw my two cents in nonetheless:

It is rare to find a place that balances quality coffee with quality food this well. Their food menu is healthy-ish, quite hearty, and I have enjoyed everything I've tried. Their biscuits and gravy, breakfast burrito, and grilled cheese are standouts.

They now have their own coffee beans, but they also feature local roasters such as Blueprint Coffee. Their syrups are house made--the rosemary syrup in a cappuccino is excellent. While there is better coffee in the neighborhood (Sump is only a few blocks away), there is no better place to grab breakfast or lunch and. Good coffee.

They are frequently busy, but I have always found that by the time I finish waiting in line to order, a table has opened up. Takeout is also a good option.

text:
I was headed to Target, I swear. I had a mission. But how could I ignore the new (to me) buildings on that side (east) of 301? How could I ignore the hunger pangs that rode with me from downtown Tampa? I couldn't! The breakfast lure is powerful, so I pulled in. That was easy to do because Eggs Up Grill is in a strip mall-lots of free, easy-peasy parking. Good start. 

Inside, it's cute-super bright and airy, with lots of sunny yellows, happy blues and clean whites. It's welcoming and energizing. I may have been humming "Good morning, good morning" from "Singing In the Rain" as I chose my own spot. It was about 8:15 on a Tuesday morning-I had lots of choices-and picked a place on the south wall-with one chair and one booth seat. I had a commanding view from there. Not that my eyes strayed for long from the menu. You can have breakfast, brunch or lunch anytime! I was super tempted by the turkey avocado blt, but decided to get my bacon fix in "the classic" breakfast instead. I know, I know-it's standard-but it's a good starting point with someplace new. Two eggs (I chose scrambled) bacon or sausage (), home fries or grits (home fries) and for toast-I went wheat. Everything was cooked well, arrived piping hot, was very tasty and it just felt quality. The portions were just right: you leave feeling full... not full of regret. 

The server was pleasant and efficient... the place was clean and well cared for. It got crowded as I was leaving. On a Covid note: the staff wasn't masked and there's no outside seating. The menu is large, but not overwhelming. I'll be back sometime for that blt, the Greek omelette and the bananas Foster French toast I spied. But first... I gotta get to Target!
label: positive

text:
My experience was tainted by incredibly slow service. And I pinky promise I'm not being dramatic. And sooooo I would highly suggest ordering ahead for takeout.

The service aside, my soup was very mild in flavor and none of the chicken was edible as it was alllllllll gristle and fat. I ate the noodles and used the rest as a broth to cook other meat in for dinner the next night. And used the chicken as grease to fry gnocchi. So at least I got use out of the meal.

text: Took my beloved wife for our first anniversary celebration at suasana suite and hotel . The valentine package that i chose was every penny worth it and we were both happy that night. From the full course meal to the services , everything was perfect and to see how surprised my wife was for the last surprise i planned for her at the end of our dinner, i felt utmost satisfied and beyond happy. There was also an additional surprise from the manager himself when we got an extra brownie with icecream beautifully plated in celebration of us becoming parents to be Definitely recommending this to everyone
label: positive

text: at first i thought it was going to be one of those typical tourist eatery but i was so wrong. it felt so homey almost as if its just your next door kopitiam. it wasnt a full house when i went there which was great for me, and my family. eventhough we didnt know anyone there but the other customers were all smiles and talking to the owners as if they have known them a lifetime. even i had a few chats here and there and didnt feel like an outsider. maybe due to its small size dining area which makes it more intimate and cozy. food was great too and very affordable. since it has limited space u might be waiting long for a seat if you are there during peak season.
label: positive

text: A short drive from our hotel. We decided to have our dinner here as it came recommended by my friend. The thosai we ordered were not freshly made and the chutney straight out of the fridge. Made me sad when the chicken varuval arrived, hardly any chicken.
label: neutral

text: Great location with good service. The location is right on Jonkers Walk, right in the centre of things. Cold draft beer and good selection of food. Worth a visit

text: The buffet Ramadhan has many varieties of food. Local, western, arabic, japanese, chinese and dessert, you name it.
